findings.area: Connection flow: ID/IP entry, connect button, P2P→relay, network mode, reconnect
desktopCapabilities: Connect by 9-digit relay ID OR by IP / IP:port — shared parsing/validation in src/lib/connectTarget.ts (isAddr, ipRe, ≥6-digit partial-ID resolution, grouped-3 formatting)
No upfront password: connect issues a one-time-password prompt via events; host Allow/Deny supported; auth-time pauses the connect timeout (sessions.svelte.ts notifyAuthStart/Submit, #authInProgress)

Real Connecting screen with REAL transport milestones (no fake progress): reach → actual transport (P2P vs relay) → auth → preparing, driven by the conn-phase event (src-tauri/src/play.rs:187, events.rs:162 ConnPhase), plus a 12s slow-host hint and a Cancel button (src/lib/screens/Connecting.svelte)
Connect deadline + post-auth deadline (CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MS 45s / POST_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MS 30s) so the screen can't spin forever (sessions.svelte.ts:60-68)
Localized friendly error mapping for raw core ConnError strings — relayDown, peerUnreachable, notOnline, p2pFailed, unreachable, timeout (sessions.svelte.ts friendlyConnectError)
Network mode (auto / p2p-only / relay-only) + relay host:port persisted to the Rust Config via get_config/set_config (read live each tick, applies without reconnect)
Stable device identity persisted (Identity::load_or_create) → same relay ID across restarts; mobile re-generates identity per connect

Recents/history + LAN online dots, reconnect from a recent/LAN entry (src/lib/screens/Home.svelte, peers.svelte, LanDevices), 'connect to me by IP' shows own LAN IP + bound node port (localIp/nodePort, onNodePort live update)
CLI --connect auto-connect target with password+mode (auto_connect_target, events.rs AutoConnect, lib.rs:302), holdConnecting splash gate

missingPieces.feature: Connecting screen / connection progress + transport feedback
missingPieces.description: Mobile has NO connecting screen. connect_host (mobile/src/client.rs:91) is one big await: the UI just sets msg='Bağlanıyor…', disables the button, then either flips body.in-session or shows 'Bağlanılamadı' (ui/index.html:315-326). There is no reach→auth→prepare step list, no 'P2P vs relay' indication, no slow-host hint, no cancel. The net pill (ui/index.html:141) is a STATIC label of the chosen mode, not the actual transport used.
missingPieces.approach: Add a full-screen touch-first connecting overlay in ui/index.html (mirror desktop Connecting.svelte semantics: target id + mode, 3-step reach/auth/prepare, slow hint, large Cancel). Emit real milestones from mobile/src/client.rs: after node.connect() resolves, app.emit a 'conn-phase' event carrying sess.transport() (Direct vs Relay) before authenticate(); emit again after auth accepted. Listen via window.__TAURI__.event.listen in index.html and drive the step list + update the net pill to the REAL transport. Set body.in-session only after first decoded AU (already feeding via feed_au) — or at minimum after request_stream succeeds.

missingPieces.feature: Connect by IP / IP:port (LAN direct)
missingPieces.description: Mobile only accepts a 9-digit relay ID: the input is inputmode=numeric and strips all non-digits, capped at 9 (ui/index.html:156,311), and connect_host calls DeviceId::parse(&id) which rejects anything that isn't a 9-digit id (mobile/src/client.rs:102). Desktop supports IP / IP:port direct connect (connectTarget.ts). The product 'connect to me by IP' LAN path is unreachable from mobile.
missingPieces.approach: Port connectTarget.ts logic into the mobile UI (allow digits + . + : like fmtTarget/isAddr/canConnectTarget) and stop forcing numeric-only. In mobile/src/client.rs branch on the target: if it parses as a SocketAddr use node.connect_addr / direct path (whatever pulsar-core exposes for address connect — verify against desktop play.rs connect), else DeviceId::parse for the relay id. Keep the grouped-3 formatting only for the pure-digit ID case.

missingPieces.feature: Friendly + localized connect-error mapping
missingPieces.description: On failure the mobile UI shows raw Rust error strings ('Hata: ' + e.message — ui/index.html:313 guard, and 'Bağlanılamadı' otherwise). client.rs returns raw debug-formatted core errors like 'connect failed: {e:?}', 'auth denied (wrong password?)' (mobile/src/client.rs:114-132). Desktop maps these to friendly Turkish copy (relay down, peer unreachable, not online, P2P failed, timeout — friendlyConnectError).
missingPieces.approach: Port friendlyConnectError's substring→Turkish-copy table into a small JS helper in ui/index.html and run connect errors through it before say(...). Keep the Rust error strings stable/matchable (they already echo core ConnError text). Optionally add the same timeout marker the desktop uses.

missingPieces.feature: Connect timeout (no infinite spinner)
missingPieces.description: Mobile connect_host has no deadline: node.connect()/authenticate()/request_stream are awaited unbounded (mobile/src/client.rs:121-169). If the host accepts the transport but never answers auth/caps, the button stays disabled and 'Bağlanıyor…' shows forever. Desktop guards with a 45s connect + 30s post-auth deadline and tears down a late-resolving attempt.
missingPieces.approach: Wrap the connect/auth/request_stream awaits in tokio::time::timeout inside connect_host (e.g. 45s overall), returning a distinct timeout error string that the JS error-mapper recognizes. Re-enable the connect button + clear in-session on the JS side when the promise rejects. Pair with the Cancel button from the connecting-screen item so the user can abort.

missingPieces.feature: Network mode + relay persisted to Rust Config (not just localStorage)
missingPieces.description: Mobile stores relay/netmode/codec/name only in localStorage (ui/index.html:299-306) and re-passes them per-invoke; client.rs maps netmode via parse_mode (mobile/src/client.rs:73) but the Node is bound fresh each connect with a throwaway Identity::generate() (client.rs:106-112). Desktop persists relay + network_mode in the Rust Config (get_config/set_config) read live. Consequence: mobile mode/relay live only in the webview, and there is no shared config the Rust side owns.
missingPieces.approach: Add lightweight get_config/set_config commands in the mobile crate (reuse pulsar_core::config::Config if it cross-compiles to Android, or a minimal serde struct persisted to the app data dir) and have the UI read/write those instead of (or in addition to) localStorage. Have go_online/connect_host read relay+netmode from that config when args are omitted.

missingPieces.feature: Stable persisted device identity (consistent ID across restarts)
missingPieces.description: Both connect_host (mobile/src/client.rs:111) and go_online (mobile/src/host.rs:46) bind with Identity::generate() — a NEW keypair every time. The relay maps pubkey→id (by_pubkey) so a fresh identity means a NEW 9-digit ID each launch. Desktop uses Identity::load_or_create to keep a stable per-user ID. This breaks 'Cihazım' ID stability and history matching.
missingPieces.approach: Implement an Identity load-or-create that persists the X25519 key to the mobile app data dir (Tauri path API) and use it in both Node::bind_with_identity calls instead of Identity::generate(). Verify Identity::load_or_create from pulsar-core is reachable on the mobile target.

missingPieces.feature: Auto-reconnect / session-drop handling
missingPieces.description: When the read loop sees the session close (Ok(None) → break, mobile/src/client.rs:209-211) it just stops the stream and removes the input sender; the webview is never told, so body.in-session stays true and the user is stuck on a frozen surface with the control bar. There is no reconnect prompt or auto-retry. Desktop emits play-ended and the SessionManager closes the tab / surfaces it.
missingPieces.approach: Emit a 'play-ended' (or 'session-closed') event from the read loop's Ok(None)/error exit in client.rs; in ui/index.html listen for it, drop body.in-session, and show a touch-first 'Bağlantı kesildi — Tekrar bağlan' card on the connect tab (one-tap reconnect to the last id+slot, reusing doConnect). Optionally a bounded auto-retry for transient relay drops.

missingPieces.feature: Remove the TEMP auto-self-connect diagnostic
missingPieces.description: ui/index.html:414-424 has a TEMP block that, 2s after load, auto-calls go_online as 'TestHost' then connect_host back to itself — a debugging hack that will fire on every app start for real users (registers a stray host + a self connection). Not a desktop feature; it pollutes the real connect flow.
missingPieces.approach: Delete the setTimeout diagnostic block (ui/index.html:414-424). If a self-test is still wanted, gate it behind a build flag / hidden debug toggle instead of running unconditionally.

findings.notes: Mobile connection flow is a working happy-path skeleton (connect_host with relay/id/password/slot/netmode/name, 9-digit numeric ID entry with grouping + clipboard paste, localStorage history/recents, net-mode segmented control, share-id, go_online host path) but lacks the robustness layer the desktop has: no real connecting screen / transport (P2P-vs-relay) feedback, no IP connect, no timeout, no friendly/localized error mapping, no session-drop handling or reconnect, and no Rust-side config/identity persistence (identity is regenerated every connect so the relay ID is not stable). Highest-impact + cheapest wins: delete the TEMP auto-self-connect diagnostic (index.html:414-424, P1), add a connecting overlay driven by a real conn-phase event emitted from client.rs (P1), then add timeout + friendly error mapping (P2) and session-drop→reconnect (P2). Note: the net pill currently shows the CONFIGURED mode, not the achieved transport — desktop shows the real one. Verify pulsar-core exposes Session::transport() and an address-based connect on the mobile build target before wiring IP connect; everything proposed reuses existing pulsar-core APIs already imported in client.rs/host.rs. Per product rules this area is mode-agnostic (applies to both remote and game), so nothing here is excluded by the game-mode restrictions.

findings.area: Saved/recent devices, device list, unattended access, forget device, naming
desktopCapabilities: Address book of SAVED devices (Devices.svelte) — explicitly added, persisted, separate from connection history (peers.svelte.ts: saved flag, savedPeers())
Per-device custom NAME, editable in place (add/edit modal; updatePeer, renameGamePeer)
Per-device IMAGE: pick one of 4 built-in line icons OR upload a picture (cover-cropped to 96px data URL) — Devices.svelte:87-110, ICONS list
FORGET/remove a saved device (removePeer) and remove a single entry from history (removeFromHistory / removeFromGameHistory) and clearHistory()
FAVORITE/star toggle per device (toggleFav)
SEARCH/filter devices by name or id (Devices.svelte q + filtered)
ONLINE presence dot per saved device via LAN discovery beacon polled every 3s (api.lanDevices, isOnline)
Last-connected relative-time label per device (relTime)
SEPARATE remote-desktop vs game-streaming recents timelines (historyPeers vs gameHistoryPeers); history capped at 20, address book uncapped
Cached pushed peer identity (name + avatar) decorating recents/LAN rows even without a live session (setPeerIdentity, avatarFor)
9-digit relay-id grouping/normalization + de-dup merge on re-key (normalizeId, fmtPeerId)
UNATTENDED ACCESS as a HOST setting (settings.unattended; config.unattended_access) — fixed password, skip approval; surfaced as a warning chip on Home SelfCard
Live ACTIVE-CONNECTIONS window listing inbound sessions with kick + view-only per session (connections.rs: list_connections, set_view_only, show_connections)

missingPieces.feature: Real saved-devices address book (port the peers store)
missingPieces.description: Mobile only keeps a flat history array of {id, ts} (index.html:330, max 12). There is no concept of a SAVED device, no name, no per-device metadata, no de-dup/normalization, and no separation of saved vs recent. Desktop's peers.svelte.ts is the canonical model with saved/fav/name/image/lastConnected/gameConnected and id normalization. Mobile needs an equivalent persisted store.
missingPieces.approach: Mobile is one plain HTML/JS file with no bundler, so peers.svelte.ts (a Svelte-rune module) cannot be imported. Port its pure logic to a small vanilla JS object embedded in ui/index.html: a peers array persisted to localStorage under 'pulsar.peers.v1' (match the desktop KEY so a future shared store stays compatible), with normalizeId/fmtPeerId, savedPeers/historyPeers/gameHistoryPeers, recordConnection, addPeer, updatePeer, removePeer, removeFromHistory, toggleFav. Replace the ad-hoc addHistory/LS.get('history') (index.html:330,335,340) with recordConnection. Keep it pure JS (no Tauri command needed — this is client-local, identical to desktop which stores it in the webview's localStorage, NOT in Rust).

missingPieces.feature: Saved Devices tab/screen (touch-first)
missingPieces.description: There is no Devices screen on mobile at all — the bottom nav has Bağlan / Cihazım / Ayarlar / Geçmiş (index.html:241-254). Geçmiş is recents-only (id + time, tap to reconnect). Desktop's Devices grid (name, image, online dot, last-seen, connect/edit/forget, favorite, search) has no mobile equivalent.
missingPieces.approach: Add a 5th-or-replacement nav entry 'Cihazlar' (or fold saved devices into the Geçmiş tab as a 'Kayıtlı' section above 'Son bağlananlar'). Design a touch-first vertical list (reuse the existing .item / .row-list styles, index.html:78-86): each row = icon/image + name + grouped id + online dot + last-seen, tap-to-connect, and a trailing kebab/long-press for Edit / Forget / Favorite (mobile should use a bottom sheet or swipe-to-delete instead of the desktop's hover x/edit buttons which don't work on touch). Add a '+ Cihaz ekle' button opening a sheet with name + id input + the 4 icon chips (image upload optional on mobile, lower priority). Wire connect through the same doConnect(id, 0) path (index.html:315) and call recordConnection on success.

missingPieces.feature: Device naming + edit
missingPieces.description: Mobile recents show only the grouped numeric id (itemHtml, index.html:333) — no name, no rename. Desktop lets you name a device on add and edit it later (newName, updatePeer, renameGamePeer).
missingPieces.approach: Part of the add/edit sheet from the Devices screen: a name text input bound into addPeer/updatePeer. Render the saved name in the item row (fall back to fmtPeerId when unnamed, mirroring recordConnection's placeholder logic). Touch-first: full-width input in a bottom sheet, large tap targets, no desktop modal port.

missingPieces.feature: Forget device / remove from history
missingPieces.description: Mobile has no way to delete a recent or saved device — the history array only ever grows/rotates at 12 (index.html:330). Desktop has removePeer, removeFromHistory, removeFromGameHistory, clearHistory with care to keep saved entries.
missingPieces.approach: Add swipe-to-delete or a long-press bottom-sheet action ('Geçmişten kaldır' for recents, 'Cihazı unut' for saved) on each .item row, calling removeFromHistory / removePeer from the ported store, then re-render. Add a 'Geçmişi temizle' button at the top of the Geçmiş tab calling clearHistory. Touch gestures, not the desktop hover buttons.

missingPieces.feature: Online presence on saved/recent devices
missingPieces.description: Mobile cannot show whether a saved device is reachable — there is no LAN discovery call wired on mobile (desktop polls api.lanDevices every 3s and renders an online dot, Devices.svelte:55-75). The mobile Rust bridge exposes no lan_devices command (lib.rs:30-37 only has connect_host/send_pointer/send_button/go_online/a11y_*).
missingPieces.approach: Lower priority for a thin mobile client (LAN beacon discovery may not be a goal on cellular). If wanted: add a lan_devices Tauri command to mobile/src/client.rs backed by pulsar-core's LAN discovery, expose it in the invoke_handler (mobile/src/lib.rs:30), poll it from index.html on a 3s interval, and render the .dot.on state on device rows (reuse desktop isOnline/addrHost matching logic). Skip if mobile is connect-out-only.

missingPieces.feature: Separate remote vs game recents timelines
missingPieces.description: Mobile records every successful connect into ONE history list keyed only by id (addHistory, index.html:330) regardless of mode, even though the connect screen has a remote/game mode toggle (m-remote/m-game, index.html:151-152, setMode). Desktop keeps lastConnected and gameConnected as distinct timelines so gaming recents stay separate.
missingPieces.approach: When recording a connection, pass the current body.dataset.mode into recordConnection(id, name, 'pc', mode==='game'?'game':'remote') (the ported store already supports both timelines). Decide UX: either one Geçmiş list with a mode chip per row, or filter by the active connect mode. Reuse desktop's gameHistoryPeers/historyPeers split.

missingPieces.feature: Unattended access for the phone-as-host (Cihazım)
missingPieces.description: The mobile host tab (Cihazım, index.html:170-186) always issues a one-time password on go_online (host-pw shown). There is no toggle to enable unattended access (fixed password / skip approval), which desktop exposes as config.unattended_access and the core supports (go_online honors an unattended_access flag that skips the per-connection Auth). Mobile go_online (mobile/src/host.rs) does not accept or pass an unattended flag.
missingPieces.approach: Add a touch toggle in the Cihazım card ('Gözetimsiz erişim') with the desktop warning copy (i18n.tr settings.unattended/unattendedDesc, home.unattendedWarn). Thread the flag from index.html's go_online invoke (index.html:350) into mobile/src/host.rs go_online and through to pulsar-core's host serve loop (mirror desktop src-tauri config.unattended_access → skip Auth). Persist the toggle in localStorage. Note: the desktop unattended also depends on a stable/fixed password story; verify the mobile host issues a persistable password rather than a fresh one-time one when unattended is on.

missingPieces.feature: Active inbound-connections view + kick (phone-as-host)
missingPieces.description: When the phone is a host, there is no way to see who is currently connected or to disconnect/kick them, nor a view-only toggle. Desktop has a dedicated connections window (connections.rs: list_connections/set_view_only/show_connections, ConnRow per session). Mobile has neither a command nor any UI.
missingPieces.approach: Add mobile Tauri commands list_connections + disconnect_session (and optionally set_view_only) in a new mobile/src/connections.rs backed by the same AppState active-sessions map the mobile host serve loop maintains; register them in mobile/src/lib.rs invoke_handler. In the Cihazım tab add a live 'Bağlı cihazlar' list (reuse .row-list/.item) with a kick (x) action per row, polled or driven by a session event. Touch-first list, not the desktop separate-window model.

findings.notes: Mobile is a single plain HTML/JS file (desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html, withGlobalTauri, no SvelteKit/bundler), so desktop's peers.svelte.ts and Devices.svelte CANNOT be imported — their pure logic must be re-implemented in vanilla JS inline. Today mobile's only device-memory is a flat localStorage history array of {id, ts} (max 12), built by addHistory (index.html:330) and rendered by renderHistory (index.html:334) and renderRecentQuick (index.html:339). It carries no name, image, saved flag, favorite, online state, or mode split. Desktop stores the entire address book in the webview's localStorage too (NOT in Rust), so the client-local pieces (saved devices, naming, forget, favorites, recents split) need NO Rust changes — port the JS. Rust changes are only needed for the host-side pieces: unattended access (mobile/src/host.rs go_online takes no unattended flag) and the active-connections list/kick (no command exists; mobile/src/lib.rs:30-37 only wires connect_host/send_pointer/send_button/go_online/open_a11y_settings/a11y_enabled). The mobile bridge also has no lan_devices command, so online-presence dots are unavailable. There is also a TEMP self-connect diagnostic block (index.html:414-424) that auto-go-online + self-connect on launch — unrelated to this area but should be removed before shipping. Per product rules, file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor were correctly NOT considered here. The 'split' button (index.html:261, doConnect slot 1) implies mobile multi-session-as-client, which interacts with recents (only slot 0 is recorded, index.html:324) — keep that behavior when porting recordConnection.

findings.area: One-time-password auth: entering host password, host issuing/showing password, incoming-request accept/deny
desktopCapabilities: CLIENT entering password up-front: connect form takes target id + optional OTP (handled by lib/screens connect UI)

CLIENT interactive password prompt: host emits auth-prompt -> webview shows PasswordModal (routes/page/PasswordModal.svelte), user types OTP or persistent connect password, submit_password resolves it; supports a FIFO queue for multiple concurrent connects
CLIENT wrong-password retry loop: a re-fired auth-prompt for the same req replaces the modal in place and shows 'wrong password' error (+page.svelte:524-565); user can retry without reconnecting
CLIENT races the host's Allow click against the user typing the password (client_authenticate in auth.rs:609); accepted on whichever lands first
CLIENT cancel: submit_password(null) cancels the prompt and aborts the connect
HOST issues an OTP on go_online (gen_password 7yf2-qk style), shows it on the Home screen, and exposes session_password / new_password commands
HOST single-use OTP rotation: try_consume_otp atomically matches+rotates the OTP on a correct auth, and emits 'session-password' so the displayed code refreshes live (commands.rs:249-299)
HOST persistent reusable 'connect password' (Settings -> Guvenlik) accepted in addition to the rotating OTP (auth.rs:524-529)

HOST Allow/Deny incoming-request popup: a separate always-on-top focused window (open_approval_window) rendering Approve.svelte with peer id + password status (ok/bad/none) + 30s auto-deny countdown; respond_request resolves it
HOST races the popup against an arriving password (race_host_auth in auth.rs:438) so an operator can approve passwordlessly while the client is still being prompted

HOST brute-force/DoS protection: per-peer lockout after 5 wrong guesses, source-independent global counter that force-rotates the OTP after 20 total wrong guesses, rotation cooldown, and a MAX_CONCURRENT_POPUPS=3 cap
HOST same-host co-op preauth + recent-auth window so split/couch-coop and fetch->launch skip re-prompting (auth.rs:232-294)
HOST unattended access: an empty OTP skips the consume + Allow/Deny popup entirely
HOST idle (60s) + absolute (120s) deadlines that auto-deny a stalled auth race

missingPieces.feature: Client interactive OTP prompt (host-requested password)
missingPieces.description: On mobile, the client only takes the OTP up-front in the connect form (ui/index.html:159-162 'pw' field). client.rs:126-133 calls pulsar-core authenticate() ONCE and, if the host replies NeedPassword (empty/wrong pw), it just returns Err('host requires a password') and the connect fails. There is NO password-prompt UI and NO retry. Desktop instead drives an auth-prompt event -> PasswordModal -> submit_password retry loop. So a mobile user who leaves the pw blank or mistypes it cannot recover without re-running the whole connect, and a host that pops Allow/Deny while asking for a pw is not handled gracefully.
missingPieces.approach: Replace the single-shot authenticate() in client.rs::connect_host with the desktop's race loop: send empty Auth first, then loop on recv_host_auth — on NeedPassword emit an 'auth-prompt' Tauri event (req id + peer) and await a oneshot resolved by a new submit_password command; on Ok/Denied finish. Port the pw_pending HashMap + next_auth AtomicU64 + open_pw_prompt/client_authenticate logic from desktop src-tauri/src/auth.rs into mobile/src/client.rs (it is desktop-window-free already). Register submit_password in mobile/src/lib.rs invoke_handler and add InputSenders-style state for pw_pending. In ui/index.html add a touch-first bottom-sheet password modal (re-design, not the desktop centered card): big mono input, Gonder/Iptal, inline 'yanlis sifre' error on re-fire; listen via window.__TAURI__.event.listen('auth-prompt'). Keep the existing up-front pw field as the first attempt.

missingPieces.feature: Host incoming-request Allow/Deny prompt (and password racing)
missingPieces.description: The mobile HOST (host.rs:53-74) auto-accepts ANY connection whose password matches the static OTP with NO operator Allow/Deny prompt and NO passwordless-Allow path. Desktop shows an Approve popup (Approve.svelte) raced against the password (race_host_auth) so the phone's owner can see who is connecting and approve/deny. On mobile there is no UI surface at all for an incoming request — the phone silently accepts. This is a real security/consent gap for the host personality.
missingPieces.approach: In host.rs, replace the inline recv_auth/need_password/accept gate with a race: emit a 'session-request' Tauri event (req id, peer id, pw-status ok/bad/none) and concurrently read client auth via recv_client_auth; resolve on either an operator decision (new respond_request command -> oneshot) or a correct password. Add a pending-requests map to a managed state struct in lib.rs and register respond_request in the invoke_handler. In ui/index.html add a touch-first incoming-request sheet (full-width modal over the Cihazim tab or a system-style overlay): shows the connecting device id + 'sifre dogru/yanlis/yok' chip + Izin Ver / Reddet buttons + a 30s auto-deny countdown mirroring Approve.svelte. Since the phone may be backgrounded, also wire a local notification later (out of scope here). Keep the auto-accept ONLY when no operator UI is feasible behind a flag, defaulting to prompt.

missingPieces.feature: Single-use OTP rotation + live code refresh on the host
missingPieces.description: Mobile host generates ONE password in go_online (host.rs:50 gen_password) and reuses it forever for every incoming session — it is never rotated after a successful auth, so the same code unlocks unlimited sessions. The UI shows it once (index.html:354) and never updates. Desktop rotates the OTP on each successful consume (try_consume_otp) and emits 'session-password' so the displayed chip refreshes. This breaks the product's 'one-time password' guarantee on mobile.
missingPieces.approach: Move the OTP into managed state (Arc<Mutex<String>>) in mobile/src/lib.rs instead of a captured local in go_online. After a successful auth in host.rs, regenerate with gen_password(), store it, and emit a 'host-password' Tauri event. In ui/index.html listen for 'host-password' and update the #host-pw chip + #host-id status text. Optionally add a dash/case-insensitive canonicalization mirroring desktop try_consume_otp (commands.rs:266-299) so '7yf2-qk' / '7YF2QK' both match. No new design needed (reuse the existing pw-chip element).

missingPieces.feature: Brute-force / prompt-spam throttle on the mobile host
missingPieces.description: Mobile host has NO equivalent of desktop's auth throttle module (auth.rs:33-218): no per-peer lockout, no global wrong-guess counter, no OTP force-rotation under attack, no concurrent-popup cap. Combined with the never-rotating OTP, a relay-id-cycling attacker can brute-force the phone's static code indefinitely. Desktop treats both passwords + the popup as remotely-triggerable and hardened.
missingPieces.approach: Reuse the desktop throttle: factor desktop-app/src-tauri/src/auth.rs::throttle (and the global rotate-on-N-failures hook) into a shared location or copy it into mobile/src. In host.rs, before opening the (future) Allow/Deny prompt, check throttle::locked_out(peer) and deny silently; on each wrong password call record_failure(peer) + note_global_failure() and rotate the stored OTP (ties into the rotation piece above) when the global threshold is crossed. Add the MAX_CONCURRENT prompt cap if/when the incoming-request UI lands. No UI design needed.

missingPieces.feature: Same-host co-op preauth (skip re-auth for already-authorized device) on the host
missingPieces.description: Mobile host has no preauth map (desktop auth.rs:232-294), so when the mobile client opens a SPLIT second pane (ui/index.html:365 btn-split -> doConnect slot 1) against a mobile host, pane B would be asked for the already-rotated/single-use OTP it cannot present. On desktop the same-host co-op + recent-auth window skips the consume + popup for a device already authorized. Lower priority because the common case is a mobile client -> desktop host (which already has this), but a mobile-host split would break.
missingPieces.approach: Port the preauth module from desktop auth.rs (keyed on Session::peer_pubkey) into the mobile host: on a successfully authed session call preauth::add(pubkey) after accept, and at the top of the per-session auth handler call preauth::is_authorized(pubkey) to skip the OTP consume + (future) prompt; call preauth::remove on session teardown and preauth::clear_all on a fresh go_online. No UI. Only needed once OTP rotation (piece 3) makes the static-code shortcut invalid.

findings.notes: Current mobile reality: CLIENT auth works ONLY for the happy path where the user types the correct OTP up-front in the connect form (ui/index.html:159-162) and the host accepts on the first Auth frame; client.rs:126-133 uses pulsar-core authenticate() single-shot and turns NeedPassword/Denied into a hard error with no prompt/retry UI. HOST (host.rs) accepts any connection matching a never-rotating static OTP with no operator consent UI, no throttle, no co-op preauth. So the building blocks (gen_password, recv_auth, need_password, accept/reject, authenticate, AuthOutcome) are all present and wired, but every interactive + hardening layer the desktop has (PasswordModal retry loop, Approve.svelte Allow/Deny popup raced against the password, single-use rotation, throttle, preauth) is ABSENT on mobile. Two new touch-first mobile UIs are required: a client password bottom-sheet and a host incoming-request accept/deny sheet — both must be re-designed for portrait/touch (the desktop ones are separate-window / centered-card layouts that do not port). Note the desktop also accepts a persistent 'connect password' alongside the OTP; mobile has no settings surface for that yet (out of scope for these pieces, but the client prompt should pass whatever the user types through verbatim so it would work once the host supports it). Per product rules this area is shared across both remote and game modes (auth happens before the mode-specific session), so no mode-gating of these UIs is needed. The TEMP self-connect block at ui/index.html:414-424 is diagnostic only and should be removed before shipping.

findings.area: In-session toolbar/dock (remote mode)
desktopCapabilities: Native egui overlay over the video (overlay.rs) opened via a draggable Pulsar-mark hotspot button (Session.svelte:826) or Ctrl+Shift+M / Escape; auto-hides while controlling
Stat-tile header in the overlay root: RTT, FPS, encode ms, decode ms, target bitrate Mbps, bandwidth (overlay.rs:675-694)
Remote-mode root hub = 8 category boxes + End (overlay.rs:711-720): Stream, Display, Audio, Chat, Files, Tools, Controllers, Gauges
Stream view: codec / encoder / resolution / fps / bitrate-limit + quality(latency|quality) + frame-pacing, live-applied to the host (overlay.rs draw_stream; controls.svelte.ts setters)
Display view: view-fit modes (fit/stretch/original), multi-monitor screen picker, 'screen adaptation' toggle (overlay.rs draw_display)
Audio view: host-audio transmit toggle, mute-host toggle, mic toggle (overlay-cmd atx/amute/mic in Session.svelte:613-615)
Tools view: send-clipboard, reverse direction (role swap), fullscreen toggle, native file pick (overlay.rs draw_tools:998-1045)
Gauges view: live stats HUD toggle; Controllers view (pad list/swap/emulation) — these are remote-applicable but controller/gauges are mostly game-leaning
Red End-session button as the last nav item; leave-combo (Ctrl+Shift+Q) ends, 3xRightCtrl releases control (Session.svelte onKbdLeave/onKbdReleased)
Connection/transport label (P2P vs Relay) derived and shown (Session.svelte:102)
Click-to-engage control + cursor-start mapping; auto-fading in-control hint (Session.svelte onScreenDown, hintFade)

missingPieces.feature: In-session overlay/menu (the toolbar itself)
missingPieces.description: Mobile has ONLY a single bottom glass pill (ui/index.html:257-267) with a status dot, a codec/transport meta string, a Split button and a red End ('Kes') button. There is NO equivalent of the desktop overlay hub — no way to reach Stream/Display/Audio/Tools/stats from inside a session. The desktop's rich egui menu is entirely absent; mobile shows status + Split + End only.
missingPieces.approach: Design a touch-first in-session overlay (bottom sheet or expanding dock) opened by tapping the status pill or a dedicated FAB, since the desktop hotspot+keyboard-combo model does not fit touch. Render it in ui/index.html as DOM over the transparent webview (NOT egui — mobile has no pulsar-render; the native video is the tauri-plugin-pulsar-video surface behind the webview). Group sections as collapsible cards (Stream / Display / Audio / Tools) styled with the existing tokens (.card/.seg) already in ui/index.html. Each control needs a new Tauri command on the mobile Rust side to forward to the host over the live session (mirror desktop controls.svelte.ts setters using pulsar-core service messages once pulsar-core lands at M4).

missingPieces.feature: Live session stats (RTT / FPS / decode / bitrate / bandwidth)
missingPieces.description: Desktop overlay root shows six stat tiles (overlay.rs:675-694) pushed from the host Stats message + local decode metrics. Mobile only shows a static one-line 'bar-meta' set once at connect to codec + 'p2p'/'oturum' (ui/index.html:322) and never updates; no RTT/FPS/decode/bitrate.
missingPieces.approach: Emit a periodic stats event from the mobile native video plugin (decode ms / fps) plus parse the host Stats message in client.rs, and surface them via a Tauri event the webview listens to. Render a compact stat strip inside the new overlay sheet (reuse desktop tile labels via i18n). Wire decode/fps from tauri-plugin-pulsar-video; RTT/bitrate from the session in client.rs.

missingPieces.feature: Stream controls (codec / quality / bitrate / resolution / fps)
missingPieces.description: Desktop Stream view live-applies codec/encoder/res/fps/bitrate/quality/pacing to the host (overlay.rs draw_stream; Session.svelte:588-596). Mobile cannot change ANY stream parameter mid-session; codec is fixed at connect (client.rs connect_host) and there is no in-session control command.
missingPieces.approach: Add mobile Tauri commands (e.g. set_stream { slot, field, value }) in client.rs that send the same control message pulsar-core uses on desktop (RequestStream / control field). Surface as segmented controls (the .seg atom already exists in ui/index.html) inside the new overlay's Stream card. Skip encoder selection (host-side concern); expose codec / bitrate / resolution / fps / quality.

missingPieces.feature: View-fit + multi-monitor (Display section)
missingPieces.description: Desktop Display view offers fit/stretch/original and a host-monitor picker + screen adaptation (overlay.rs draw_display). Mobile always stretches the native surface, has no fit toggle, and no monitor switching even though multi-monitor IS a remote-desktop feature.
missingPieces.approach: Add a fit-mode setter on the native video plugin (tauri-plugin-pulsar-video) and a monitor-switch command in client.rs forwarding to the host. Render a fit segmented control + monitor list in the overlay Display card. Monitor list comes from the host's QueryStreamCaps/displays already queried at connect.

missingPieces.feature: Audio controls (host transmit / mute host / mic)
missingPieces.description: Desktop Audio view toggles host-audio transmit, mute-host, and mic (Session.svelte:613-615). Mobile has no audio playback wiring shown in-session and no audio toggles at all.
missingPieces.approach: Add transmit/mute/mic toggle commands in client.rs mapping to the same pulsar-core audio control fields; render toggles (the .setting row atom exists in ui/index.html) in an Audio card. Mic capture needs an Android permission + native capture path (defer mic; ship transmit/mute first).

missingPieces.feature: Reverse direction (role swap)
missingPieces.description: Desktop Tools offers 'reverse' to make the host connect back so roles swap (Session.svelte:98-100, reverse cmd). Mobile has no reverse-direction control.
missingPieces.approach: Add a reverse_play Tauri command in client.rs (send the reverse request with this device's id over the session) and a single button in the overlay Tools card. Reuses the existing host go_online path on mobile to receive the inbound connection.

missingPieces.feature: Right-click / scroll / multi-button pointer input
missingPieces.description: Mobile only forwards left-button tap + absolute move (client.rs send_button button:0; ui/index.html sends button 0 only). The desktop input path supports right-click, middle, and wheel (Session/input.svelte.ts onWheel). Practical remote-desktop control needs at least right-click and scroll.
missingPieces.approach: Extend the touch handler in ui/index.html with gestures: two-finger tap -> right click (button 2), two-finger drag -> wheel scroll, long-press -> right click. send_button already takes a button index; add an InputEvent::Scroll/Wheel path in client.rs and forward it. Design a small on-screen hint for the gestures.

missingPieces.feature: On-screen keyboard for remote text input
missingPieces.description: Remote desktop needs to type into the host. Mobile forwards no keyboard at all (no key command in mobile/src/client.rs; only pointer/button). Desktop forwards keys via the OS-level capture (kbdhook/evdev).
missingPieces.approach: Add a keyboard toggle in the overlay that focuses a hidden <input> to raise the soft keyboard; translate keydown/composition to InputEvent::Key{down} and add a send_key Tauri command in client.rs forwarding over the session. Map mobile KeyboardEvent.key to the evdev codes pulsar-core expects (port keymap.ts logic).

missingPieces.feature: Connection/transport indicator detail
missingPieces.description: Desktop shows P2P vs Relay clearly (Session.svelte:102) and a live connection state. Mobile sets 'oturum'/'p2p' into bar-meta only once at connect (ui/index.html:322) and never reflects relay vs P2P explicitly or reconnect state.
missingPieces.approach: Have connect_host return transport (direct/relay) and show a 'P2P'/'Relay' chip in the in-session bar; reuse the existing net-pill styling. Update bar-meta from a transport field already derivable in client.rs (ConnectResult.mos).

findings.notes: Desktop in-session UI is the NATIVE egui overlay (crates/pulsar-render/src/overlay.rs) drawn over the video on every platform; the webview Menu was removed (Session.svelte:816-819). Remote-mode root = 8 boxes + End (overlay.rs:711-720). Mobile has NO pulsar-render and no egui — its only in-session control is a DOM glass pill in ui/index.html:257-267 with status + Split + red End ('Kes'); End invokes plugin:pulsar-video|detach (line 366). Touch forwards only left-tap + absolute move (ui/index.html:379-390, client.rs send_pointer/send_button). So any mobile overlay must be built as DOM over the transparent webview using the existing token atoms (.card/.seg/.setting/.btn already in ui/index.html), NOT ported from egui. Per product rules, do NOT propose file-transfer/clipboard/chat in GAME mode, but this area is REMOTE mode where the desktop legitimately exposes Files/Clipboard/Chat/Mic/Multi-monitor — I left full file-manager/chat out of scope here (they are their own audit areas) and focused on the toolbar/dock controls: stream/display/audio/tools/reverse/fullscreen/stats plus the missing keyboard and right-click/scroll input that make the remote toolbar usable. Mobile mode is currently hardcoded toward a single touch view; the bottom bar is shared between remote and game with no mode-awareness (no [data-mode] switching of the in-session bar). The TEMP auto-go-online + self-connect block (ui/index.html:414-424) is diagnostic scaffolding, not a feature.

findings.area: Clipboard sync (remote-desktop only)
desktopCapabilities: Client -> host: read the local OS clipboard and push it to the remote as DataMsg::Clipboard over the encrypted session (sidechannels.svelte.ts:68-85 -> send_clipboard command io_cmds.rs:18-27 -> send_data client.rs:117)
Host receives a client's clipboard push, shows a toast with a Copy button, and can write it to the host OS clipboard (host.rs:1386-1391 on_clipboard event -> HostChat.svelte:36-77)
Client receives the host's clipboard push (onDataClip) and auto-writes it to the local OS clipboard, with a status flash + in-video toast (sidechannels.svelte.ts:50-55)
App-side OS clipboard read/write via arboard, used because WebKitGTK's navigator.clipboard fails when occluded (io_cmds.rs:217-245, api.dom.ts:6-65)
Bidirectional, mode-gated to remote-desktop only (NOT shown in game mode) per desktop-app/CLAUDE.md two-usage-modes table
Wire + transport already in pulsar-core: DataMsg::Clipboard(String) wire.rs:457-458, send_data/decode_data client.rs:116-135, host on_clipboard handler service/host.rs:211

missingPieces.feature: Outbound clipboard: send phone clipboard text to the remote host (client -> host)
missingPieces.description: Mobile can connect and stream but has no way to push its local clipboard to the remote. pulsar-core's send_data + DataMsg::Clipboard are already linked (used indirectly via client.rs) but no Tauri command exposes a clipboard push, and the session sender stored in InputSenders (client.rs:178) is only used for pointer/button. Must add a send_clipboard command that reuses the per-slot SessionSender, plus a touch-first UI control. Remote-desktop mode only - must NOT appear when the in-session bar is in game mode.
missingPieces.approach: Rust: add a `send_clipboard(slot, text)` #[tauri::command] in desktop-app/mobile/src/client.rs mirroring send_pointer/send_button - look up the SessionSender from InputSenders (client.rs:32-38) and call pulsar_core::service::send_data(&s, &DataMsg::Clipboard(text)) (client.rs:116-119, import DataMsg from pulsar_core::service). Register it in desktop-app/mobile/src/lib.rs:30-37 invoke_handler. UI: in desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html add a clipboard icon-btn to the in-session .bar (lines 257-267), shown only when the session mode is 'remote' (track the connect mode from the m-remote/m-game seg, lines 287-289). On tap read text via navigator.clipboard.readText() (already used for paste-id at lines 393-399) and core.invoke('send_clipboard', { slot, text }); flash a small confirmation toast. Reuse the existing data-mode theming so the button hides in game mode.

missingPieces.feature: Inbound clipboard: receive the remote host's clipboard onto the phone (host -> client)
missingPieces.description: The mobile session read loop (client.rs:189-220) only parses TAG_VIDEO/TAG_AUDIO media and never decodes side-channel DataMsg, so any DataMsg::Clipboard the host pushes is silently dropped. Need to call service::decode_data on non-media frames, emit a Tauri event to the webview, and have the webview write it to the Android/iOS clipboard. The desktop auto-copies inbound clipboard to the local OS clipboard (sidechannels.svelte.ts:50-55); mobile should do the same or show a 'tap to copy' toast (mobile clipboard write may need a user gesture / a Rust-side clipboard write).
missingPieces.approach: Rust: in the read loop in desktop-app/mobile/src/client.rs, when service::media::parse returns None (not media), call pulsar_core::service::decode_data(&bytes) (client.rs:127-135); on Some(DataMsg::Clipboard(text)) use app.emit (tauri::Manager already imported) to fire a 'clipboard-in' event carrying { slot, text }. UI: in desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html add window.__TAURI__.event.listen('clipboard-in', ...) and write the text with navigator.clipboard.writeText (fallback to a 'Panoya kopyala' toast button if the write is blocked without a gesture). If browser clipboard write proves unreliable on Android WebView, add a small write_clipboard Rust command using a Tauri clipboard plugin and invoke it from the listener. Only act on inbound clipboard in remote mode.

findings.notes: Clipboard sync is bidirectional on desktop and fully scaffolded in pulsar-core (DataMsg::Clipboard at wire.rs:457-458, send_data/decode_data at client.rs:116-135), which the mobile crate ALREADY links. So this is one of the cheaper side channels to bring to mobile - no new Rust core work, just wiring two thin commands/event paths plus touch UI. Two real blockers to verify during impl: (1) the mobile session read loop client.rs:189-220 never inspects non-media frames, so inbound clipboard is dropped today; (2) Android/iOS WebView clipboard writes often need a user gesture, so the inbound path may need a Rust-side clipboard write (a Tauri clipboard plugin) rather than navigator.clipboard.writeText. Per desktop-app/CLAUDE.md the clipboard is remote-desktop-only and must be hidden in game mode (the mobile in-session .bar at index.html:257-267 currently has only split+end and no mode awareness, so a mode flag must be threaded through). Note index.html lines 414-424 contain a TEMP self-connect diagnostic block that should be removed before shipping this UI. The desktop's HostChat 'show toast + Copy button' inbound UX (HostChat.svelte:36-77) is a good touch-first reference for the mobile inbound toast.

findings.area: File transfer + remote file browser (remote-desktop only)
desktopCapabilities: Two-pane AnyDesk-style file manager (Files.svelte): LEFT = local device, RIGHT = remote host; both HOME-jailed, '/'-separated, breadcrumb navigation, dirs-first sort

Remote directory listing: client sends DataMsg::FsList(path) via fs_list command (session_cmds.rs:1321); host answers asynchronously with FsEntries over the encrypted session (fs_browse.rs make_on_fs), surfaced as the 'fs-entries' event
Local directory listing: local_ls command (fs_browse.rs:334) lists the client's own HOME (same jail + JSON shape so both panes behave identically)

Download (host->client): fs_get command (session_cmds.rs:1337) sends DataMsg::FsGet; host streams FileBegin/FileChunk/FileEnd; client reassembles in play/hold.rs (per-transfer-id BTreeMap, 8 concurrent cap, idle sweep, 2 GiB ceiling) and saves to '~/Pulsar Alınanlar' via files::save_received_file_chunks; result surfaced as 'file-recv'/'file-begin' events

Upload (client->host): two paths — send_file (io_cmds.rs:86) pushes webview-read bytes (50 MB cap, native file picker via fileInput), and send_file_path (io_cmds.rs:130 -> fs_browse::send_file_at) streams a HOME-jailed local file straight from disk with no size cap

Concurrency + reliability machinery on the client: MAX_WIRE_XFERS=6 wire-limit queue, no-response/safety timeouts, xferId-keyed pending map (Files.svelte), filename sanitization mirrored Rust<->JS (files.rs / Files.svelte)
Dedicated per-session file-manager WINDOW (files_window.rs open_files_window, FilesWindow.svelte) so the file manager can float separately from the session, force-closed on session end

Security hardening: symlink-escape-safe HOME jail (fs_browse::resolve_jailed), filename sanitization incl. Windows reserved names/ADS, wire-budget capping with a '… N daha' sentinel row (fs_browse::cap_for_wire), TOCTOU-safe create_new save

missingPieces.feature: Mobile session read-loop must decode DataMsg side-channel messages
missingPieces.description: The mobile client read loop only inspects RTP video/audio (service::media::parse) and never calls service::decode_data, so every FsEntries/FileBegin/FileChunk/FileEnd the host sends is silently dropped. This is the foundational blocker: without it NO file feature can work on mobile. Need to branch the recv loop: if it parses as media -> existing path; else try service::decode_data(&bytes) and route DataMsg variants to a new file-transfer handler + emit Tauri events to the webview.
missingPieces.approach: In desktop-app/mobile/src/client.rs read loop (around the `match tokio::time::timeout(... sess.recv())` block, lines 190-208), after the media::parse branch fails, call pulsar_core::service::decode_data(&bytes). Port the inbound reassembler from desktop-app/src-tauri/src/play/hold.rs (FileBegin/FileChunk/FileEnd state machine: per-xferId BTreeMap, 8-concurrent cap, idle sweep, MAX_XFER_BYTES ceiling) into a new mobile module. On FsEntries emit a 'fs-entries' event (app.emit); on FileBegin emit 'file-begin'; on completed FileEnd save the bytes (see next item) and emit 'file-recv'. Mirror the FilePayload/FsEntries event shapes from desktop-app/src-tauri/src/events.rs so the webview JS matches.

missingPieces.feature: Remote file browser commands on mobile (fs_list / fs_get)
missingPieces.description: Mobile has no command to request a remote directory listing or a remote file. The desktop fs_list/fs_get commands (session_cmds.rs) live in pulsar-tauri (not compiled for mobile). Need thin mobile equivalents that send DataMsg::FsList / DataMsg::FsGet over the per-slot SessionSender already stored in client::InputSenders.
missingPieces.approach: Add #[tauri::command] fs_list(slot,path) and fs_get(slot,path) in a new desktop-app/mobile/src/files.rs. Reuse the existing client::InputSenders map (mobile/src/client.rs:32) to get the SessionSender for the slot, then call pulsar_core::service::send_data(&session, &DataMsg::FsList{path}) — note: send_data takes &Session; the mobile side only keeps a SessionSender, so either add a SessionSender::send_data helper in pulsar-core/src/service/client.rs (mirroring send_input_via) or store the Session. Register both commands in mobile/src/lib.rs invoke_handler.

missingPieces.feature: Saving downloaded files to Android/iOS storage (Pulsar Alınanlar equivalent)
missingPieces.description: Desktop saves downloads to '~/Pulsar Alınanlar' via files::save_received_file_chunks (files.rs). On Android there is no usable HOME; writes must go through scoped storage / the Downloads MediaStore (or the app's external files dir + a share/open intent), and iOS uses the app sandbox + share sheet. files.rs cannot be reused as-is.
missingPieces.approach: Add a mobile save helper in desktop-app/mobile/src/files.rs. Simplest first cut: write into the app's external files dir (resolvable via tauri path API) under a 'Pulsar Alınanlar' subdir, reusing the dedup-suffix + .part-then-rename logic ported from files::save_received_file_chunks. For user-visible Downloads on Android, add tauri-plugin-fs / a small Kotlin MediaStore insert (the tauri-plugin-pulsar-video plugin already shows the native-plugin pattern to copy). On iOS, present the share sheet. Keep MAX_XFER_BYTES ceiling from files.rs:13.

missingPieces.feature: Mobile local-pane listing + upload (local_ls / send_file_path equivalent) — content-URI based
missingPieces.description: Desktop's local pane uses local_ls over the client's HOME and uploads via send_file_path (disk path). On mobile there is no browsable HOME filesystem; the platform idiom is the system file/document picker returning a content:// URI. So the local pane should be replaced by a native picker for uploads, not a HOME tree.
missingPieces.approach: Do NOT port local_ls (no meaningful mobile HOME tree). Instead add a mobile pick-and-send command: use a file/document picker (tauri-plugin-dialog or a small native plugin) to get a URI, read its bytes, and stream them as FileBegin/FileChunk/FileEnd over the SessionSender — reuse the chunker logic from io_cmds.rs:86 send_file (CHUNK=2048 to stay under the macOS host UDP datagram limit noted in fs_browse.rs:22) and next_transfer_id. Touch-first UI: a single 'Gönder' button that opens the OS picker, plus a transfer-progress list. Drop the two-pane desktop layout for upload.

missingPieces.feature: Touch-first mobile file-manager UI (remote browser + download/upload + transfer status)
missingPieces.description: The mobile UI (ui/index.html, one plain HTML/JS file, grep 'file' = 0 hits) has NOTHING for files. The desktop Files.svelte two-pane 320px layout is not touchable/portrait-friendly and must be redesigned. Needs: a remote-only single-column directory browser with breadcrumb + large tap targets, per-file download button, an upload entry point (OS picker), and a transfer/progress list with success/fail feedback. Remote-desktop mode ONLY — must be absent in game mode per product rules.
missingPieces.approach: Add a Files panel/sheet to desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html using withGlobalTauri (window.__TAURI__.core.invoke + event.listen, matching how connect_host/send_pointer are already called). Single-column remote browser: tap a folder row to invoke('fs_list',{slot,path}); render the 'fs-entries' event payload; tap a file's download icon to invoke('fs_get',...) and track it against 'file-begin'/'file-recv' events (port the xferId pending-map + concurrency-queue logic from Files.svelte:113-291, simplified). Bottom 'Gönder' opens the picker (previous item). Reuse the existing i18n keys (files.*/session.file* in i18n.tr.ts:489-514). Gate the whole panel behind the remote (non-game) connection mode. Style touch-first (44px rows, oklch tokens from design/project/assets/tokens.css, electric-indigo accent).

missingPieces.feature: Transfer progress + cancel + reliability machinery on mobile
missingPieces.description: Desktop has rich client-side reliability (MAX_WIRE_XFERS=6 wire limit + queue, no-response/safety timeouts, xferId-keyed slots in Files.svelte; 8-concurrent reassembler cap + idle sweep in hold.rs). Mobile has none of this and additionally wants visible progress/cancel for large transfers over mobile networks. Without the wire-limit queue, mobile would trip the host/reassembler 8-concurrent cap and silently drop transfers.
missingPieces.approach: Port the concurrency queue + timeout logic from Files.svelte (enqueueXfer/startXfer, MAX_WIRE_XFERS, DOWNLOAD_NO_RESPONSE_MS/SAFETY timers, waitingByName/pendingById maps) into the mobile UI JS. For visible progress, extend the mobile reassembler (files.rs from item 1) to emit a periodic 'file-progress' event (bytes received / total from FileBegin.size) — desktop does not do this, so it is a mobile addition; design a compact progress row in index.html. Cancel = stop tracking the slot client-side (the host stream just gets ignored; no cancel wire message exists today, acceptable for v1).

findings.notes: File transfer + remote file browser is COMPLETELY ABSENT on mobile (grep 'file' in desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html = 0 hits; mobile/src has no fs/file code; mobile/src/lib.rs:30-37 registers only connect_host/send_pointer/send_button/go_online/open_a11y_settings/a11y_enabled). The single hard blocker is that mobile/src/client.rs's read loop (lines 190-208) only handles RTP media and never calls pulsar_core::service::decode_data, so all host->client DataMsg (FsEntries/FileBegin/FileChunk/FileEnd) are dropped on the floor — fix that first (priority-1 items). The wire protocol itself is fully shared and portable: DataMsg + FsEntry live in pulsar-core/src/service/wire.rs and the SessionSender is already held per-slot in client::InputSenders, so the Rust side is mostly porting hold.rs's reassembler + thin fs_list/fs_get senders, NOT new protocol work. The genuinely new design work is mobile-specific: (a) Android/iOS storage for saved downloads (no '~/Pulsar Alınanlar' HOME), (b) replacing the desktop local-HOME pane with a native document picker for uploads, and (c) a touch-first single-column remote browser + transfer-progress UI. IMPORTANT product guard: this whole feature is remote-desktop ONLY — it must be hidden in game mode (CLAUDE.md two-modes table explicitly excludes file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor from the game UI). Reusable assets already in place: i18n keys files.*/session.file* (i18n.tr.ts:489-514), the CHUNK=2048 UDP-safe chunker (io_cmds.rs:86, fs_browse.rs:22), filename sanitization (files.rs), and the full client-side concurrency/timeout logic to copy from Files.svelte:113-303.

findings.area: In-session text chat (remote-desktop only)
desktopCapabilities: Bidirectional text chat over the encrypted session via the DataMsg::Chat(String) side-channel variant (desktop-app/crates/pulsar-core/src/service/wire.rs:350,397,460) -- rides the same held session as the video, no extra connection.
Client -> host send: send_chat(id,text) Tauri command pushes DataMsg::Chat (desktop-app/src-tauri/src/io_cmds.rs:31).
Host -> client reply: host_send_chat(sid,text) routed per session id so two panes from one device reply to the right pane (desktop-app/src-tauri/src/io_cmds.rs:45).
Host-side chat backlog persisted in AppState.chat_log (peer,text,me), capped at 500 lines, exposed via chat_log() command to reseed a re-opened window (io_cmds.rs:65-82, host.rs:1402-1419).

Inbound delivery events: 'chat-msg' to the client UI (peer = play id) and 'host-chat' to the host UI (peer = device id) -- desktop-app/src-tauri/src/events.rs:62-63, host.rs:1418; bridged as onChatMsg/onHostChat (desktop-app/src/lib/api.events.ts:81-84).

Client UI: SessionSideChannels owns a chat panel with message list, composer, unread badge, auto-scroll; only shown in remote-desktop mode as a session side channel (desktop-app/src/lib/screens/Session/sidechannels.svelte.ts:120-133, wired in Session.svelte:185,454,742-747).

Native gaming overlay Chat view: render_chat(id,dir,text) draws inbound/outbound lines on the native renderer and renderToast surfaces inbound chat while the overlay is closed (api.commands.ts:221-227, Session.svelte:183-189).
Host UI: per-device message modal with full conversation history + composer in Connections.svelte (lines 130-166,247-265) plus inbound toasts in HostChat.svelte.
Chat is explicitly a remote-desktop-only side channel (alongside clipboard/file/mic); the gaming overlay can still show a native Chat log but it is not part of the slim game menu.

missingPieces.feature: Wire chat into the mobile session read loop + event bridge
missingPieces.description: The mobile client read loop only inspects media-tagged datagrams (TAG_VIDEO/TAG_AUDIO) and silently drops everything else; non-media DataMsg::Chat is never parsed or surfaced. There is no Rust command to SEND a chat line and no Tauri event emitted to the webview for an inbound line. This is the foundational plumbing every other piece depends on.
missingPieces.approach: In desktop-app/mobile/src/client.rs connect_host read loop (lines 189-220), after the service::media::parse() branch, add an else branch that decodes the datagram as a DataMsg (the session already yields decrypted bytes; mirror how the desktop play loop deserializes). On DataMsg::Chat(text) emit a Tauri event e.g. app2.emit('chat-msg', {slot, text}) so the webview can show it. Add a new #[tauri::command] send_chat(app, slot, text) next to send_pointer/send_button (client.rs:36-53) that looks up the slot's SessionSender from InputSenders and calls a service send-data helper with DataMsg::Chat(text) (use the same data-send path pulsar-core exposes for the desktop send_chat; if no public helper exists for SessionSender, add one in service/client.rs alongside send_input_via). Register send_chat in the invoke_handler in desktop-app/mobile/src/lib.rs. Restrict emit/command to remote (game_mode=false) sessions to keep chat remote-desktop-only.

missingPieces.feature: Touch-first in-session chat panel UI (client side)
missingPieces.description: Mobile has no chat surface at all. The in-session control bar (ui/index.html:256-267) only has connection status, a split toggle and an end button. A mobile user in a remote-desktop session cannot read or send chat lines. Needs a slide-up/over panel with a message list, unread badge on a new chat icon-button in the bar, a text composer + send, and keyboard-aware layout over the transparent video surface (Path A).
missingPieces.approach: Add a chat icon-button to the .bar in desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html (only render it when the active session is remote mode, not game) with an unread badge. Build a bottom-sheet chat panel (plain HTML/JS, matching the existing card/btn atoms and tokens.css colors -- electric indigo accent) that sits above the transparent native video surface; ensure it raises above the soft keyboard (viewport/visualViewport resize handling) since the webview overlays a native decoder surface. Listen for the new 'chat-msg' event via window.__TAURI__.event.listen to append inbound lines and bump the unread badge while closed; on send call core.invoke('send_chat', {slot, text}) and optimistically append the outbound line. Echo my-vs-them styling like the desktop messages list. Keep it portrait+landscape friendly (touch targets >= 44px).

missingPieces.feature: Host-role inbound chat handling on mobile (optional, lower priority)
missingPieces.description: When the mobile device acts as host (go_online path in host.rs), an inbound chat line from a connecting desktop/mobile client is not handled or shown -- there is no host-side chat surface on mobile. Desktop shows this in the Connections per-device modal/toasts. Lower priority because the primary mobile use case is thin client.
missingPieces.approach: In desktop-app/mobile/src/host.rs, in the per-session serve/read path, decode inbound DataMsg::Chat and emit a 'host-chat' event ({peer,text}); add a host_send_chat command mirroring the desktop io_cmds.rs:45 routing by session/peer. In ui/index.html add a lightweight host-side chat notification/toast + reply affordance on the Cihazim tab. Reuse the same send-data helper added in piece 1. Defer until host role on mobile is a real target.

findings.notes: Chat is product-scoped to remote-desktop sessions only (it is a session side channel alongside clipboard/file/mic, per desktop-app/CLAUDE.md two-modes table); any mobile chat UI must be gated to remote mode and NOT shown in the slim game menu. Mobile status is unambiguously absent: `grep -rin chat desktop-app/mobile/` returns nothing. Root cause is structural: the mobile read loop (client.rs:189-220) only handles media-tagged RTP and never deserializes non-media DataMsg, and the webview gets no Tauri events except via the video plugin -- so piece 1 (wire plumbing + event bridge + send_chat command) is the prerequisite for any UI work. The desktop already proves the full path end to end (DataMsg::Chat -> send_chat/host_send_chat -> chat-msg/host-chat events -> SessionSideChannels / Connections modal), so the mobile work is mainly re-plumbing the existing pulsar-core data channel and designing a touch-first panel, not new protocol. Note the keyboard-over-native-surface concern: on Path A the webview is transparent over a native decoder surface, so the chat composer must handle the soft keyboard via visualViewport to avoid being obscured. The mobile pieces should reuse the existing tokens/atoms in ui/index.html for visual consistency; no SvelteKit involved (single HTML/JS file).

findings.area: Microphone / two-way audio (remote-desktop only)
desktopCapabilities: Client mic capture to raw s16le/48kHz/mono PCM via spawn_mic_recorder (Linux parecord/pw-record/arecord, Windows ffmpeg WASAPI, macOS ffmpeg AVFoundation) - desktop-app/src-tauri/src/audio_io.rs:70
mic_start/mic_stop Tauri commands stream DataMsg::Audio frames (~20ms) over the held encrypted session and emit DataMsg::AudioEnd on stop - desktop-app/src-tauri/src/io_cmds.rs:142-215

Host plays the incoming client mic in real time via spawn_audio_player (paplay/pw-cat/aplay) wired through make_on_audio -> on_audio handler - desktop-app/src-tauri/src/audio_io.rs:14, desktop-app/src-tauri/src/host.rs:1427,1669
Core routes DataMsg::Audio/AudioEnd to the host's on_audio handler - desktop-app/crates/pulsar-core/src/service/host.rs:216

UI mic toggle + 'Voice call' switch that pairs mic (us->host) with host audio (host->us) - desktop-app/src/lib/screens/Session/sidechannels.svelte.ts:111-117, desktop-app/src/lib/screens/Session.svelte:611-621
Mic exposed only in the remote-desktop menu, deliberately excluded from game mode per product rules - desktop-app/CLAUDE.md two-usage-modes table

missingPieces.feature: Client-side microphone capture + upstream (phone mic -> remote host)
missingPieces.description: The mobile client can already RECEIVE host audio (Opus/RTP decoded natively via pulsar_video().feed_audio, mobile/src/client.rs:199-205) but has NO path to capture the phone's microphone and send it to the host. There are no mic_start/mic_stop commands in the mobile handler list (mobile/src/lib.rs:30-37), no DataMsg::Audio is ever sent from the mobile client, the in-session control bar (mobile/ui/index.html:257-267) has only split + end buttons, and AndroidManifest.xml (mobile/gen/android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml) declares only INTERNET (no RECORD_AUDIO). This is the core two-way-audio gap: mobile is receive-only today.
missingPieces.approach: 1) Add RECORD_AUDIO permission to mobile/gen/android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml and request it at runtime before first capture. 2) Implement native mic capture in the pulsar-video Android plugin: use Android AudioRecord (16kHz/48kHz mono PCM, VOICE_COMMUNICATION source for AEC/NS) feeding either raw PCM or, better, an Opus encoder, exposed as a new plugin command/callback. Add matching desktop.rs no-op + mobile.rs binding in crates/tauri-plugin-pulsar-video/src/{desktop.rs,mobile.rs,commands.rs} and the Kotlin/Android side. 3) Add Rust commands mic_start/mic_stop in mobile/src/client.rs mirroring desktop io_cmds.rs: pull captured PCM/Opus from the plugin and sess.sender().send DataMsg::Audio frames over the session, sending DataMsg::AudioEnd on stop (reuse the InputSenders sender already stored at mobile/src/client.rs:178). Note desktop host expects raw s16le PCM in DataMsg::Audio (it pipes straight to paplay/aplay), so unless an Opus negotiation is added the mobile client must send raw PCM to interop with desktop hosts. 4) Register the new commands in mobile/src/lib.rs invoke_handler. 5) Add a touch-first mic toggle button to the in-session bar in mobile/ui/index.html (only in remote mode, never game mode) with an active/recording state, calling core.invoke('mic_start'/'mic_stop', {id}). Mute on background (visibilitychange) to avoid hot-mic.

missingPieces.feature: Mobile host plays a connecting client's microphone (phone-as-host)
missingPieces.description: When the phone is the HOST (Cihazim / go_online), serve_with is invoked with DataHandlers::default() (mobile/src/host.rs:146,158), so on_audio is a no-op and any client mic PCM (DataMsg::Audio) arriving over the session is silently discarded. The desktop host plays it via spawn_audio_player; mobile has no equivalent native PCM playback wired into the data handler.
missingPieces.approach: Wire data.on_audio in mobile/src/host.rs to feed inbound DataMsg::Audio PCM into a native Android AudioTrack (new plugin method, e.g. pulsar_video().play_mic_pcm(&pcm) / feed_mic_pcm, analogous to feed_audio but for the host playback direction; or reuse an AudioTrack in STREAM_VOICE_CALL mode). Handle DataMsg::AudioEnd to flush/stop. Add the plugin method to crates/tauri-plugin-pulsar-video/src/{mobile.rs,desktop.rs} plus the Android Kotlin side. Lower priority since phone-as-host two-way voice is a less common scenario than phone-as-client.

findings.notes: Important nuance: host->client audio (Opus/RTP, the host's system sound) is already working on mobile (mobile/src/client.rs:199-205 via pulsar_video().feed_audio); that belongs to the audio-streaming area, not the mic area. The mic area specifically = the UPSTREAM client->host PCM voice channel, which is entirely absent on mobile in both roles. Wire-protocol support already exists end to end (DataMsg::Audio/AudioEnd in pulsar-core service::host/wire), so no proto changes are needed - only mobile capture/playback + UI. Interop caveat: desktop hosts consume DataMsg::Audio as RAW s16le 48kHz mono PCM piped directly to paplay/aplay (audio_io.rs:10), so the mobile client should send raw PCM (not Opus) for the first cut to interop, or add Opus negotiation on both sides. Keep the mic UI strictly remote-desktop-only; per desktop-app/CLAUDE.md the game-mode menu must NOT show mic. The phone's mic capture should prefer Android VOICE_COMMUNICATION source to get hardware AEC/noise-suppression for a usable voice channel.

findings.area: Multi-monitor selection (remote-desktop only)
desktopCapabilities: Host advertises all streamable monitors via StreamCaps::displays (Vec<DisplayInfo>{idx,name,width,height,primary}) on QueryStreamCaps — wire.rs:113-121; primary at index 0
Initial monitor chosen at connect through StreamReq::display_idx (wire.rs:229); host capture path indexes the same pulsar_capture::list_displays() enumeration

Live in-session monitor switch via Tauri command set_play_monitor(id, display_idx) -> sends Restream::Display(idx) over the session's mpsc to the host stream loop, which stops capture and rebuilds the DXGI/VAAPI pipeline on the new output (session_cmds.rs:619-724)

Per-session leading-edge debounce (MON_COOLDOWN_MS=400ms, MON_STATE) so spamming the picker collapses to first+final instead of N decoder teardowns; forget_monitor_debounce on stop_stream (session_cmds.rs:576-612)
TS bridge api.setPlayMonitor (api.commands.ts:195-197); HostDisplay type (api.types.ts:68-76); host_displays carried on the play result (api.types.ts:52-54)

UI: controls.svelte.ts streamDisplay state + setMonitor(idx) with a 4000ms stall-detector suppression window (controls.svelte.ts:42,150-161); picker rendered in Session.svelte, dispatched via the 'display' overlay command (Session.svelte:54-55,87,592), labels session.monitor='Ekran'/session.monitorPrimary='ana' (i18n.tr.ts:522-523)

Bonus desktop-only (Windows): Parsec-style screen adaptation in display_mode.rs (set_best_mode/revert) toggled by set_play_adapt — switches the captured monitor's resolution to best-fit a pane and reverts on teardown; no-op stub on non-Windows
This is explicitly a remote-desktop-mode feature: the two-mode table in desktop-app/CLAUDE.md lists multi-monitor under Remote Desktop and NOT in the slim game-mode menu

missingPieces.feature: Receive and store the host's monitor list on connect
missingPieces.description: Mobile calls query_stream_caps (client.rs:135) but only reads caps.codecs/caps.features and throws away caps.displays. The phone never learns the host has >1 monitor, so it cannot offer a picker. The connect StreamReq also hard-codes display_idx: 0 (client.rs:162), always streaming the primary.
missingPieces.approach: In client.rs after `let caps = query_stream_caps(...)`, capture `caps.displays` (Vec<DisplayInfo> from pulsar_core::service). Serialize each to a small struct {idx,name,width,height,primary} and emit it to the webview via a Tauri event (e.g. app.emit('host-displays', ...)) alongside the existing connected detail, AND stash it in a per-slot shared state (parallel to InputSenders) so a later switch command can validate the idx. Keep display_idx:0 as the default initial capture (matches desktop default). Only surface the picker when displays.len()>1 and game_mode is false (multi-monitor is remote-desktop-only).

missingPieces.feature: set_play_monitor command + in-session restream-on-new-monitor path
missingPieces.description: There is no mobile command to switch the streamed monitor, and no plumbing to re-request the stream on a new display_idx. Unlike desktop (which owns an mpsc Restream channel into the host stream loop), the mobile read loop in client.rs owns `sess` directly with no restream channel, so a live switch needs a way to re-issue request_stream(display_idx=N) on the existing session (and re-arm the native decoder via pulsar_video().start_stream for the new SPS).
missingPieces.approach: Add a Tauri command `set_play_monitor(app, slot, display_idx)` in client.rs and register it in lib.rs:30 invoke_handler. Mirror the desktop Restream pattern: introduce a per-slot tokio mpsc<u32> (display switch) stored in shared state next to InputSenders; the read loop selects! over recv()/timeout AND this channel, and on a new idx calls request_stream(&mut sess, &StreamReq{display_idx:idx, ..same as initial}) then re-arms app.pulsar_video().start_stream(mime, slot) so the native surface accepts the new SPS/resolution. Reuse the desktop's 400ms leading-edge debounce idea to avoid stacking reopens. Validate idx against the stored host_displays count from the previous piece.

missingPieces.feature: Touch-first monitor picker UI in the mobile session overlay
missingPieces.description: ui/index.html has zero monitor/screen-picker UI (no monitor/display/screen tokens anywhere in the file). The in-session control bar (.bar, only End wired) has no screen-switch affordance. The desktop picker is a session-menu list keyed off hostDisplays — it must be re-designed as a touch-first sheet for the phone, shown only in remote-desktop mode when >1 monitor exists.
missingPieces.approach: Add a monitor button to the in-session glass control bar (.bar, ui/index.html ~line 115) that opens a bottom sheet listing the host displays received via the 'host-displays' event — each row showing name + WxH + an 'ana' (primary) badge, large tap targets, current selection highlighted with the brand-soft accent (reuse the .item/.seg atoms already in the file and design/project/assets/tokens.css oklch brand color). Tapping a row calls window.__TAURI__.core.invoke('set_play_monitor',{slot,displayIdx}) and shows a brief 'switching' spinner (the stream rebuilds, like desktop's #beginSwitch window). Hide the button entirely in game mode and when displays.length<=1. Plain JS, no SvelteKit, consistent with the existing withGlobalTauri file.

findings.notes: Multi-monitor is a remote-desktop-only feature (desktop-app/CLAUDE.md two-mode table excludes it from game mode), so the mobile picker must be gated to remote mode + displays>1. The full wire protocol already supports it end-to-end (StreamCaps::displays, StreamReq::display_idx, request_stream re-issue) and the mobile host even captures host frames, so this is purely a CLIENT-side gap: mobile discards caps.displays (client.rs:135), hard-codes display_idx:0 (client.rs:162), registers no monitor command (lib.rs:30-37), and has no UI. The key implementation wrinkle vs desktop: desktop switches via an internal Restream mpsc into a long-lived host stream loop; mobile's read loop owns `sess` directly, so the cleanest path is to re-call request_stream on the same session from within the loop via a new per-slot switch channel (piece 2). Do NOT port desktop's Windows-only screen-adaptation (display_mode.rs / set_play_adapt) to mobile — it is a host-side Win32 ChangeDisplaySettingsExW concern and a phone client streaming a Windows host already benefits from it transparently; no mobile work needed. Recommended order: piece 1 (S) -> piece 2 (M) -> piece 3 (M, needs design). Overall priority is moderate (2-3): single-monitor hosts and game-mode sessions are unaffected, but multi-monitor desktops are a core remote-desktop use case.

findings.area: Gaming personality + in-session gaming overlay (perf HUD, combo-toggled rich menu)
desktopCapabilities: Full gaming personality shell: GamingShell.svelte renders an immersive cyan/indigo backdrop, a controller-first home, a games library screen, and a BottomDock instead of the remote sidebar; gaming mode refuses inbound host (pure client) — desktop-app/src/routes/page/GamingShell.svelte:1-217
Mode-aware data-mode='game' theming flips the whole app to cyan accent + slim game-only personality (CLAUDE.md 'Two usage modes'); mode is chosen at connect time and drives menu/overlay/look/encode
In-session gaming overlay is a NATIVE egui overlay drawn by pulsar-render over the video on every platform (desktop-app/crates/pulsar-render/src/overlay.rs, 2099 lines)
Perf HUD: live stat tiles for RTT/latency, FPS, encode ms, decode ms, bitrate target, bandwidth, fed each frame from host Stats push — overlay.rs:24-32, 674-686
Always-on mini stats HUD toggle (stats_hud) persisted and synced to the renderer via setStatsHud — controls.svelte.ts:184-190, Session.svelte:596 (case 'statshud'), overlay.rs:46-48

Combo-toggled rich menu: Ctrl+Shift+M (OS keyboard hook / evdev) opens the overlay WITHOUT ending the session; convergent debounced open/close with input-grab suspend + mpv pause on Linux — SessionUi in ui.svelte.ts:31-110, Session.svelte:561-578
Parsec-style draggable overlay-open hotspot button (Pulsar mark) as an alternative to the combo, position persisted — Session.svelte:285-363, 826-837

Leave-combo hint + in-control auto-fade hint, click-to-engage; leave combo Ctrl+Shift+Q ends a native game session — Session.svelte:509-537
Game mode forces control immediately on connect (Moonlight/Parsec style: kbdEngage right after capture armed) — Session.svelte:149-163

missingPieces.feature: Pass game mode through to the backend so the session is actually a game session
missingPieces.description: Mobile has a Remote/Oyun (Uzak Masaüstü/Oyun) segmented toggle but it ONLY recolors the connect form via data-mode (index.html:287-289). The selected mode is never sent: connect_host is invoked with no mode argument (index.html:319) and the Rust client hardcodes StreamReq.game_mode=false (client.rs:154). So 'Oyun' streams are identical to remote streams — no low-latency encode profile, no gaming personality in-session.
missingPieces.approach: Read the current data-mode in doConnect() and pass mode/game flag in the invoke args. In Rust client.rs add a `game_mode: bool` (or `mode: String`) param to connect_host and thread it into StreamReq.game_mode (replace the hardcoded false at client.rs:154); also set quality to QualityPref latency when game. This is the precondition for everything else gaming.

missingPieces.feature: Touch-toggled in-session perf HUD (latency / fps / bitrate)
missingPieces.description: Desktop draws a native egui perf HUD over the video with RTT/fps/encode/decode/bitrate/bandwidth (overlay.rs:674-686) plus an always-on mini stats HUD toggle. Mobile's in-session UI is a single fixed control bar showing only a static 'Bağlı' label and a codec/transport meta string (index.html:257-267, 322); there is no fps/latency/bitrate readout at all. The mobile video plugin (mobile.rs feed_au/feed_audio) surfaces no decoder stats to JS.
missingPieces.approach: Two parts. (1) Stats source: in the client.rs read loop (client.rs:189-220) already counting frames/aus/audio — derive fps (frames over the 1.5s keepalive window) and emit a Tauri event (e.g. app.emit('mobile-stats', {fps, codec, transport, kbps})). Optionally have the native decoder report decode ms via a new plugin event in mobile.rs. (2) HUD UI: a compact translucent pill/strip overlaying the video, drawn as plain HTML over the transparent webview, listening to the event with @tauri-apps/api event. Touch-toggle: tap a small HUD chip (or long-press) to show/hide; show by default only in game mode (data-mode game). Use cyan accent + JetBrains Mono per tokens.
