NEW touch-first design — desktop's egui tiles must be re-laid-out for a phone (single horizontal mono strip, large enough to read at arm's length, safe-area aware).

missingPieces.feature: Combo/gesture-toggled rich gaming menu (in-session settings)
missingPieces.description: Desktop opens a rich game-only menu via Ctrl+Shift+M (or the draggable hotspot) with Stream/Gauges/Controllers sections that live-switch codec/encoder/res/fps/bitrate/quality/pacing on the host (ui.svelte.ts, overlay.rs:773-868, Session.svelte:585-740). Mobile has NONE of this in-session: the control bar only has Split + End. There is no way to change codec, fps, bitrate, or quality once a mobile session is live.
missingPieces.approach: Mobile has no keyboard combo, so the trigger must be touch/gesture: e.g. a draggable Pulsar-mark hotspot (mirror Session.svelte:826-837) or an edge-swipe that opens a full-height bottom-sheet panel over the transparent webview. The sheet shows game-only controls (codec, fps, bitrate Mbit, quality latency|quality, encoder) — NOT file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor (product-excluded in game mode). Each control invokes a NEW Tauri command that re-issues request_stream / sends a live-switch over the session sender (the desktop equivalents are setCodec/setFps/setBitrate/setQuality in controls.svelte.ts). Backend: add set_play_* commands in client.rs that look up the slot's SessionSender (InputSenders) and send the control message pulsar-core already supports. Acceptable that video freezes/resumes during a switch (per CLAUDE.md gaming-overlay note). Design a touch-first bottom sheet (segmented pills like the existing .seg, big tap targets).

missingPieces.feature: Gaming personality on the connect/library surface (cyan immersive shell + games list)
missingPieces.description: Desktop's gaming mode swaps to an immersive shell and a per-host games library (GamingHome/GamesScreen, openGames→listRemoteGames in GamingShell.svelte:60-96). Mobile's 'Oyun' toggle only changes accent color of the same generic ID-entry form; there is no game library, no host games list, no immersive gaming home. A mobile user in game mode cannot browse/launch a specific game on the host — only blind ID connect.
missingPieces.approach: When data-mode='game', after connect-or-on a dedicated games tab, call a new mobile command list_remote_games(id) wrapping pulsar-core service list/launch games (the same service the desktop uses via api.listRemoteGames). Render the returned GameInfo[] as a touch grid of game cards (artwork from GameInfo.image, the built-in non-deletable 'Desktop' card pinned first, mirroring GamingShell.svelte:73-74). Tapping a card connects with that gameId. NEW touch-first grid design (portrait 2-col / landscape 3-4-col cards), cyan gaming theming. Backend: add list_remote_games + pass gameId into request_stream's launch path in client.rs.

missingPieces.feature: Leave/release hint + game-mode immediate control affordance
missingPieces.description: Desktop game mode engages control immediately and shows a fade-out 'how to release' hint plus a leave combo (Session.svelte:149-163, 509-537). Mobile has no in-session hint, no documented way to release/leave other than the End (X) button — and no controller/leave affordance for game streaming.
missingPieces.approach: Add a brief auto-fading toast over the video on session start in game mode ('Dokun: kontrol · Çubuğu kullan: çıkış' style), reusing the existing .bar styling vocabulary. Since mobile uses on-screen touch (no physical leave combo), the End button in the control bar (index.html:264-266) is the leave path — just surface a transient hint pointing to it and to the (future) overlay hotspot. Pure UI; no backend change.

findings.notes: Mobile gaming is effectively cosmetic-only today: the Oyun toggle recolors the connect card (data-mode='game', index.html:28/287-289) but the mode is never sent — connect_host takes no mode arg (index.html:319) and StreamReq.game_mode is hardcoded false (client.rs:154), so 'game' streams are byte-for-byte the same as remote. In-session the entire surface is one fixed control bar (Bağlı + codec/transport meta + Split + End, index.html:257-267); there is NO perf HUD, NO combo/touch-toggled rich menu, NO live codec/fps/bitrate switching, and NO games library. The desktop's whole gaming overlay is a native egui surface (pulsar-render/src/overlay.rs) driven by host Stats pushes; on mobile the decoder plugin (tauri-plugin-pulsar-video mobile.rs) currently surfaces NO stats to JS, so a HUD needs new event plumbing (cheapest source is the fps already countable in the client.rs read loop, lines 189-220). Per product rules (CLAUDE.md 'Two usage modes' + 'Gaming overlay'), the mobile rich menu must stay game-only: codec/bitrate/fps/res/quality/encoder/controllers — explicitly NOT file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor. Recommended order: (1) thread game_mode through connect [S, unblocks all], (2) perf HUD + (3) touch-toggled rich menu, then (4) game library and (5) hints. All in-session overlays must be plain HTML over the transparent webview (Path A) and re-designed touch-first, not ported from the egui/desktop layout.

findings.area: Stream quality controls: codec, encoder/decoder, bitrate (Mbit), fps, resolution, quality/perf
desktopCapabilities: Persisted pre-connect quality prefs in settings.svelte.ts: quality, res, fps, bitrate (Mbit), codec (auto/h264/h265/av1), encoder (auto/nvenc/qsv/amf/videotoolbox/vaapi/rkmpp/software), hdr, framePacing (UiSettings, settings.svelte.ts:17-77, DEFAULTS:125-150)

Per-platform encoder family filtering: encodersForPlatform() restricts the encoder list to the host OS family (PLATFORM_ENCODERS, settings.svelte.ts:110-123), plus probe-driven availability via available_encoders Tauri cmd (api.commands.ts:117-118) and caps.svelte.ts

LIVE in-session setters that re-request the stream from the host without reconnecting (controls.svelte.ts SessionControls): setRes (1080p/1440p/4K/auto/WxH), setFps (auto/30/60/120), setBitrate (Mbit->kbps), setQuality (latency|quality), setCodec (h264/h265/av1), setEncoder (per-platform), setMonitor, setAdapt, setFramePacing

Each live setter calls a dedicated Tauri command (api.commands.ts:185-206: set_play_resolution/encoder/codec/fps/bitrate/quality) which sends Restream::{Resolution,Encoder,Codec,Fps,Bitrate,Quality} over the held session (session_cmds.rs), and the host kills+rebuilds the ffmpeg/encoder pipeline (Restream(Some(StreamReq)) in service/host.rs:116,167)
A 'switching' veil + stall-detector suppression window during the encoder rebuild (controls.svelte.ts #beginSwitch, 1600-4000ms tuned per change kind)
Decoder is auto-selected and shown read-only (decoder pref intentionally removed, settings.svelte.ts:161)

Game-mode gaming overlay (combo-opened) surfaces the slim quality menu: codec/encoder/fps/quality/bitrate(Mbit)/resolution per desktop-app/CLAUDE.md 'Two usage modes' + 'Gaming overlay' sections; Session.svelte:588-594 dispatches menu picks to the controls setters

missingPieces.feature: Codec preference actually applied at connect (currently ignored)
missingPieces.description: The mobile Settings tab has a 'Codec tercihi' segmented control (Otomatik/H.265/H.264) at ui/index.html:216-222 that only writes localStorage 'pulsar.codec' (index.html:306). connect_host (client.rs:92-169) ignores it entirely: it picks codec = caps.codecs.first() (client.rs:136) and builds StreamReq with codec from caps, encoder 'auto', and decode_codecs = [that codec] (client.rs:144-160). So the user's codec choice never reaches the host, and since the mobile host only advertises h264 (host.rs:152) the round-trip is always h264.
missingPieces.approach: Thread the persisted codec pref into connect_host: add a `codec` param to the #[command] connect_host (client.rs:92) and the JS core.invoke('connect_host', {...}) call (index.html:319) reading LS.get('codec','auto'). When non-auto and present in caps.codecs, use it instead of caps.codecs.first() and set StreamReq.codec + decode_codecs + the native decoder mime (mime_for/codec_enum, client.rs:64-90). Keep falling back to caps.codecs.first() for 'auto'.

missingPieces.feature: Pre-connect bitrate / fps / resolution / quality selection (game mode)
missingPieces.description: Mobile hardcodes StreamReq.width=0, height=0, fps=0, bitrate_kbps=0, quality=QualityPref::default(), game_mode=false (client.rs:148-156). There is NO UI anywhere in ui/index.html to pick bitrate (Mbit), fps, resolution, or quality/perf before connecting. The desktop persists all of these (settings.svelte.ts) and pushes the persisted bitrate to the host on connect (controls.svelte.ts:80-87). Game-mode connect on mobile also never sets game_mode=true, so the host uses the quality-focused (not low-latency) encode profile.
missingPieces.approach: Add a touch-first quality section to the mobile UI shown in/under the Oyun (game) connect path: segmented controls for fps (Oto/30/60/120), resolution (Oto/1080p/1440p/4K), quality (Gecikme/Kalite) and a bitrate stepper/slider in Mbit, persisted to localStorage like the existing codec/netmode segs (seg() helper, index.html:292-296). Pass these (+ derive game_mode from the remote/game mode toggle at index.html:286-289) into connect_host; in client.rs build StreamReq with the chosen width/height/fps/bitrate_kbps (Mbit*1000)/quality and set game_mode for the Oyun path. Reuse the desktop res->WxH mapping logic from controls.svelte.ts:100-109. Mobile is a thin CLIENT so the host still does the encoding; only the request params change.

missingPieces.feature: Live in-session quality controls (change bitrate/fps/res/codec/quality without reconnecting)
missingPieces.description: Desktop changes these live mid-session via Restream::* over the held session (controls.svelte.ts + session_cmds.rs:29-563 + service/host.rs:116,167). On mobile there is NO equivalent: the in-session control bar (index.html:257-267) has only Split and End buttons. The mobile client keeps only a SessionSender for input (InputSenders, client.rs:32, used by send_pointer/send_button) and a spawned RTP read loop (client.rs:181-223); it never sends a Restream/request_stream after the initial one, and there are no set_play_* Tauri commands registered (lib.rs:30-37 only has connect_host/send_pointer/send_button/go_online/a11y).
missingPieces.approach: 1) Expose the in-session restream path: the read loop already owns `sess`; either store sess.sender() is not enough (request_stream needs &mut Session round-trip). Cleanest: keep an mpsc<Restream-like enum> per slot in InputSenders' sibling state, and have the read loop select! on it (mirror service/host.rs Ev::Restream) to call client::request_stream(&mut sess, &new_req) with updated fps/bitrate/res/codec/quality. 2) Add #[command] set_play_bitrate/fps/resolution/codec/quality(slot, ...) in client.rs that push onto that channel, and register them in lib.rs invoke_handler. 3) Build a touch-first in-session quality sheet (bottom-sheet opened from a new gear icon-btn in .bar, index.html:257) with the same segmented controls + bitrate slider; for game sessions follow the gaming-overlay model (video may pause during rebuild). Wire button taps to the new core.invoke set_play_* commands.

missingPieces.feature: Encoder selection awareness (host-side encoder, read-only-friendly on a phone client)
missingPieces.description: Desktop lets you pick the host encoder (nvenc/vaapi/qsv/etc.) and probes available_encoders (api.commands.ts:117). Mobile hardcodes StreamReq.encoder='auto' (client.rs:147) and offers no UI. Since mobile is a thin client connecting to a desktop/phone host, exposing host encoder choice is lower value (the host already auto-selects its best HW encoder), and the desktop CLAUDE.md only lists encoder/decoder in the slim game menu as advanced.
missingPieces.approach: Optional/advanced: after connect, query the host's StreamCaps.encoders (already returned by query_stream_caps, client.rs:135) and present an advanced 'Encoder' segmented control in the in-session quality sheet that feeds StreamReq.encoder through the same live-restream channel as the bitrate/fps work above. Default stays 'auto'. Only worth doing once the live-restream plumbing (priority 3 item) exists. Decoder stays auto/read-only exactly like desktop (settings.svelte.ts:161).

findings.notes: Mobile status is ABSENT for real stream-quality control: the only quality-adjacent UI is the Settings 'Codec tercihi' seg (index.html:216-222) and it is dead-wired (writes localStorage only, never read by connect_host). All StreamReq quality fields are hardcoded to auto/0 (client.rs:144-160), and there is no in-session restream path at all (only Split/End in the control bar, index.html:257-267; lib.rs invoke_handler has no set_play_* commands). The core protocol fully supports everything needed — StreamReq carries codec/encoder/width/height/fps/bitrate_kbps/quality/game_mode (used by client.rs request_stream), and the host honors live Restream(Some(StreamReq)) (service/host.rs:116,167) — so the gap is purely the mobile UI + the param/command wiring, not the Rust core or wire protocol. Note the product rule (desktop-app/CLAUDE.md 'Two usage modes'): codec/bitrate(Mbit)/fps/resolution/quality/encoder are the SLIM GAME-mode menu; do NOT add file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor here. Recommended order: (1) honor the existing codec pref [S], (2) add pre-connect bitrate/fps/res/quality + game_mode for the Oyun path [M], (3) build the in-session live restream channel + bottom-sheet [L].

findings.area: Physical controller support (gamepad detection, virtual pad) on mobile
desktopCapabilities: Event-driven SDL3 physical-pad detection + hotplug (controllers.rs run/refresh loop), 0 CPU idle, up to native report rate (1000Hz)
Normalizes buttons/sticks/triggers into GamepadState (read_state) incl. Y-axis sign fix and trigger 0..32767->0..255
GamepadKind detection from VID/PID (DS3/DS4/DS5/Xbox/Standard) via vid_pid_from_sdl_guid + GamepadKind::from_vid_pid
Forwards each pad over the session as InputEvent::GamepadSlot { slot, kind, target, state } with send-on-change @250Hz + ~1Hz keepalive (play.rs:1498)
Per-pad EmulationTarget (Auto/Xbox360/Ds4) chosen in UI, resolved on host to a virtual pad (host.rs:1170)
Rumble/force-feedback round-trip: host -> Rumble msg -> SdlPads::rumble -> SDL_RumbleGamepad with per-pad strength levels (controllers.rs apply_rumble; spawn_rumble_forward host.rs:1193)
Multi-controller: stable uuid keys, controllerOrder, per-session controller lock for split co-op, disabled set, test-rumble pulse
Battery % per pad (SDL_GetGamepadPowerInfo)
GamepadDisconnect emitted on unplug so host releases the virtual pad

missingPieces.feature: Forward a physical gamepad from the phone to the host (core path)
missingPieces.description: A connected Bluetooth/USB controller on the Android phone produces zero remote input today. The mobile webview never reads the W3C Gamepad API and there is no Tauri command that sends InputEvent::GamepadSlot, even though the host already accepts that wire variant unchanged. This is the single critical gap that makes mobile game streaming with a real pad usable.
missingPieces.approach: Use the browser Gamepad API in ui/index.html (no native plugin needed; Android System WebView exposes navigator.getGamepads()). Add a requestAnimationFrame poll loop (only while body.in-session) that reads gamepad.buttons/axes, maps the standard mapping to Pulsar's button bits (A=buttons[0], B=1, X=2, Y=3, LB=4, RB=5, BACK=8, START=9, L3=10, R3=11, DPAD up/down/left/right=12..15, GUIDE=16; triggers buttons[6]/[7].value*255; axes[0..3] -> i16 via *32767, negate Y to match GamepadState up-positive in read_state controllers.rs:474-479). Send-on-change only (diff against last snapshot) like play.rs:1498, plus ~1Hz keepalive. Add a new Tauri command send_gamepad(slot, buttons, lx, ly, rx, ry, lt, rt, kind) in mobile/src/client.rs mirroring send_button (client.rs:47): build pulsar_core::input::GamepadState, look up the slot's SessionSender from InputSenders, and send_input_via(&s, &InputEvent::GamepadSlot { slot, kind, target: EmulationTarget::Auto, state }). Register it in mobile/src/lib.rs invoke_handler (lib.rs:30). Map gamepadconnected/disconnected events: on disconnect invoke a send_gamepad_disconnect command emitting InputEvent::GamepadDisconnect { slot } so the host releases the virtual pad (host has the variant in wire.rs:56). Kind can default to Xbox/Standard from the Gamepad API id string (no reliable VID/PID in the browser) -> let host Auto-resolve.

missingPieces.feature: Multiple physical controllers + correct player-slot mapping
missingPieces.description: Desktop forwards up to 4 pads with stable uuid->slot ordering and split-pane lock (play.rs second pass). Mobile has no notion of >1 pad; the poll loop must map each navigator.getGamepads() entry to a distinct host player slot. For split mode mobile already routes touch to slot 0/1 (index.html touchTarget), but a physical pad has no on-screen region to infer slot from.
missingPieces.approach: In the index.html poll loop, iterate navigator.getGamepads() and assign each non-null pad a slot by its gamepad.index (0..3), sending send_gamepad with that slot. Reuse the existing send-on-change diff keyed per slot. For mobile split mode (splitActive), keep it simple: forward all physical pads to the connected session's slot 0 host unless a future per-pad picker exists; do NOT replicate the desktop's controller-lock UI initially (it is a desktop split-co-op feature). No new wire work: InputEvent::GamepadSlot already carries slot.

missingPieces.feature: Rumble / force-feedback back to the phone's controller
missingPieces.description: Desktop receives host force-feedback (Rumble msg) and drives the physical pad's motors (controllers.rs apply_rumble, host.rs spawn_rumble_forward). On mobile the read loop in client.rs (client.rs:189) only parses TAG_VIDEO/TAG_AUDIO media and never handles the session's control-channel Rumble messages, and there is no path to vibrate the controller. The Gamepad API on Android WebView exposes the GamepadHapticActuator (playEffect 'dual-rumble') for many pads, so the round-trip is achievable without native code.
missingPieces.approach: On the client read loop in mobile/src/client.rs, also handle the non-media control messages: today connect_host only reads media via service::media::parse (client.rs:192). Add handling for the session's DataMsg/Rumble control frames (see desktop host.rs:565 region and how desktop client consumes Rumble) and emit a Tauri event (e.g. app.emit('rumble', {slot,large,small})). In ui/index.html, listen via window.__TAURI__.event.listen('rumble', ...) and call gamepads[slot].vibrationActuator.playEffect('dual-rumble', { duration: 200, strongMagnitude: large/255, weakMagnitude: small/255 }). Gate on actuator existence (degrade silently). Confirm the exact control-message type pulsar-core surfaces to the client (check service.rs recv path) before wiring; if Rumble currently only flows host-internally, expose it on the client SessionSender/recv similar to desktop.

missingPieces.feature: Connected-controller status indicator in the in-session bar
missingPieces.description: Desktop shows controller status in the gaming overlay (CLAUDE.md gaming overlay: 'perf HUD + controller status'). Mobile's in-session bar (index.html .bar, lines 256-267) shows only codec/transport and Split/End buttons with no controller feedback, so the user has no confirmation their pad is detected and forwarding.
missingPieces.approach: Add a small controller glyph + count to the existing in-session .bar in ui/index.html (a new span next to #bar-meta), toggled by gamepadconnected/gamepaddisconnected events and a live indicator that pulses when buttons change in the poll loop. Pure webview UI; no Rust change. This is the only piece needing a touch-first mobile design decision (placement in the floating glass bar, game-mode cyan accent via the existing [data-mode='game'] tokens). Keep it minimal so it never obstructs the video.

findings.notes: Mobile has NO physical-controller support at all: the webview never touches navigator.getGamepads(), and the only client->host input commands wired are send_pointer/send_button (mobile/src/lib.rs:30-37, mobile/src/client.rs:36-53). The hard part is already done elsewhere: the wire protocol (InputEvent::GamepadSlot / GamepadDisconnect, wire.rs:45-56) is additive and back-compat, and the HOST side fully replays gamepads and forwards rumble (src-tauri/src/host.rs:1170,1193). So bringing mobile to parity does NOT require core/relay/host changes for the basic path -- only a webview poll loop + 1-2 thin Tauri commands in the mobile crate. Mobile deliberately excludes SDL3/gilrs (lib.rs:4-7 lists SDL3 as a non-portable desktop dep), so detection MUST go through the Android WebView Gamepad API, not the desktop's controllers.rs SDL path. Axis/button mapping must match read_state (controllers.rs:453-483): triggers 0..255, sticks i16, left/right Y negated to up-positive; let the host Auto-resolve EmulationTarget since the browser Gamepad API gives no reliable VID/PID for GamepadKind detection (vid_pid_from_sdl_guid is unavailable in-webview). NOTE the index.html currently has a TEMP self-connect diagnostic block (lines 414-424) that should be removed before shipping controller work. Per product rules controllers ARE allowed in BOTH remote and game mode (api.commands.ts:132), so the poll loop should not be gated on game mode.

findings.area: On-screen TOUCH controls for gaming (virtual gamepad overlay) — mobile-specific
desktopCapabilities: Desktop reads PHYSICAL controllers (DS3/4/5, Xbox, standard) via SDL/gilrs in desktop-app/src-tauri/src/controllers.rs (read_state at line 453) and normalizes them to pulsar-core GamepadState (buttons bitmask + left/right sticks i16 + L/R triggers u8).

Desktop forwards controller state over the encrypted session as InputEvent::Gamepad / InputEvent::GamepadSlot{slot,kind,target,state} / GamepadDisconnect (wire enum at crates/pulsar-core/src/service/wire.rs:25-74), supporting up to 4 player slots + emulation-target selection (Auto/Xbox360/Ds4).

Host replays received gamepad state via the VirtualGamepad trait (crates/pulsar-core/src/input/types.rs:180) — real uinput backend on Linux (input/uinput.rs), ViGEm/Xbox360 on Windows (input/vigem.rs), recording stub on macOS/ARM. Host dispatch handles all 3 gamepad variants in src-tauri/src/host.rs:1160-1206.

Desktop has NO on-screen/touch virtual gamepad — it is mouse/keyboard (Session.svelte) or physical pad only. So there is genuinely NO desktop UI analog for a touch overlay; the touch UI must be designed fresh for mobile.

Pure, unit-tested helpers already exist to build/serialize pad state: button:: bit constants, axis_to_i16, trigger_to_u8 (input/types.rs:114-168), so a touch overlay only needs to populate a GamepadState and send it.

missingPieces.feature: send_gamepad Tauri command on the mobile client
missingPieces.description: Mobile only wires send_pointer + send_button (mouse) in client.rs:36-53; there is no command to forward controller state. Need a command that builds a pulsar-core GamepadState and sends it as InputEvent::GamepadSlot over the active session's SessionSender.
missingPieces.approach: Add #[tauri::command] async fn send_gamepad(app, slot:u8, buttons:u32, lx:f64, ly:f64, rx:f64, ry:f64, lt:f64, rt:f64) in desktop-app/mobile/src/client.rs mirroring send_pointer (look up InputSenders by slot, send_input_via). Build GamepadState with axis_to_i16/trigger_to_u8 from pulsar-core (already a dep, see client.rs use of send_input_via/InputEvent). Wrap in InputEvent::GamepadSlot{slot, kind: GamepadKind::Standard, target: EmulationTarget::Auto, state}. Register it in the invoke_handler in desktop-app/mobile/src/lib.rs:30-37. The host side already handles GamepadSlot — no Rust host change needed.

missingPieces.feature: Touch-first on-screen gamepad overlay UI (Moonlight-style)
missingPieces.description: No virtual gamepad overlay exists in the mobile UI (desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html). In game mode (data-mode=game / cyan) over the transparent video surface, the user has no way to send controller input — today touch only forwards an absolute pointer (sendTouch at index.html:379-390), which is wrong for games. Need on-screen dual analog sticks, D-pad, ABXY face buttons, L1/R1/L2/R2 shoulders+triggers, Start/Select, and a guide button.
missingPieces.approach: Design a new touch-first landscape overlay layer in ui/index.html shown only when in-session AND game mode (body.in-session[data-mode=game]). Build with absolutely-positioned touch zones over the transparent video (pointer-events on the controls only, the rest passes through). Left/right floating analog sticks computed from touchstart anchor → touchmove delta, clamped to a radius, normalized to [-1,1]; D-pad + face/shoulder buttons as touch targets toggling bits in a local buttons:u32 mirroring pulsar-core button:: constants (A=1<<0 ... DPAD_RIGHT=1<<14, see input/types.rs:114-131). Use multi-touch (e.touches with identifier tracking) so a stick + a button work simultaneously. On any state change, call core.invoke('send_gamepad', {...}). Throttle to ~60–120 Hz with requestAnimationFrame coalescing (send only on change). Triggers can be digital (button) for v1 or analog drag for v2. Use tokens.css design tokens (cyan game accent, --r-pill, glass blur like .bar) for a Moonlight-class minimal immersive look. Add a control-bar toggle (icon-btn in #bar) to show/hide the pad. Coordinate with the existing pointer touch handler so pointer forwarding is disabled while the gamepad overlay is active in game mode (gate sendTouch in index.html:379 on !gamepadActive).

missingPieces.feature: GamepadDisconnect on session end + slot wiring
missingPieces.description: When the game session ends (#btn-end at index.html:366) or the pad overlay is toggled off, the host's virtual pad should be released; otherwise stuck buttons/last state persist on the host pad. Host honors InputEvent::GamepadDisconnect{slot} (host.rs:1201).
missingPieces.approach: Add a send_gamepad_disconnect(app, slot) command in mobile/src/client.rs sending InputEvent::GamepadDisconnect{slot} via send_input_via (mirror send_button), register in lib.rs, and invoke it from the End handler and on overlay-off in ui/index.html. Reuse the existing InputSenders slot map.

missingPieces.feature: Layout customization / haptics polish (nice-to-have)
missingPieces.description: Moonlight-class touch pads let users reposition/resize controls and give haptic feedback on press. Not required for a usable v1 but expected for comfort.
missingPieces.approach: Persist per-control positions/scale in localStorage (LS helper already in index.html:273) with a drag-to-edit mode; trigger navigator.vibrate() on button down for haptics; optional opacity slider. Pure front-end in ui/index.html — no Rust changes.

findings.notes: The hard part (wire protocol + host replay) is DONE and unchanged: InputEvent::GamepadSlot{slot,kind,target,state} (wire.rs:25-74) is fully handled by the host (host.rs:1160-1206) with a real Linux uinput VirtualGamepad. So this feature is almost entirely a MOBILE-CLIENT job: one small Rust command (send_gamepad, ~S) + a substantial new touch-first overlay UI (~L). There is NO desktop UI to port — desktop uses physical controllers (controllers.rs) only, so the on-screen pad must be designed from scratch (Moonlight is the reference per pulsar-mobile-plan). Two correctness gotchas to respect: (1) GamepadState stick Y is UP-positive in pulsar-core (controllers.rs:451 negates SDL's down-positive Y; ds4_report_fields re-inverts) — the mobile overlay must send UP-positive Y so it matches. (2) Today the mobile pointer-touch handler (index.html:379-390) sends absolute PointerMotion on every touch, which would conflict with a gamepad overlay; it must be gated off while the pad is active in game mode. Per product rules, do NOT add file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor to the game overlay. Also note the codebase already exposes button:: bit constants, axis_to_i16, trigger_to_u8 (input/types.rs) that the JS overlay should mirror exactly so bits line up with xinput_buttons/ds4_report_fields.

findings.area: Touch→mouse mapping, tap/drag/scroll, on-screen keyboard + special keys (remote desktop on a phone)
desktopCapabilities: Absolute VNC-style pointer positioning normalized 0..1 over the video canvas with a rAF pump that sends the latest position at refresh rate (src/lib/screens/Session/input.svelte.ts:55-65,86-90,129-133)
Left/right/middle mouse buttons with browser->host button remap b==2?1:b==1?2:0 (input.svelte.ts:127,141-160); right-click context menu works
Smooth wheel scroll (dx,dy) throttled to ~30ms, forwarded as InputEvent::Scroll (input.svelte.ts:161-168)

Full physical keyboard via KeyboardEvent.code -> evdev keycode table (src/lib/keymap.ts) sent as InputEvent::Key{code,down}, with held-key tracking + release-on-blur so keys never stick (input.svelte.ts:169-249,98-109)
Layout-independent WYSIWYG text entry: printable keys in remote mode resolved through the client's own layout to a Unicode codepoint and sent as InputEvent::Char (input.svelte.ts:179-226)
Special/OS-reserved keys: Win/Meta, Alt+Tab, Ctrl+Esc, F-keys, arrows, Enter, modifiers all mapped; Windows kbdCaptureStart hooks reserved keys (input.svelte.ts:114-116, keymap.ts EVDEV table)
Game mode forces raw scancode/VK path (no Char) so games see WASD/space via RawInput (input.svelte.ts:188-202)
Leave/disengage combo Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+F12 (input.svelte.ts:171-177); click-to-engage capture model
Wire protocol also defines PointerRelative{dx,dy} for relative/trackpad-style movement (crates/pulsar-core/src/service/wire.rs:59-61)

missingPieces.feature: Right-click and middle-click on touch (long-press = right-click, two-finger tap = middle)
missingPieces.description: Mobile only ever sends button 0 (left). index.html:386 hardcodes button:0; the Rust send_button accepts a button arg but JS never passes 1 or 2. A phone needs gestures to reach right/middle click, which remote desktop relies on heavily (context menus). The wire variant PointerButton{button} and host injection already support all three buttons.
missingPieces.approach: In ui/index.html replace the flat touchstart/move/end handlers (lines 368-390) with a gesture recognizer: track touch start time + finger count; long-press (>~450ms with no movement) emits send_button(button:1) down+up at the touch point; two-finger tap emits send_button(button:2). Pass the resolved button through the existing core.invoke('send_button',{slot,button,down}) call. No Rust change needed - client.rs send_button already forwards InputEvent::PointerButton{button,down} (client.rs:46-53).

missingPieces.feature: Two-finger scroll (vertical + horizontal wheel)
missingPieces.description: There is no scroll at all on mobile. No send_scroll command exists in the Rust handler (lib.rs:30-37) and the JS never sends a wheel. Remote desktop is unusable without scrolling documents/pages. Desktop forwards InputEvent::Scroll{dx,dy} (input.svelte.ts:161-168); the wire variant exists (wire.rs:65) but the mobile crate never sends it.
missingPieces.approach: Add a send_scroll command in mobile/src/client.rs mirroring send_button (look up the slot's SessionSender, send_input_via with InputEvent::Scroll{dx,dy}); register it in lib.rs invoke_handler. In ui/index.html, in the gesture recognizer detect a two-finger drag (both touches moving same direction), accumulate delta between touchmove events, throttle (~30ms like desktop) and core.invoke('send_scroll',{slot,dx,dy}). Optionally add inertial/momentum decay for natural feel. Scale deltas to wheel units (host uinput/win/mac scroll handlers already convert pixels).

missingPieces.feature: On-screen keyboard for typing into the remote desktop
missingPieces.description: Mobile has NO keyboard input whatsoever - no send_key/send_char command in the Rust handler (lib.rs:30-37) and no key handling in the UI. Typing on the remote machine is impossible. Desktop sends InputEvent::Char for text and InputEvent::Key for special keys; both wire variants (wire.rs:67,73) exist but are unwired on mobile. This is the single biggest gap for remote-desktop-on-a-phone.
missingPieces.approach: Rust: add send_char(slot, ch: char) -> InputEvent::Char and send_key(slot, code: u32, down: bool) -> InputEvent::Key in client.rs (same SessionSender lookup pattern as send_button), register both in lib.rs. UI: add a keyboard toggle button to the in-session .bar (index.html:257-267) that focuses a hidden/offscreen contenteditable or <input> to summon the native soft keyboard; capture its 'input'/beforeinput events -> for each printable char core.invoke('send_char',{slot,ch}); capture keydown for Backspace/Enter/Tab/arrows and map via an evdev table ported from src/lib/keymap.ts to core.invoke('send_key',{slot,code,down}). Reuse the EVDEV map verbatim from keymap.ts.

missingPieces.feature: Special-key bar (Esc, Tab, Ctrl, Alt, Win/Meta, arrows, F-keys, Ctrl+Alt+Del)
missingPieces.description: Soft keyboards on phones do not expose Esc/Tab/Ctrl/Alt/Win/F-keys/arrows, and cannot hold a modifier while pressing another key (needed for Ctrl+C, Alt+Tab, Ctrl+Alt+Del). Remote desktop requires these. Desktop gets them from a physical keyboard via the evdev table + held-key tracking (input.svelte.ts:234-248); none of this exists on mobile.
missingPieces.approach: Build a touch-first special-keys toolbar (a scrollable pill row above the soft keyboard, shown when keyboard mode is on). Modifier chips (Ctrl/Alt/Shift/Win) are sticky/latching: tapping holds the key down (send_key down) and the next non-modifier key releases all latched modifiers after (matching desktop held-key release semantics in input.svelte.ts:98-109). Dedicated buttons for Esc(1)/Tab(15)/arrows(103/105/106/108)/F1-F12 and a one-tap Ctrl+Alt+Del macro. Reuse the send_key command from the keyboard piece; codes from the ported EVDEV table.

missingPieces.feature: Trackpad/relative-cursor mode (precision pointing) + visible cursor
missingPieces.description: Mobile uses only absolute 1:1 finger-as-cursor mapping (index.html:370-378). On a small phone screen this is imprecise (finger occludes target, no fine control) and there is no visible remote cursor indicator. The wire protocol already has InputEvent::PointerRelative{dx,dy} (wire.rs:61) used by the desktop native renderer, but the mobile crate only sends PointerMotion.
missingPieces.approach: Add send_pointer_rel(slot,dx,dy) -> InputEvent::PointerRelative in client.rs + register in lib.rs. Add a mode toggle (absolute 'direct touch' vs 'trackpad') in the in-session bar. In trackpad mode, treat single-finger drag as relative cursor movement with an acceleration curve and tap-to-click / tap-and-a-half-to-drag, and draw a small on-screen cursor dot in the webview so the user sees where the host pointer is (host already moves its real cursor; the dot is a client-side affordance synced from accumulated deltas). Absolute mode keeps the current path.

missingPieces.feature: Tap vs drag disambiguation + pointer-move throttling/coalescing
missingPieces.description: Current handlers send a pointer + left-down on every touchstart and never debounce moves: each touchmove fires send_pointer synchronously (index.html:388-390), flooding the session at touch sample rate (>120Hz) with one IPC round-trip each, unlike desktop which rAF-coalesces to refresh rate (input.svelte.ts:55-65). Also a quick tap and the start of a drag are treated identically, so there is no clean click vs drag-select distinction.
missingPieces.approach: In ui/index.html coalesce touchmove via requestAnimationFrame (store latest x,y, flush once per frame) mirroring the desktop pump. Add a movement threshold so a touch that never crosses ~8px is a tap (down+up at one point) while exceeding it becomes a drag (down, stream moves, up). This also prevents accidental drag-selection on a quick tap.

missingPieces.feature: Double-tap = double-click
missingPieces.description: No double-click gesture exists; opening files/folders on the remote desktop needs it. Desktop gets it natively from a physical mouse; mobile must synthesize it.
missingPieces.approach: In the gesture recognizer in ui/index.html detect two taps within ~300ms at the same point and emit two rapid send_button(button:0) down/up pairs at that location. No Rust change (send_button already supports it).

findings.notes: Mobile is PARTIAL: it can move the remote cursor (absolute 1:1) and left-click/left-drag, but nothing else. The wire protocol in crates/pulsar-core/src/service/wire.rs:25-74 already defines every event the desktop uses (PointerMotion, PointerRelative, PointerButton{button}, Scroll, Key, Char), and the host-side injection (uinput/Windows/macOS) already handles them - so almost all the gap is in the thin mobile client: only send_pointer + send_button(button hardcoded to 0) are wired (mobile/src/client.rs:36-53, mobile/src/lib.rs:30-37, mobile/ui/index.html:368-390). The biggest missing pieces are an on-screen keyboard, a special-key/modifier bar, and two-finger scroll - all blocking for usable remote desktop on a phone. Right-click, double-click and trackpad mode follow. The clean reference to port is desktop's src/lib/screens/Session/input.svelte.ts (pointer/wheel/key/char engine, held-key release, button remap) and src/lib/keymap.ts (the EVDEV code table - reuse verbatim). NOTE these are all REMOTE-DESKTOP gestures; per desktop-app/CLAUDE.md the slim game-mode overlay excludes keyboard-typing/scroll niceties - game mode wants raw controller + keyboard scancodes, so the keyboard/special-key UI should be a remote-mode affordance (the mobile UI already tracks body[data-mode]).

findings.area: Video rendering/decode pipeline on mobile (native surface vs WebCodecs), HDR, scaling, aspect
desktopCapabilities: Native HW-decode renderer on EVERY platform (WebCodecs video path was REMOVED as too slow) — Linux rkmpp/mpv, Windows Media Foundation+D3D11, macOS mpv/VideoToolbox (desktop-app/src/lib/screens/Session/media.svelte.ts:11-13; src-tauri/src/native_view.rs; render.rs)
Live in-session codec switch h264/h265/av1 with renderer respawn + SDP rewrite (src-tauri/src/session_cmds.rs:86 set_play_codec)
Live in-session resolution change (src-tauri/src/session_cmds.rs:16 set_play_resolution)
Live in-session encoder switch nvenc/amf/qsv/vaapi/videotoolbox/software (session_cmds.rs:40 set_play_encoder)
HDR toggle + resolution list + codec preference persisted in Settings, fed into StreamReq.hdr / decode_codecs (src/lib/screens/Settings/DisplayTab.svelte:169,128,91)
StreamReq carries hdr, yuv444, decode_codecs, bitrate, fps, quality — full encode-profile control

Rich client-side video stats: fps, incoming Mbps, decode latency, RTT/half-RTT latency, jitter, loss, decoder codec + HW/SW badge, host encode summary, fps sparkline (media.svelte.ts via play-vstats/play-rtt/play-stats/play-decoder events)
Stall/freeze detection that surfaces an error when frames stop (media.svelte.ts:163-195)
Switch-aware stall suppression so codec/res switches don't false-trip the stall detector
Native renderer reports the decoder it actually opened + HW/SW (play-decoder event)
Aspect/letterbox + multi-monitor selection handled by the desktop renderer

missingPieces.feature: Honor the user's codec preference (H.265/H.264) on connect
missingPieces.description: The mobile UI has a working H.265/H.264 codec segment that is saved to localStorage (ui/index.html:217-220,306) but is NEVER passed to connect_host. connect_host (mobile/src/client.rs:135-139) blindly takes caps.codecs.first() from the host and ignores any client preference, so the toggle is dead. Desktop sends decode_codecs in StreamReq. H.265 is the gaming default and gives far better quality-per-bit on a phone link.
missingPieces.approach: Add a codec: String param to connect_host (default "auto"). When not "auto", set StreamReq.decode_codecs = vec![codec] and prefer it when choosing from caps.codecs (intersect pref with caps, fall back to caps.first). Use the chosen codec for mime_for/codec_enum and the depacketizer. In ui/index.html doConnect (line 319) pass codec: LS.get('codec','auto'). The Kotlin start_stream already takes the mime, so no plugin change needed.

missingPieces.feature: Live performance HUD (fps / bitrate / latency / decode-time) on mobile
missingPieces.description: Desktop shows a rich per-session HUD (fps, Mbps, decode ms, half-RTT latency, jitter, loss) driven by play-vstats/play-rtt events (media.svelte.ts). Mobile shows only a static 'H265 · oturum' label in bar-meta (ui/index.html:322) and emits no stats at all — the read loop only logs frame counters to logcat (client.rs:218). For a Moonlight-class client a touch-first perf overlay is expected, especially in game mode.
missingPieces.approach: In client.rs read loop, accumulate frames/aus/bytes + sample wall-clock to compute fps and incoming Mbps over a ~1s window, and emit a Tauri event (e.g. app.emit('play-vstats', {slot,fps,mbps})). Add a lightweight RTT from the existing keepalive cadence if pulsar-core exposes a pong, else show fps/mbps only. Decode latency would need MediaCodec to report buffer timing back from Kotlin (PulsarVideoPlugin.feedAndDrain) via an event — optional. In ui/index.html add a small translucent HUD chip in the in-session bar that listens to the event and updates fps/mbps; touch-first, collapsible.

missingPieces.feature: Stall / stream-stopped detection + user feedback
missingPieces.description: Desktop detects when frames stop (host revoked share, renderer died) and shows an error (media.svelte.ts:182-191). Mobile has NO stall detection: if the host stops sending video the SurfaceView just freezes on the last frame with no UI feedback; the only signal is the 6s session keepalive teardown. The read loop already counts frames so the data is there.
missingPieces.approach: Reuse the vstats event from the HUD piece: if no TAG_VIDEO frames arrive for >3s while in-session, emit a 'play-stalled' event from client.rs and show a 'Bağlantı durdu / görüntü dondu' toast/overlay in ui/index.html, with the existing End button. No new plugin code.

missingPieces.feature: AV1 decode path
missingPieces.description: Desktop supports av1 in codec switching/StreamReq (session_cmds.rs:82, mime_for maps av1->video/av01). Mobile mime_for maps av1 to video/av01 (client.rs:67) BUT codec_enum falls through to H264 and the depacketizer has no AV1 OBU path (rtp.rs comment line 84-86: 'av1 would need its own OBU path'). So requesting AV1 would feed OBU RTP through an H.264 NAL reassembler and produce garbage. Newer phones have HW AV1 decoders and AV1 is excellent at low bitrate.
missingPieces.approach: Implement an AV1 RTP depacketizer (RFC 9to AV1 aggregation header + OBU fragment reassembly) as a new Codec::Av1 arm in rtp.rs, emitting length-delimited OBUs (not Annex-B). Map codec_enum av1->Av1. In Kotlin, configure MediaCodec video/av01 with csd from the sequence-header OBU. Until done, explicitly exclude av1 from the mobile codec preference so it can't be selected. Lower priority than h264/h265 parity.

missingPieces.feature: HDR (HDR10/HLG) decode + tonemap on mobile
missingPieces.description: Desktop has an HDR toggle that flows into StreamReq.hdr (DisplayTab.svelte:169; mobile sets hdr:false hard-coded at client.rs:163). Mobile never requests HDR and the MediaCodec configure (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:524-533) sets no HDR static metadata / color-transfer, and SurfaceView is created OPAQUE with no HDR/wide-gamut path. Many flagship phones have HDR panels; product advertises HDR up to 120fps.
missingPieces.approach: Plumb an hdr flag from UI -> connect_host -> StreamReq.hdr (so the host encodes HEVC Main10/PQ). In Kotlin, for video/hevc with a 10-bit profile pass KEY_COLOR_TRANSFER/KEY_COLOR_STANDARD/KEY_COLOR_RANGE on the MediaFormat and forward HDR static info (csd or KEY_HDR_STATIC_INFO) parsed from SEI; ensure the Activity window + SurfaceView opt into HDR (setColorMode / Display.HdrCapabilities check). Gate on Android O+/device HDR support, fall back to SDR tonemap. Heavy; do after core h264/h265 parity.

missingPieces.feature: Aspect / fit mode + orientation handling (fit vs fill, force-landscape)
missingPieces.description: The Kotlin renderer letterboxes by computing CENTER fit from the decoded crop rect (applyAspect, PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:555-569) — there is NO user choice of fit/fill/stretch and no orientation lock. A 16:9 desktop stream on a portrait phone is letterboxed into a tiny strip with no way to rotate/fill. Moonlight offers force-landscape + a fill/stretch option; this is core to a usable phone client. Desktop doesn't need this (window resize), so it's mobile-specific UX.
missingPieces.approach: Add a fit-mode arg (fit|fill|stretch) to the plugin (new command setAspect(slot,mode) or extend startStream) and branch in applyAspect: fit=min-scale (current), fill=max-scale + crop, stretch=MATCH_PARENT. Add an orientation control: call activity.setRequestedOrientation(SENSOR_LANDSCAPE) when entering a landscape stream, restore on detach. Surface a small toggle in the in-session bar (ui/index.html near btn-split) — touch-first segmented control. Persist last choice in LS.

missingPieces.feature: Client-requested resolution / bitrate / fps (encode profile) on mobile
missingPieces.description: connect_host hard-codes width/height/fps/bitrate_kbps all to 0 and quality to default (client.rs:144-166), so the host always streams its native resolution at its default profile — there is no mobile equivalent of desktop's resolution list, bitrate (Mbit) and fps controls (DisplayTab.svelte:128; session_cmds set_play_resolution/encoder). On a cellular link a 4K-native host stream is unwatchable with no way to cap it.
missingPieces.approach: Pass desired width/height/fps/bitrate_kbps/quality from the UI into connect_host and into StreamReq. Add a touch-first quality sheet (Auto / Data-saver / Balanced / Performance presets mapping to res+bitrate+fps) in ui/index.html shown before/inside a session. For live changes, optionally wire a mobile set_play_resolution-style command that re-sends a StreamReq over the held session (pulsar-core supports re-request_stream / Restream on the host). Start with connect-time selection; live switching is a follow-up.

missingPieces.feature: Decoder error recovery / keyframe resync feedback
missingPieces.description: On Android, MediaCodec throwing (e.g. unsupported SPS, lost keyframe corruption) is caught and only logged (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:505) — the user sees a frozen/garbage frame with no recovery and no request for a fresh IDR. The depacketizer drops corrupt frames silently. Desktop's WebCodecs path re-derived codec/IDR and the native path respawns; mobile has neither resync nor feedback.
missingPieces.approach: Detect decode failure / prolonged garbage (codec exception or N seconds without a successful output buffer) in the Pane, emit an event, and from client.rs request a keyframe if pulsar-core's service exposes a request-IDR control (check service:: for a force-keyframe/restream nudge; if absent, re-arm the decoder by releasing+reconfiguring on the next SPS/PPS). Surface a brief 'yeniden eşitleniyor' overlay in the UI.

findings.notes: Architecture note: mobile correctly uses NATIVE MediaCodec decode onto a SurfaceView under a transparent webview (Path A) — NOT WebCodecs. This matches the desktop's current direction (desktop ALSO removed its WebCodecs video path and now renders natively everywhere; media.svelte.ts:11-13). So the mobile decode mechanism itself is sound and on-strategy; the gaps are in CONTROL and FEEDBACK around it, not the core decode. H.264 + H.265 single/STAP-A/FU-A/AP/FU depacketization is implemented and unit-tested (rtp.rs:208-303). Most impactful and cheapest fix: the codec preference toggle (H.265/H.264) is already in the UI and saved to localStorage but is silently dropped — connect_host never receives it and the host's caps.codecs.first() wins (client.rs:135-139, ui/index.html:306,319). Biggest mobile-specific UX gaps (need new touch-first design): aspect fit/fill + orientation lock, a quality/bitrate/resolution preset sheet (host always streams native res today, client.rs:144-166), and a perf HUD + stall feedback (mobile emits zero stats and freezes silently on stream stop). AV1 and HDR are real but lower-priority Ls — AV1 is actively unsafe today because mime_for maps av1->video/av01 while the depacketizer has no OBU path and would feed OBUs through the H.264 NAL reassembler (rtp.rs:84-86, client.rs:67,81-87). All findings stay within game-mode-allowed scope (codec/bitrate/fps/resolution/quality/encoder/HDR) — no file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor proposed.

findings.area: Audio playback on mobile (Opus decode + output, mute, volume)
desktopCapabilities: Host->client Opus/RTP audio stream parallel to video (second UDP port or media-over-session), described in desktop-app/CLAUDE.md 'Audio streaming'
Client decode + playback: WebCodecs AudioDecoder -> WebAudio (desktop-app/src/lib/opus-audio.ts:41) with jitter buffer (~60ms lead, clamped both sides for under/overrun at opus-audio.ts:91)
Surround-correct playback: honours the decoder's reported channel count (2/6/8), never folds to stereo (opus-audio.ts:70)
Native Linux fallback: ffmpeg -> PulseAudio player with capped output buffer (~80ms) to bound audio lag (audio_io.rs / spawn_native_audio per CLAUDE.md)

Live session-menu audio toggles applied mid-session: transmit_audio (host->client on/off) and mute_host_audio (silence host speakers) via set_play_audio (api.commands.ts:238 -> session_cmds.rs:1279 -> Restream::Audio)
Persisted Settings toggles: transmit_audio + mute_host_audio (DisplayTab.svelte:174/178) and an audio source picker (list_audio_sources, api.commands.ts:123)
Graceful degrade to silent video if WebCodecs audio is unavailable (opus-audio.ts:47-50, i18n key audio.noWebcodecs)

missingPieces.feature: Client-side mute toggle (local audio on/off)
missingPieces.description: Mobile can decode and play host audio (full path already works: client.rs:199 -> feed_audio -> PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:159 -> MediaCodec(audio/opus) -> AudioTrack) but there is NO way for the user to mute it. The in-session control bar (ui/index.html:257-267) has only Split and End buttons; the Kotlin AudioTrack is never muted/paused. This is the single most important gap for a usable mobile client (muting the phone speaker in public).
missingPieces.approach: Local client-side mute is the right model on mobile (instant, no round-trip, like Moonlight). Add a speaker/mute icon-button to the in-session bar in desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html (next to btn-split, reuse .icon-btn + an inline SVG; toggle a muted CSS class + persist via the existing LS helper). Wire a click handler that invokes a new plugin command 'setAudioMuted'. In desktop-app/crates/tauri-plugin-pulsar-video/src/mobile.rs add pub fn set_audio_muted(&self, muted: bool) (run_mobile_plugin 'setAudioMuted'); add the desktop.rs no-op twin; add a permission toml under permissions/autogenerated/commands/. In android/src/main/java/PulsarVideoPlugin.kt add @Command fun setAudioMuted that, under audioLock, calls audioTrack?.setVolume(if muted 0f else gain) (or pause()/play()). No Rust client.rs change needed since playback is local.

missingPieces.feature: Client-side volume control
missingPieces.description: No volume control on mobile. AudioTrack plays at fixed gain (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:391-404, no setVolume call). Desktop also has no client-gain slider, but on mobile the OS hardware volume rocker typically routes to USAGE_MEDIA streams, so this is partially covered for free. An in-app fine-grained slider is nice-to-have, not critical.
missingPieces.approach: First verify the hardware volume keys already control the AudioTrack (it is built with USAGE_MEDIA at PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:392-394, so they should). If an in-app slider is still wanted, extend the same setAudioMuted command into setAudioVolume(gain: f32) and call audioTrack?.setVolume(gain). Add a small range/stepper control to the in-session bar overflow in ui/index.html and persist via LS. Most likely lowest-effort win is to rely on OS volume and skip the in-app slider.

missingPieces.feature: Jitter buffer / underrun-overrun handling on the audio path
missingPieces.description: Mobile feeds each Opus packet straight into MediaCodec the instant it arrives (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:409-429 feedAudioPacketLocked, called from client.rs:204 per-packet) with no jitter buffer. AudioTrack(MODE_STREAM) absorbs some jitter via its ~16KB buffer (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:401) but there is none of the desktop's explicit ~60ms-lead clamp-on-both-sides logic (opus-audio.ts:91), so network jitter can cause audible gaps or growing lag behind the video.
missingPieces.approach: No new UI. Two viable approaches: (a) tune the AudioTrack buffer size at PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:401 (e.g. ~80-120ms target like the desktop native player's -buffer_duration cap) and rely on MODE_STREAM backpressure; (b) add a small bounded queue in feedAudio that drops the oldest packet when it grows past a target (mirrors the leaky-queue / playHead-clamp philosophy used elsewhere). Measure A/V sync live (Pi/PC->phone) before adding complexity; start with buffer-size tuning (smaller change) then add a leaky queue only if drift is observed.

missingPieces.feature: Surround (5.1/7.1) audio + negotiated channel layout
missingPieces.description: Mobile hardcodes stereo end-to-end: client.rs:165 sends audio_layout: ChannelLayout::Stereo, and the Kotlin decoder/AudioTrack are fixed to 48000/2 (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:382 createAudioFormat 'audio/opus',48000,2 and CHANNEL_OUT_STEREO at :399). Desktop honours the stream's true channel count (opus-audio.ts:70). On a phone (speaker/headphones) stereo is almost always correct, so this is low priority for the mobile thin client.
missingPieces.approach: Only worth doing if mobile is ever used with multichannel output. Would require reading the decoded MediaCodec output format's channel count after INFO_OUTPUT_FORMAT_CHANGED and rebuilding AudioTrack with the matching channel mask, plus negotiating a non-stereo audio_layout in client.rs. Recommend leaving as stereo for the phone client (matches typical hardware) and not investing here now.

missingPieces.feature: Surface a 'no audio' / audio-unavailable state to the user
missingPieces.description: If MediaCodec(audio/opus) configuration fails it is swallowed (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:168 catch + Log.e only) and feed_audio's ok flag is dropped on the Rust side (client.rs:204 uses let _ = ...). The user gets silent video with no indication, unlike desktop which shows i18n audio.noWebcodecs. Minor UX gap.
missingPieces.approach: Optional polish. Have feedAudio emit a one-time plugin event (trigger(...)) on configure failure, or expose audioConfigured via the AttachResponse already returned by feed_audio, and have ui/index.html show a small muted-audio indicator in the bar. Low value; only do after the mute toggle (priority 1) ships.

findings.notes: Audio is the most complete mobile media path so far: full Opus decode->output is wired end-to-end (client.rs:199-205 -> mobile.rs feed_audio:82 -> Kotlin feedAudio PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:159 -> MediaCodec(audio/opus):386 -> AudioTrack:391, 48kHz stereo, USAGE_MEDIA). Audio is requested only on the primary pane: client.rs:151-152 set audio_port and transmit_audio only when slot==0; mute_host is hardcoded false (client.rs:154). So 'absent' is NOT the status here -- the decode+output is PRESENT; what is missing is user-facing control. The single critical gap is a client-side mute toggle in the in-session bar (ui/index.html:257-267 currently only Split+End). Mobile rightly uses LOCAL mute (instant, no round-trip), not the desktop's host-side set_play_audio (api.commands.ts:238 / session_cmds.rs:1279) -- do NOT port the host-mute model to the phone client. Volume is likely already handled by the Android hardware volume rocker (AudioTrack is USAGE_MEDIA), so an in-app slider is optional. Per product rules audio IS shown in game mode (transmit forced on), so audio controls belong in both remote and game overlays -- this is not a remote-desktop-only feature like file/clipboard/mic. Jitter buffering (priority 3) is the next real-quality gap vs desktop's explicit playHead clamp (opus-audio.ts:91); surround (priority 5) is effectively N/A for a phone.

findings.area: Settings screen: relay endpoint, network mode, language, persisted config
desktopCapabilities: Full persistent Config struct in crates/pulsar-core/src/config.rs: relay, network_mode (auto/p2p-only/relay-only), device_name, language (Tr/En), node_port, unattended_access, connect_password, audio toggles, avatar_mode, native_player, ui_hardware_accel, request_admin

Atomic JSON persistence to the app config dir (Config::save writes temp+rename; Config::load tolerates missing/garbage by falling back to defaults; serde defaults keep old configs loading) — config.rs:141-180
Tauri bridge get_config / set_config exposed to UI via api.getConfig()/api.setConfig() — Settings.svelte:56-72
Tabbed Settings screen (Display/Network/Security/General) with controller-navigable rail — Settings.svelte:43-159
Network tab: relay host:port input with validation (relay_is_valid), network-mode segmented control, fixed node_port with live random-port placeholder — NetworkTab.svelte:37-96
Auto re-register only when relay/network_mode/node_port/device_name change (reconnectKey) — Settings.svelte:52,84-88
Saved-toast feedback with debounce + save-error surfacing — Settings.svelte:28-41,160-162
Language choice persisted in Config.language and applied via i18n (Tr default + En) — config.rs:30-36
Device name with OS-hostname default + placeholder mirroring, avatar mode (user/wallpaper/anonymous) — GeneralTab.svelte:33-81
Stable device ID: Identity::load_or_create persists X25519 identity per user so the relay-issued ID survives restarts (desktop core)

missingPieces.feature: Persist settings via the Rust core Config instead of localStorage
missingPieces.description: Mobile stores relay endpoint, network mode, device name and codec pref ONLY in browser localStorage (index.html:273 LS helper, 299-306) and passes them as per-call params to connect_host/go_online (client.rs:94-110, host.rs:38-46). There are no get_config/set_config commands on mobile. localStorage can be cleared by the OS/webview and is not the source of truth the core expects; desktop persists an atomic JSON Config in the app config dir. Mobile should load/save the same Config so settings survive, are validated, and feed the node directly.
missingPieces.approach: Add get_config/set_config Tauri commands to the mobile crate that load/save pulsar_core::Config to the Tauri app_config_dir (reuse Config::load/Config::save from config.rs). Wire them in lib.rs invoke_handler. In index.html replace the LS-backed relay/netmode/name/codec reads/writes (lines 299-306) with core.invoke('get_config')/('set_config') and drop the per-call relay/netmode/name args from connect_host/go_online (or have those commands read the persisted Config). Add a relay host:port validity check (Config::relay_is_valid) and surface an inline error like the desktop saveErr.

missingPieces.feature: Persistent device identity (stable device ID) on mobile
missingPieces.description: Mobile calls Identity::generate() on every connect_host (client.rs:111) and go_online (host.rs:46), so the relay-issued 9-digit ID changes on every launch. Desktop uses Identity::load_or_create persisted per-user so the ID is stable across restarts (a core product guarantee). This belongs to the settings/persisted-config area because identity persistence is part of persisted device state and directly affects the 'Cihazım' ID shown.
missingPieces.approach: Resolve the Tauri app_config_dir on mobile and call Identity::load_or_create(<dir>/identity.key) (same API the desktop uses) instead of Identity::generate(), in both client.rs:106-112 and host.rs:46. Pass the loaded identity into Node::bind_with_identity. No UI change needed; the 'Cihazım' ID will then be stable.

missingPieces.feature: Language selection (Tr/En) on mobile
missingPieces.description: Desktop persists Config.language (Tr default, En) and applies it via i18n. Mobile has NO language control: index.html is hardcoded lang="tr" (line 2) with all copy inline in Turkish and no i18n layer, so English users cannot switch. The Hakkında card (index.html:225-228) is the natural place for a language segmented control.
missingPieces.approach: Add a small JS i18n table (tr/en) for the mobile strings in index.html and a language segmented control in the Ayarlar tab (mirror the netmode .seg pattern at index.html:200-206). Persist the choice into Config.language via set_config (depends on the config-persistence piece) and re-render strings on change; set the <html lang> attribute dynamically. Design a compact touch-first segmented Tr/En toggle in the Hakkında/About card.

missingPieces.feature: Honor codec preference from settings
missingPieces.description: Mobile shows a Codec tercihi segmented control (auto/h265/h264) in Ayarlar (index.html:216-222) and saves it to localStorage (line 306), but the value is never passed to connect_host — the Rust client hardcodes its codec request (client.rs codec_from / Codec::H264 path) and ignores the user choice. The desktop applies codec/quality prefs per session. So the control is currently a no-op.
missingPieces.approach: Pass the persisted codec pref into connect_host (add a param or read it from the persisted Config) and map it in client.rs where the QualityPref/Codec is chosen (around the codec_from helper) so the host stream request honors auto/h265/h264. Remove the dead localStorage-only handling once config persistence lands.

missingPieces.feature: Node port / fixed-port setting (optional)
missingPieces.description: Desktop exposes Config.node_port (fixed local port for predictable port-forwarding, with a live random-port placeholder — NetworkTab.svelte:68-95). Mobile has no equivalent. Low priority for a phone (rarely port-forwarded), but it is part of the persisted Config and would come along once get_config/set_config exists.
missingPieces.approach: Optionally add a node_port field to the mobile Ayarlar > Ağ card mirroring NetworkTab's number input (placeholder = live random port), persisting via set_config. Otherwise simply leave node_port at its Config default (0 = auto) — no UI needed. Skip unless port-forwarding to a phone host is a real requirement.

findings.notes: Mobile settings exist as UI (index.html:188-229: relay endpoint input, network-mode segmented control, device name, codec pref, About/GPLv3) but are entirely localStorage-backed (index.html:273,299-306) and shuttled as per-call params to connect_host/go_online (client.rs:94-110, host.rs:38-46) — there is NO Rust Config load/save and NO get_config/set_config command on mobile, unlike desktop (config.rs Config::load/save + Tauri bridge, Settings.svelte:56,71). Biggest correctness gap: Identity::generate() per call (client.rs:111, host.rs:46) makes the device ID change every launch, violating the desktop stable-ID guarantee (Identity::load_or_create). Language switching is absent (hardcoded lang=tr, no i18n) and the codec-pref control is a no-op (never reaches the Rust client). Recommended order: (1) core-backed config persistence + (2) persistent identity first, then (3) codec wiring and (4) language toggle. Scope note: avatar_mode/unattended/audio/admin/hw-accel Config fields are host/desktop-oriented and not required for a thin mobile client; excluded here as out of scope for the settings parity needed on mobile. Touch-first redesign only needed for the language toggle (rest reuse existing .seg/.field/.input mobile atoms already in index.html).

findings.area: Host mode on mobile (share THIS device: go_online, show ID/password, screen-share Android)
desktopCapabilities: go_online: bind node, register with relay -> stable 9-digit ID (Identity::load_or_create), LAN discovery beacon, re-runnable with full teardown of prior serve loop/node (host.rs:255)
One-time password auth issued per online session (gen_password) OR skipped when cfg.unattended_access is on (host.rs:435-443); live password is re-readable so new_password takes effect mid-session

Screen capture host: X11 ffmpeg / Windows DXGI+NVENC / macOS avfoundation / Wayland XDG portal+GStreamer; HW encoders (NVENC/VAAPI/QSV/VideoToolbox); multi-codec caps probe (h264/h265/av1) pre-warmed off hot path

Per-session serve loop accepts MULTIPLE concurrent incoming peers, tracks them in incoming/host_out/active maps, surfaces each in a Connections window with peer name + transport, removes row on disconnect
Incoming peer name decoration (PeerName), avatar payload, mode-aware focus policy (remote vs game) for the connections window
Host audio capture parallel Opus/RTP stream; mute_host toggle; reverse-direction; file/clipboard/chat side channels (remote-desktop mode only)
Input injection from connected client: Wayland RemoteDesktop portal / uinput / ViGEm virtual gamepad; rumble forward back to client
Adaptive re-stream on client quality/resolution change; display-mode watcher; full SessionCleanupGuard RAII teardown of encoders/portal/maps on disconnect or abort
Persisted config drives relay endpoint, network_mode, node_port (pinned port for firewall rules), device_name, unattended_access

missingPieces.feature: Stable host device ID (identity persistence)
missingPieces.description: Mobile go_online calls Identity::generate() on every invocation (host.rs:46), so the relay hands back a DIFFERENT 9-digit ID every time the phone goes online. The whole point of a host ID is that a remote operator can save it and reconnect later; today it is useless across restarts. Desktop persists identity via Identity::load_or_create(identity_path) so the relay maps pubkey->id and the ID is stable per device (CLAUDE.md 'Stable device ID').
missingPieces.approach: In go_online, replace Identity::generate() with a load-or-create against the app data dir. Use Tauri's app.path().app_data_dir() to derive an identity.key path (Android: app-private storage, no extra permission) and call pulsar_core::crypto::Identity::load_or_create(path) the same way desktop does. Store the resolved Identity once and reuse it for both host (go_online) and client (connect_host) so the phone has ONE stable identity. UI: show the ID immediately on app open by persisting it / re-querying, instead of only after pressing Cevrimici Ol.

missingPieces.feature: Go offline / stop hosting (lifecycle control)
missingPieces.description: There is no way to STOP being a host. go_online spawns a detached accept loop (host.rs:53-169) that lives forever with no handle stored, no go_offline command, and the UI has no 'Cevrimdisi Ol' button. stop_host exists on the plugin but is only called when a streaming session ends, never to take the device offline. A user who taps Cevrimici Ol can never undo it short of killing the app; the phone keeps heartbeating to the relay and accepting connections indefinitely.
missingPieces.approach: Store the accept-loop JoinHandle + the Node in shared Tauri state (a HostState struct managed alongside InputSenders). Add a go_offline command that aborts the accept task, drops the Node (releasing the relay heartbeat + UDP socket), and calls pulsar_video().stop_host() to tear down any MediaProjection. Make go_online re-runnable by tearing down a prior loop first (mirror desktop host.rs:292-325). UI: toggle the primary button between 'Cevrimici Ol' and 'Cevrimdisi Ol', reset the idbig/status/pw chips to the offline placeholder state on stop.

missingPieces.feature: Incoming-connection awareness + status (who is connected / viewing)
missingPieces.description: When a peer connects, the mobile UI shows nothing beyond a static 'Bekleniyor' line (index.html:355). There is no event back to the webview when a session is accepted, so the user never sees that someone is now viewing/controlling their screen, the peer's name, or how many are connected. Desktop emits SessionEvent and renders a live Connections window. On mobile the only signal is the OS foreground-service notification 'Ekran paylasiliyor'.
missingPieces.approach: Emit Tauri events from the serve closure on accept/disconnect (e.g. app.emit('host-peer-connected'/'host-peer-disconnected', { name, transport })). Capture the peer name from the session (the desktop PeerName decoration) and pass it through serve_with's on_connect callback (currently a no-op |_id| {} at host.rs:158). In the webview, subscribe via window.__TAURI__.event.listen and update host-status to a live 'X bagli - ekranini goruyor' state with a connected-peer chip. Design a compact touch-first connected-peers list for the Cihazim tab.

missingPieces.feature: Connection approval prompt (security)
missingPieces.description: The mobile host auto-accepts any peer that supplies the correct one-time password (host.rs:69-74) with no user-facing approve/reject step. There is no way to see who is asking and deny them, and no kick-an-active-peer control. For a screen-share-this-phone product this is a meaningful trust gap, especially combined with the always-on a11y remote control which lets the peer tap anything on the device.
missingPieces.approach: Before calling accept() in the auth path, emit a 'host-approval-request' event with the peer name to the webview, await a oneshot resolved by a new approve_connection(allow: bool) Tauri command driven by a touch-first modal sheet (Onayla / Reddet) in index.html. Add a per-peer 'Baglantiyi kes' button that resolves the session's stored teardown oneshot (mirror desktop's incoming map of oneshot senders). Optionally a 'her zaman izin ver' remembered-peer list to avoid prompting trusted devices.

missingPieces.feature: Unattended access toggle (no-password mode)
missingPieces.description: Mobile always requires the one-time password (host.rs:64-74). Desktop supports cfg.unattended_access to allow saved/trusted devices to connect with no password (CLAUDE.md). Without it, a mobile host can only ever be reached by reading out a fresh password each time - no set-and-forget remote access to your own phone.
missingPieces.approach: Add an unattended toggle to settings (localStorage now, Rust Config later) and pass it into go_online; when enabled, skip gen_password and the need_password/recv_auth handshake (set authed=true), mirroring desktop's require_auth = !cfg.unattended_access (host.rs:437). Surface a clear warning in the UI since this removes the password gate. Best paired with the remembered-peer allowlist from the approval item.

missingPieces.feature: Remove the TEMP auto-go-online + self-connect diagnostic
missingPieces.description: index.html:414-424 contains a TEMP diagnostic that, 2s after load, automatically calls go_online AND then connect_host back to itself, logging PULSARHOST/PULSARCONN. This auto-hosts the phone (requesting MediaProjection) on every launch without the user pressing Cevrimici Ol, and triggers the screen-capture permission dialog unexpectedly. It is test scaffolding that must not ship.
missingPieces.approach: Delete the setTimeout diagnostic block (index.html:414-424). Hosting must only start on the explicit Cevrimici Ol tap. Keep the legitimate go-online handler at index.html:347-361.

missingPieces.feature: Host codec capability (H.265) + caps probe
missingPieces.description: The mobile host hardcodes h264 only in StreamCaps (host.rs:151-156) with a comment that HEVC MediaCodec support is unprobed and the emulator lacks an HEVC encoder. Desktop advertises multi-codec caps from a real encoder probe. On modern phones with a HW HEVC encoder this leaves bandwidth/quality on the table, and there is no AV1 path.
missingPieces.approach: Add a plugin command that enumerates MediaCodec encoders (MediaCodecList.getCodecInfos() filtered to encoder + video/hevc, video/av01, video/avc) and returns the supported MIME list to Rust. Build StreamCaps.codecs dynamically from that probe (prefer h265>h264 when an HEVC encoder is present), and pass the negotiated codec through to HostEncoder (startHost already accepts h265/hevc -> video/hevc at PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:220). Keep h264 as the universal fallback.

missingPieces.feature: Wire host settings to persisted Rust config (relay/netmode/name)
missingPieces.description: Host config is read from localStorage in the webview and passed as raw args to go_online each call (index.html:350). There is no pulsar-core Config persistence on mobile, so node_port pinning, unattended_access, device_name normalization (desktop falls back to OS display name) and codec preference are not honored server-side. The desktop drives all of this from a persisted Config (CLAUDE.md 'Configurable relay').
missingPieces.approach: Introduce a lightweight Config persisted to app_data_dir on mobile (or reuse pulsar_core::config) and add get_config/set_config commands so the Ayarlar tab persists through Rust rather than only localStorage. go_online and connect_host then read this Config instead of webview-supplied args, giving a single source of truth and enabling node_port/unattended/codec server-side.

findings.notes: Mobile host mode is far more complete than 'a stub': the full control + media pipeline already works. go_online (mobile/src/host.rs:35) binds a Node, registers with the relay, returns a 9-digit ID + one-time password, accepts incoming sessions with a proper auth handshake (recv_auth -> need_password -> accept/reject, host.rs:63-74), advertises FEAT_MOS media-over-session, and on StartStream drives the Android plugin (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt startHost -> MediaProjection screen-capture-intent + a mediaProjection foreground service HostService.kt -> HostEncoder.kt MediaCodec H.264 + Opus -> RTP) forwarded as TAG_VIDEO/TAG_AUDIO frames into the encrypted session. Remote control of the phone works via PulsarA11yService.kt dispatchGesture (tap/swipe), with open_a11y_settings + a11y_enabled wired into the Cihazim tab. The AndroidManifest declares all needed permissions/services (FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PROJECTION, RECORD_AUDIO, the two services + the a11y service). Overlapping reconnects share one projection via ACTIVE_STREAMS refcount (host.rs:26,162). So status is 'partial' mainly because of LIFECYCLE + IDENTITY + TRUST gaps rather than missing media: (1) the host ID is regenerated every call (Identity::generate, host.rs:46) so it is not stable/reconnectable - the single most important fix; (2) you cannot go offline or stop hosting once started (no handle stored, no go_offline command, no UI); (3) no feedback/approval when a peer actually connects (the on_connect callback is a no-op |_id|{} at host.rs:158); and (4) ships a TEMP auto-go-online+self-connect diagnostic (index.html:414-424) that must be removed. Codec is h264-only by deliberate choice (host.rs:151) pending an encoder probe. Per product rules I did NOT propose file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor host features (remote-desktop-only, and the mobile host is the screen being shared - those belong on the connecting side, not in game mode).

findings.area: Device identity, stable ID display, online/offline status, relay registration UI
desktopCapabilities: Persistent per-user X25519 identity via Identity::load_or_create(identity_path(app)) -> SAME 9-digit relay ID every launch (desktop-app/src-tauri/src/host.rs:383, util.rs:44-49)
go_online command binds node, registers with relay, returns grouped device ID; fails -> UI shows 'offline' but keeps LAN discovery + serving alive (host.rs:255, host.rs:1837)

Rich self-identity card (SelfCard.svelte): big device ID + copy-ID button, online/connecting/ready/offline status badge, local ip:port row with copy, one-time password display + copy + refresh(rotate), unattended-access warning, live connected-peers list with per-peer kick
Live online status surfaced in BottomDock + Sidebar with a 'Cevrimici ol' reconnect button bound to onGoOnline (BottomDock.svelte:93-99)
ID-rotation watcher: relay restart reissues a different id -> emits 'node-id' event + re-advertises on LAN beacon (host.rs:1850-1878)
node-port emitted/cleared on every (re)bind so 'ip:port' stays live (host.rs:333,413); node_port command for late mounts
session-password command + new_password (rotate) command + 'session-password' event (api.commands.ts:64-67, host.rs:581)
Auth throttle / OTP auto-rotation under brute-force (auth.rs throttle module)
node-version-error event -> 'update required' + clean offline (host.rs:1879+, api.events.ts:76)
unattended access mode: no OTP issued, anyone with the ID connects (host.rs:435, SelfCard warn)

missingPieces.feature: Stable / persistent device identity on mobile
missingPieces.description: Mobile generates a brand-new Identity on EVERY connect_host and EVERY go_online (Identity::generate() at mobile/src/client.rs:111 and mobile/src/host.rs:46). Because the relay maps pubkey->id, the phone gets a DIFFERENT 9-digit ID every time it goes online. This directly violates the product's 'stable device ID issued by the relay' premise and makes the 'Cihazim' ID, Gecmis reconnect, and any saved/unattended access meaningless. Desktop solves this with Identity::load_or_create(identity_path(app)).
missingPieces.approach: Add a persistent identity file in the mobile crate mirroring desktop util.rs::identity_path: resolve app.path().app_local_data_dir()/identity.key and call pulsar_core::crypto::Identity::load_or_create(path) instead of Identity::generate() in BOTH go_online and connect_host. Load it once and reuse (e.g. a manage()'d struct or a helper fn taking AppHandle). The client identity should ALSO be stable so the host can recognize the same phone across reconnects (TOFU/saved peers). No UI change.

missingPieces.feature: Live online/offline + ID-rotation + node-port event handling
missingPieces.description: Mobile sets 'online' implicitly from the one-shot go_online return value and never reacts to changes. There is no listener for 'node-id' (relay restart reissues a new id -> mobile keeps showing/advertising a dead id), no 'node-port' (mobile never shows a direct ip:port for LAN connects), and no 'node-version-error' (update-required) handling. The status line is a static 'Bekleniyor' string set once.
missingPieces.approach: In mobile/src/host.rs add the same emit('node-id', new_id.grouped()) rotation watcher and a node-version-error emit that the desktop has (port the Weak<Node>+id_changed_handle/version_error_handle pattern). In ui/index.html, replace the one-shot status with window.__TAURI__.event.listen('node-id', ...) to update #host-id, listen for a go-offline/version-error event to flip #host-status back to Cevrimdisi, and re-enable the 'Cevrimici Ol' button. Keep the existing live dot animation; add an explicit 'Cevrimdisi ol' toggle.

missingPieces.feature: One-time password lifecycle: copy + rotate/refresh
missingPieces.description: Mobile shows the OTP once in #host-pw (returned by go_online) but provides no copy button and no refresh/rotate. Desktop has copy + a refresh button (new_password) and a 'session-password' event so the displayed code stays current after auto-rotation. The throttle-driven auto-rotation that desktop performs is also absent on mobile, so a brute-forced static OTP is never invalidated.
missingPieces.approach: Add new_password + session_password Tauri commands to the mobile host (store the password in a managed Mutex<String> instead of a local var so it can be read/rotated; emit 'session-password' on rotate).
