In ui/index.html add a copy-to-clipboard tap on the .pw-chip and a small refresh icon-button next to it (touch-first: bigger tap target than desktop). Wire the auth loop in host.rs to read the live password each session like desktop's password_store. Consider porting the global wrong-guess auto-rotate from auth.rs (smaller scope acceptable for v1).

missingPieces.feature: Copy device ID + local-address row on 'Cihazim'
missingPieces.description: Mobile has a 'Share ID' (navigator.share) button but no one-tap copy of the bare 9-digit ID, and no local ip:port row for direct/LAN connects (desktop SelfCard shows both copy-ID and a copy-able ip:port). On a phone, copy-to-clipboard is the primary way to hand off the ID.
missingPieces.approach: Add a copy-ID button (touch target ~44px) under #host-id using navigator.clipboard.writeText with the digits stripped, mirroring the desktop copyId() check-flash. Optionally surface node-port (once that event exists) as a copyable ip:port chip. Reuse the existing share-id button; keep both.

missingPieces.feature: Connected-peers list + kick on 'Cihazim'
missingPieces.description: When the phone is hosting, mobile shows no list of who is currently connected and no way to disconnect them. Desktop SelfCard lists each host session (peer id/name) with a per-session kick button (onDisconnect). This is the host-side situational awareness/security control.
missingPieces.approach: Emit a 'session' event from mobile host.rs serve_with's on-connect/on-disconnect (and a peer label) the way desktop does, plus a disconnect_session(sid) command. In ui/index.html render a touch-friendly connected-peers list under the status line with a swipe/button 'Kes' per peer. Reuses the existing .item / .row-list styles.

missingPieces.feature: Persisted relay/network settings + remove TEMP auto-online diagnostic
missingPieces.description: Mobile settings (relay, netmode, device name, codec) live only in localStorage and are passed per-call; there is no Rust Config persistence and the relay default is a hardcoded LAN IP (192.168.68.51:21116). Also ui/index.html contains a TEMP block (search 'TEMP (diagnose)') that auto-go-online's as 'TestHost' and self-connects on every load -- this will register a junk device and must be removed before parity.
missingPieces.approach: Remove the setTimeout TEMP diagnostic self-connect block in ui/index.html. Optionally add a get_config/set_config command pair backed by a small on-disk config in the mobile crate so relay/name/netmode survive reinstall-independent of the webview localStorage, matching desktop's Config. Ship a sane default relay (the public Pulsar relay) rather than a LAN literal.

findings.notes: Mobile already has a genuine 'Cihazim' (host) tab that calls go_online and shows the returned 9-digit ID, an offline/live status line, a PW chip and share button (ui/index.html, #t-host section; mobile/src/host.rs:32-160) -- so the area is 'partial', not absent. The single most important and lowest-cost fix is #1: mobile uses Identity::generate() in BOTH client.rs:111 and host.rs:46, so the device ID is NON-stable (changes every launch), which breaks the relay's stable-ID contract the whole feature depends on; desktop's exact solution is Identity::load_or_create(identity_path) (host.rs:383, util.rs:44). All other gaps (live status/rotation events, OTP copy+rotate, copy-ID, connected-peers+kick) are polish toward desktop SelfCard parity and need touch-first redesign, not 1:1 ports. Excluded by product rules and NOT proposed: file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor (remote-desktop-only). The TEMP auto-go-online diagnostic in ui/index.html must be deleted -- it registers a junk 'TestHost' on every page load.

findings.area: Session lifecycle: connecting screen, transport/stats overlay, disconnect/end, teardown, error states
desktopCapabilities: Connecting.svelte: full-screen connecting view with REAL phased milestones — reaching out, then the ACTUAL transport (P2P vs relay, via the conn-phase event keyed by target id), then auth/awaiting-host-approval, then preparing/first-frame (src/lib/screens/Connecting.svelte:25-70)
Connecting screen Cancel button + a 12s 'slow / offline host or firewall' hint (Connecting.svelte:74-107)

VideoStatus.svelte status chain over the canvas: stalled='stream stopped' (session.streamStopped), first-frame loading pulse-ring (session.connecting/waiting), error note (videoErr), click-to-control, and the Ctrl+Shift+F12 leave-combo hint (src/lib/screens/Session/VideoStatus.svelte:38-73)

Live perf HUD: fps/drops/mbps/decodeMs via the play-vstats event (events.rs:197 PlayVStats), RTT via play-rtt (events.rs:172), host encode summary via host-stats (events.rs:179) — rendered in the game overlay

Clean teardown on host-close / network error / user-leave: the host hold-loop emits play-ended (api.events.ts:156-159), client releases the keyboard/mouse grab and ends the tab (Session.svelte:539-559)
Session keepalive + host ~6s silent-death timeout; stop_remote_play closes the control session so the host sees a real disconnect (play.rs:1892)
Transport (P2P vs relay) surfaced to the user as a connecting milestone and a net-mode pill

missingPieces.feature: Connecting screen (phased, cancelable) for mobile
missingPieces.description: Mobile has NO connecting screen. doConnect only writes 'Bağlanıyor…' text into the connect card and disables the button (ui/index.html:318); the entire connect_host rust call (register -> connect -> authenticate -> query caps -> request_stream) is one opaque blocking await (client.rs:106-169) with no phase reporting, no transport (P2P/relay) indicator, no cancel, no slow-host hint. A user on a slow/offline host sees a frozen disabled button.
missingPieces.approach: Add a full-screen mobile connecting overlay (touch-first, portrait): target id + mode + animated pulse-ring (mirror the desktop Connecting steps but re-laid-out for a phone, not ported). Emit phase events from rust: in client.rs after node.connect() returns, read sess transport and app.emit a 'conn-phase' {transport} like desktop events.rs:165; emit again after authenticate() (auth done) and after request_stream() (preparing). The webview shows a steps list and a Cancel that aborts. Cancel needs a cancel path — store an abort flag/JoinHandle so connect_host can be dropped. Wire a tauri::Emitter import in mobile lib.rs/client.rs.

missingPieces.feature: Live transport/stats overlay in the in-session bar
missingPieces.description: The in-session bar shows a static 'Bağlı' + bar-meta = only 'CODEC · p2p/oturum' (ui/index.html:258-259, set once at connect ui/index.html:322). There is NO live fps / RTT / bitrate / decode-latency / transport indicator. The rust read loop already counts rtp_video_frames / aus / audio every tick but only writes them to log::info (client.rs:186-218) — nothing is emitted to the webview. Desktop has a real perf HUD (play-vstats/play-rtt/host-stats).
missingPieces.approach: In the client.rs read loop, every ~1s compute fps from the frame counter delta and the native decoder's stats (the pulsar-video plugin can expose decoded-frame/drop counts) and app.emit a 'play-stats' {fps, mbps, transport} event; capture transport from the session right after connect. In ui/index.html, listen via window.__TAURI__.event.listen and update bar-meta (or expand the bar into a compact touch HUD: a tappable chip that expands to fps/latency/bitrate/transport). Keep it game-mode-aware (cyan) per the two-mode rule; keep it minimal in remote mode.

missingPieces.feature: Proper disconnect/end teardown (close session, not just detach surface)
missingPieces.description: btn-end only calls plugin:pulsar-video|detach and removes the in-session class (ui/index.html:366). It does NOT tear down the pulsar-core session/Node — the rust read loop only exits when sess.recv() returns None (client.rs:209-211); detaching the surface leaves the spawned task and the held Node alive, so the host keeps capturing/streaming until its keepalive timeout (~6s) instead of an immediate clean disconnect. Desktop has stop_remote_play that closes the control session so the host sees the leave at once.
missingPieces.approach: Add an end_session(slot) tauri command in client.rs: store per-slot a cancellation token (e.g. tokio::sync::Notify or an AbortHandle for the spawned task) alongside the InputSenders map; on end_session, signal it so the loop breaks, then drop the session/Node (closing the encrypted session so the host's serve_with recv returns) and call stop_stream(slot). Register it in lib.rs:30. Replace the detach-only path in ui/index.html:366 to invoke end_session first, then detach. Handle split (slot 1) too.

missingPieces.feature: Mid-session error / host-gone state (play-ended equivalent)
missingPieces.description: If the connection drops mid-session (host closes, network error), the rust read loop breaks silently on recv()==None (client.rs:209-211) and stops the surface, but it emits NOTHING to the webview. The UI stays body.in-session with a frozen native surface and the 'Bağlı' bar still claiming a live connection — no error, no return-to-home, no reconnect. Desktop emits play-ended and tears the tab down cleanly (Session.svelte:539-559).
missingPieces.approach: When the read loop exits (both Ok(None) and after the post-loop cleanup at client.rs:209-223), app.emit a 'play-ended' {slot, reason} event. In ui/index.html add window.__TAURI__.event.listen('play-ended', ...) that drops in-session, detaches the surface, and shows a touch-friendly disconnect notice (e.g. a toast/sheet: 'Bağlantı kesildi' with a Reconnect button that re-runs doConnect from history). Distinguish reasons (host closed vs network error) for the message. Reuse the existing recent-history reconnect path.

missingPieces.feature: Stalled-stream (no-frames / freeze) indication during session
missingPieces.description: Mobile has no equivalent of the desktop stalled / 'stream stopped' overlay (VideoStatus.svelte:38-44) nor the first-frame 'waiting for host' loader (VideoStatus.svelte:56-64). After connect the webview immediately flips to in-session (ui/index.html:323) even before any frame arrives; if the host is slow to bring up capture (the very case client.rs:174-176 comments on) the user sees a transparent/black surface with no feedback, and a mid-session freeze (frames stop but session alive) shows nothing.
missingPieces.approach: Track first-frame and last-frame-time in the client.rs loop; emit 'play-firstframe' {slot} on the first decoded AU and 'play-stall' {slot, stalled:bool} when no TAG_VIDEO frame for ~2s (toggle off when frames resume). In ui/index.html overlay a touch-first 'Bağlanıyor / ilk kare bekleniyor' pulse loader until firstframe, and a 'Yayın durdu' overlay while stalled (mirroring VideoStatus states, redesigned for full-screen mobile). Keep it over the transparent webview so the native surface shows through once frames flow.

missingPieces.feature: Remove the diagnostic auto-connect/self-host shim
missingPieces.description: ui/index.html:414-424 has a TEMP diagnostic block that, 2s after load, auto-calls go_online then connect_host to itself. This is debug scaffolding that hijacks the session lifecycle (it can put the app into a self-session on launch) and must be removed before the lifecycle UX is real; it also pollutes any real connecting/teardown flow.
missingPieces.approach: Delete the setTimeout diagnostic block at ui/index.html:414-424. Gate any remaining dev self-test behind a query param or build flag so it never runs in the shipping mobile client.

findings.notes: Mobile is a thin client only for session lifecycle: connect works (client.rs connect_host: register->connect->authenticate->query_stream_caps->request_stream with media-over-session, RTP read loop -> rtp depacketizer -> native pulsar-video plugin), and a basic end (detach) exists, so status is PARTIAL not absent. The gaps are all about FEEDBACK and clean TEARDOWN, not the transport itself. Critical (priority 1) trio: (a) no real connecting screen/phases, (b) end only detaches the surface and never closes the pulsar-core session so the host lingers until keepalive timeout (client.rs has NO disconnect command; only connect_host/send_pointer/send_button are wired in lib.rs:30), and (c) no mid-session host-gone/error event to the webview (the rust loop breaks silently at client.rs:209-211 and emits nothing). The mobile rust side currently emits ZERO events to the webview — every feedback piece here requires adding app.emit calls in client.rs (it imports tauri::Manager but not Emitter yet) plus window.__TAURI__.event.listen wiring in the single ui/index.html. Keep all new overlays mode-aware (remote=indigo thin strip, game=cyan minimal HUD) per desktop-app/CLAUDE.md's two-mode rule, and do NOT add file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor to game mode. Reuse the existing oklch design tokens already inlined at the top of ui/index.html (matching design/project/assets/tokens.css).

findings.area: i18n (Turkish UI copy) + branding/design-system (PulsarMark logo, tokens, fonts) on mobile
desktopCapabilities: Runtime i18n with TWO catalogs (tr + en), ~370+ keys each: desktop-app/src/lib/i18n.tr.ts + i18n.en.ts, assembled in i18n.catalogs.ts
Reactive t(key, vars) helper with {var} interpolation and tr->en->raw-key fallback: i18n.svelte.ts:69-75
Language switcher (LANGS tr/en), system-language auto-detect, localStorage persistence (pulsar.lang.v1), runtime cycleLang(): i18n.svelte.ts:17-66
Frontend language change pushed to the Rust backend via set_language so tray/host strings/native overlay --lang stay in sync: i18n.svelte.ts:56-60
i18n unit test guarding key parity / fallback: i18n.test.ts

Design tokens as the single source of truth (oklch palette, indigo accent, cyan gaming accent, type scale, radii, shadows, spacing, motion): design/project/assets/tokens.css; ported into the SvelteKit app's app.css
PulsarMark component: concentric pulse-ring logo, size + color props, used across desktop + website: desktop-app/src/lib/PulsarMark.svelte, website/src/lib/components/PulsarMark.svelte (with a test)

missingPieces.feature: No i18n layer at all on mobile — Turkish strings are hardcoded in HTML/JS
missingPieces.description: Mobile ships zero translation infrastructure. Every visible string is a Turkish literal baked into desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html (e.g. 'Bağlan' line 147, 'Uzak Masaüstü' line 151, 'Cihazım' line 171, 'Çevrimiçi Ol' line 180, 'Ayarlar' line 190, 'Geçmiş' line 233, status text 'Çevrimdışı' line 175, error builders 'Hata: ' line 313, 'Bağlanıyor…' line 318, 'window.__TAURI__ yok' line 313, 'az önce'/'dk önce' in ago() line 332). There is no t() helper, no key catalog, no en catalog, no language state, no persistence. The desktop's ~370-key tr/en catalogs are completely unreachable from the mobile webview (separate frontend, not SvelteKit). This means the app is Turkish-only on mobile and the copy is duplicated/diverging from the canonical catalog (e.g. desktop uses 'Çevrimiçi ol' / 'Cihaz kimliği' / net-mode labels 'Yalnız relay'; mobile independently re-types 'Çevrimiçi Ol' / 'Cihaz ID' / 'Yalnız Relay').
missingPieces.approach: Add a tiny self-contained i18n shim to the mobile webview (do NOT import the SvelteKit module graph — mobile is plain HTML/JS). Two options: (a) lightweight — extract the relevant subset of keys into a new desktop-app/mobile/ui/i18n.js that exports { tr, en } plain objects plus a t(key, vars) function mirroring i18n.svelte.ts:69-75 (same {var} interpolation + tr->en->key fallback), wire it into index.html by giving every literal a data-i18n attribute (or replacing inline text with t('...') calls in the <script>), detect language from navigator.language and persist to localStorage 'pulsar.lang.v1' (same key the desktop uses for cross-surface consistency). (b) build-time — add a small script that re-exports the canonical desktop catalogs (i18n.tr.ts / i18n.en.ts) as a JS bundle copied into ui/ so strings never diverge. Reuse the existing LS helper at index.html:273. Rust changes: none required for the webview; optionally call the existing set_language path if mobile later adds a native overlay. Files: create desktop-app/mobile/ui/i18n.js; edit desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html (replace all hardcoded Turkish literals + the ago()/netPill/host-status string builders).

missingPieces.feature: No language switcher on mobile (Turkish-only, no English)
missingPieces.description: Desktop offers tr<->en via cycleLang()/LANGS (i18n.svelte.ts:17-66) with a UI toggle (chrome.languageToggle copy exists). Mobile has neither an English catalog nor any switcher control. The mobile Settings tab (index.html:189-229) has Ağ/Cihaz/Hakkında sections but NO 'Görünüm'/language row. <html lang="tr"> is hardcoded at index.html:2.
missingPieces.approach: Once the i18n shim exists, add a 'Dil' (language) setting row to the mobile Settings tab using the existing .seg segmented-control pattern already used for netmode/codecpref (index.html:201-205, 217-221) — a touch-first TR/EN segmented toggle is the natural mobile design, no custom component needed. On pick: call setLang(), persist to 'pulsar.lang.v1', update <html lang>, and re-render visible strings (re-run the data-i18n pass or re-call the string builders). This is a touch-first re-design of the desktop's icon toggle into a segmented control, hence designNeeded. Files: edit index.html (add the row + handler in the settings <script> block near line 304-306) and i18n.js (setLang/detect).

missingPieces.feature: PulsarMark logo is an inline ad-hoc SVG copy, not the shared component/asset
missingPieces.description: Mobile re-hand-codes the pulse-ring mark as a raw <svg class="mark"> at index.html:133-138 with the same 4-circle geometry as PulsarMark.svelte:5-9 but slightly different attributes (mobile hardcodes stroke="var(--brand)" so it follows the mode accent; desktop component takes a color prop defaulting to var(--accent)). It is a manual copy that can drift from the canonical PulsarMark.svelte. Functionally it renders correctly (correct concentric-circles geometry, pure circles, mode-aware via --brand), so branding is visually present but not DRY/source-of-truth-linked.
missingPieces.approach: Low urgency (it currently looks right). Option to de-duplicate: extract the mark SVG markup into a shared snippet/string (e.g. a const SVG in i18n.js or a tiny mark.js) injected at runtime, or keep the inline copy but add a code comment cross-referencing PulsarMark.svelte:5-9 so future geometry/brand changes are mirrored. No new design needed — the mark is already touch-appropriate (26px). Files: desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html (lines 133-138).

missingPieces.feature: Design tokens are duplicated inline in mobile, not imported from tokens.css (drift risk + already-diverged values)
missingPieces.description: Mobile copies the oklch palette/radii/shadows directly into the <style> :root at index.html:9-27 instead of importing design/project/assets/tokens.css (the documented source of truth). They are close but ALREADY diverging: mobile renames --text-on-accent to --on-accent (index.html:14 vs tokens.css:23), omits --accent-hover/--accent-press/--accent-soft-2/--ok-soft/--warn-soft/--danger-soft, uses a different --shadow-accent and --ease (mobile uses cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1) = tokens' --ease-out; tokens' --ease is 0.22,1,0.36,1), and adds mobile-only tokens (--nav-h, --brand/--brand-soft/--brand-ring mode aliases). Buttons re-implement .btn from scratch (index.html:68-73) rather than the tokens.css .btn atom (tokens.css:98-136), with mobile-tuned 16px font + full-width primary — appropriate for touch but not linked to source.
missingPieces.approach: Keep mobile-specific touch tuning (larger tap targets, 16px inputs to avoid iOS zoom, --nav-h, --brand aliases, full-width buttons) but reduce drift: (a) rename --on-accent back to --text-on-accent to match tokens.css; (b) add a short header comment in the index.html :root noting it is a touch-adapted subset of design/project/assets/tokens.css and listing intentional deltas; or (c) split the shared base tokens into a copied tokens.css in ui/ (mobile can't @import outside its dir at build time, so a copied/symlinked file) and layer mobile overrides on top. The mode-aware --brand swap ([data-mode='game'] at index.html:28) correctly mirrors the desktop cyan/indigo two-mode look and should stay. Files: desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html (:root + .btn rules).

missingPieces.feature: Diagnostic / placeholder copy and a hardcoded LAN relay shipped in the mobile UI
missingPieces.description: The mobile UI contains debug artifacts that will surface as broken branding/copy to a user: a TEMP auto-go-online-as-host block logs 'PULSARHOST'/'PULSARCONN' and force-connects on load (index.html:414-424), the default relay is hardcoded to a developer LAN IP '192.168.68.51:21116' (index.html:299) instead of the product default relay, and raw technical strings leak to the user as UI copy ('window.__TAURI__ yok' at index.html:313). These are not localized and not user-appropriate.
missingPieces.approach: Remove the TEMP diagnostic block (index.html:414-424); replace the hardcoded dev relay default (index.html:299) with the product's real default relay (match the desktop Config.relay default); route the raw 'window.__TAURI__ yok'/'Hata: ' strings through the new t() catalog so they are localized and user-friendly. Pure JS edits, no Rust change. Files: desktop-app/mobile/ui/index.html.

findings.notes: Mobile branding/design-system is visually PRESENT and faithful: it reuses the exact font families (Space Grotesk / Hanken Grotesk / JetBrains Mono via a self-hosted desktop-app/mobile/ui/fonts.css + ui/fonts/*.woff2, all 13 weights present), the oklch indigo/cyan palette, the pulse-ring mark, and the mode-aware indigo<->cyan accent swap ([data-mode='game'], index.html:28) — so the look matches the design system. The GAP is structural, not cosmetic: (1) i18n is the biggest miss — mobile has NO translation layer; every string is a hardcoded Turkish literal in index.html, there is no English catalog, no t() helper, no switcher, and the copy is independently re-typed so it can (and already slightly does) diverge from the canonical desktop tr catalog. Note mobile cannot import the SvelteKit i18n.svelte.ts module (plain HTML/JS, withGlobalTauri), so parity needs a small self-contained shim or a build-time re-export of the desktop catalogs. (2) Tokens + PulsarMark are inline copies, not linked to the design/ source of truth, with a few already-diverged token names/values (--on-accent vs --text-on-accent, --ease vs --ease-out). (3) Debug artifacts (TEMP auto-host block index.html:414-424, hardcoded dev LAN relay index.html:299, raw error strings) must be cleaned before any release. Per product rules I did NOT propose anything game-mode-excluded (file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor) — none apply to this area. All paths cited are relative to repo root /home/kahverengi/Projects/pulsar/.

findings.area: Split-screen / multiple concurrent sessions / tab switching
desktopCapabilities: Unlimited concurrent host sessions, each shown as a closable tab in a horizontal tab strip (Tabs.svelte) with a live/connecting status dot and Home tab; activeTab switches which session is on screen + receives input (sessions.svelte.ts SessionManager.sessions / activeTab)
Per-tab inline rename (pencil button), session-scoped label that falls back to target name (Tabs.svelte:24-40, sessions.svelte.ts renameTab:591)

Split mode with 3 layouts: h2 (left/right), v2 (top/bottom), grid4 (2x2) chosen in a modal SplitPicker.svelte; enter / reshape / exit without disconnecting any session (enterSplit/setLayout/exitSplit in sessions.svelte.ts:164-221)

Each pane is an INDEPENDENT session (same or different host); empty panes show a PaneGameConnect/PaneConnect connect UI so you pick a distinct target per pane (+page.svelte ~905-910, connectIntoPane:305)
Focused pane drives default keyboard/mouse/controller routing via api.setActiveSession; clicking a pane focuses it (focusPane:225, +page.svelte:866,897)
Per-cell reduced resolution sent live to the host to save bandwidth/decode: 2-up -> 720p, 4-up -> 540p, restored to host-default on exit (#paneResolution / #applyPaneResolutions:135-158)
Backend pane-count cap pushed to Rust resident-pool reaper via api.setPaneCount (#syncPaneCount:144)

Same-host multi-pane intelligence: auto-assigns a FREE host monitor to a second pane to the same host, or per-window (HWND 'Pencere') capture so two panes share one monitor (freeDisplayFor:283, displayIdx/windowHwnd plumbing)
Split personality is fixed to one mode (all-gaming OR all-remote, captured at entry, never mixed) and switching app mode auto-exits split (splitSessionMode:117, +page.svelte:97-100)
Couch co-op: per-pane controller/input device assignment modal when >=2 live game panes (assignPanes derived, +page.svelte:138-144)
Split + tab state surfaces in chrome: split button lit when active across Chrome.svelte, BottomDock.svelte, GamingShell.svelte

Dedup of repeated connects to same (host,mode,game) so a tab isn't opened twice, while explicit displayIdx/windowHwnd opt OUT so deliberate second panes become real second sessions (startConnect:421-433)
Background sessions stay mounted when dropped from a smaller layout (4->2) — never disconnected; removed/dead session vacates its pane back to an empty connect cell (removeTab:575-587)

missingPieces.feature: Multi-session tabs + tab switching
missingPieces.description: Mobile has NO concept of multiple sessions or a tab strip. doConnect always overwrites the single in-session view; there is no way to keep several hosts connected and switch between them. The desktop's core SessionManager.sessions[]/activeTab model is entirely absent — mobile tracks state only via a body.in-session CSS class and a single 'bar' overlay (index.html:256-267, 315-327).
missingPieces.approach: Introduce a lightweight session registry in ui/index.html JS (array of {slot,id,codec,mode}) mirroring SessionManager, but as a touch-first design (NOT the desktop tab strip). For mobile, a horizontal pill switcher or a swipe-down session sheet over the video reading better than tiny tabs. Each session = a native decoder slot. The Android plugin currently hard-caps slots to coerceIn(0,1) (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:113,131,150,191) and stacks them; for >2 background (hidden) sessions you'd keep extra decoders paused or tear them down on switch. Rust connect_host already keys InputSenders by slot (client.rs:32,178), so the slot abstraction extends; bump the slot cap and add a setActiveSession-equivalent to route input/audio to the foregrounded slot. Audio is currently slot-0-only (client.rs:151,199) — must follow the active session instead.

missingPieces.feature: Split layout chooser (h2 / v2 / grid4) + exit-split affordance
missingPieces.description: Mobile split is hardcoded to a single vertical 2-up (top/bottom): pressing #btn-split immediately reconnects slot 1 with NO layout choice, and there is no exit-split control. Desktop SplitPicker.svelte offers 3 layouts (incl. side-by-side h2 and 2x2 grid4) plus an explicit exit. touchTarget only splits on H/2 (index.html:365,372-376); Android positionPanes only does stacked halves (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:344-356).
missingPieces.approach: Mobile is mostly landscape for streaming, so side-by-side (h2) is actually the natural split and stacked (v2) the exception — re-think which layouts to expose rather than porting all 3. Add a small bottom-sheet layout picker (touch-first, big tap targets) opened by #btn-split instead of insta-splitting. Update touchTarget() to compute slot from the chosen layout (left/right vs top/bottom; 2x2 quadrants) and add an exit-split button to the in-session bar that detaches slot>0 decoders and returns to single. In Android positionPanes(), support left/right and quadrant gravity, not just TOP/BOTTOM.

missingPieces.feature: Per-pane target selection (distinct host per pane)
missingPieces.description: On desktop each empty pane connects to its OWN chosen target via PaneConnect, and same-host panes auto-pick a free monitor. Mobile's #btn-split blindly reuses the SAME device id from the Bağlan field for slot 1 (index.html:365 doConnect(rawId(),1)) — you cannot put a different host in the second pane, and there is no free-monitor / window-capture logic, so two panes to the same host would fight over the same display.
missingPieces.approach: When a split slot is opened empty, show a touch connect sheet (reuse the Bağlan id/pw fields in a compact modal) to pick that pane's target, instead of reusing rawId(). Pass display_idx for a same-host second pane: client.rs already accepts the StreamReq but hardcodes display_idx:0 (client.rs:160) — thread a displayIdx param through connect_host and add a mobile equivalent of freeDisplayFor (track which display each live slot claimed in InputSenders or a parallel map).

missingPieces.feature: 4-up (grid4) / more than 2 concurrent panes
missingPieces.description: Desktop supports 4 concurrent panes (grid4) with 540p per cell. Mobile is capped at 2: Android PulsarVideoPlugin.kt coerces every slot to 0..1 (lines 113,131,150,191) and only knows stacked halves.
missingPieces.approach: Likely DEFER for phones (4-up is impractical on a phone screen and on mobile decode budget) but reasonable for tablets. If pursued: raise the slot coerce range, generalize the Pane map + positionPanes() to a 2x2 grid, and reuse desktop's #paneResolution tiering (add 540p request via StreamReq width/height in client.rs:153-154, currently 0x0). Gate behind a tablet/large-screen check.

missingPieces.feature: Per-cell reduced resolution while split
missingPieces.description: Desktop asks the host for 720p (2-up) / 540p (4-up) per cell to save bandwidth+decode, critical on constrained hardware (#paneResolution:135). Mobile always requests width:0,height:0 (host default) for every slot (client.rs:153-154,156), so a 2-up split streams two full-res streams to a phone — wasteful and likely to stutter.
missingPieces.approach: In client.rs set StreamReq.width/height based on the active pane count (e.g. 1280x720 when a second slot is live). Since the mobile UI knows when it goes split, pass an explicit width/height (or a pane-count) into connect_host and into a setPlayResolution-style live update; pulsar-core request_stream already carries width/height. Simpler than desktop because there's no live reshape — just send reduced res at connect time for slot>0.

missingPieces.feature: Focused-pane input/audio routing
missingPieces.description: Desktop focuses a pane (tap) to route keyboard/mouse/controller + treat it as active (focusPane -> api.setActiveSession). Mobile routes touch to whichever slot the touch lands in geometrically (index.html:370-378) which is fine for pointer, but there is no 'active/focused pane' concept for audio (hardcoded to slot 0, client.rs:151,199) or for a foregrounded session — so audio always comes from pane 0 regardless of which pane the user is interacting with.
missingPieces.approach: Add a setActivePane(slot) Tauri command on mobile that re-points audio (and any future global input) to the focused slot, called on tap-to-focus and on session switch. On the host side audio currently only transmits for slot 0 (client.rs transmit_audio: slot==0); switch to transmitting on the active slot and muting others, or keep one audio stream and re-subscribe on focus change.

missingPieces.feature: Per-session rename / labeling
missingPieces.description: Desktop lets you rename each session tab (Tabs.svelte rename, sessions.svelte.ts renameTab). Mobile has no tabs and no labels — sessions are anonymous, identified only by the id shown in the bar meta.
missingPieces.approach: Low value until the multi-session switcher exists. Once a session list/switcher is added (item above), store an optional per-session label in the JS session registry and show it in the switcher; default to the device id like desktop's fallback to target.name.

findings.notes: Mobile is NOT single-session-only as the hint guessed — there IS a working 2-up split path (vertical stacked) end to end: webview button (index.html:261-266,365), slot-keyed touch routing (touchTarget:370-378), slot-keyed Rust input senders + read loop (client.rs:32,37-53,178,197), and a native 2-pane Android renderer (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:454 Pane(slot), positionPanes:344). So status is 'partial', not 'absent'. The real gap is everything ELSE the desktop split/tab system does: (1) no multi-session tabs / session switching at all (mobile can only show ONE single session OR a fixed 2-up split — you can't hold 3 hosts and switch), (2) no layout chooser/exit-split (split is insta-2up-vertical, no h2/grid4, no leave-split button), (3) the second pane reuses the SAME id from the connect field (index.html:365) instead of a per-pane target picker, (4) no per-cell reduced resolution (always host-default, client.rs:153-154), (5) audio is pinned to slot 0 with no focused-pane follow. Android slot cap is hardcoded 0..1 (coerceIn(0,1)). Per the product rules I did NOT propose file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor pane features in game mode. Recommend designing mobile split as touch-first landscape-side-by-side rather than porting the desktop SplitPicker 1:1; defer grid4/4-up to tablets. Note the index.html has a TEMP auto-self-connect block (lines 414-424) that should be removed before shipping any of this.

roadmap.summary: Mobile (desktop-app/mobile/) is a single ~430-line ui/index.html with one inline <script> (no bundler, no modules, withGlobalTauri) plus a thin Rust crate (client.rs/host.rs/rtp.rs/lib.rs) wiring only connect_host/send_pointer/send_button/go_online/open_a11y_settings/a11y_enabled. Versus the full desktop SvelteKit app, mobile is a working happy-path THIN CLIENT: it can connect by 9-digit relay ID, stream H.264/H.265 video + Opus audio to a native MediaCodec/SurfaceView (Path A), forward left-tap+absolute pointer, do a fixed 2-up vertical split, and act as a screen-share HOST (go_online + a11y remote control). But it lacks almost every robustness/feedback/control layer: no connecting screen or transport (P2P vs relay) feedback, no connect timeout, no friendly error mapping, no session-drop/teardown events (host lingers to keepalive), no in-session overlay/menu, no perf HUD, no live stream-quality controls, no keyboard/right-click/scroll, no on-screen gamepad, no physical-controller forwarding, no mic upstream, no clipboard/file/chat (remote-only side channels), no multi-monitor picker, no saved-devices address book, no i18n (hardcoded Turkish), and — critically — it regenerates Identity::generate() on EVERY connect/go_online so the relay ID is NOT stable, and ships a TEMP auto-self-connect+auto-go-online diagnostic (index.html:414-424) that must be deleted. The wire protocol and host side already support nearly everything (InputEvent variants, DataMsg side channels, StreamReq quality fields, StreamCaps.displays, Restream), so most gaps are mobile-client wiring + touch-first UI, not core/protocol work. Two product personalities (remote=indigo vs game=cyan) and the remote-vs-game feature split (file/clipboard/mic/multi-monitor are REMOTE-ONLY, never in game mode) must be respected throughout. The single hardest structural blocker is that the monolithic inline script must be split into ES modules before parallel feature work, and the read loop must learn to decode non-media DataMsg frames (today it drops everything except RTP video/audio) before clipboard/file/chat/rumble can work at all.

moduleStructure.purpose: Shell only: <head> token/font links, the static DOM skeleton (nav tabs, tab sections, in-session bar container, overlay mount points), and a single <script type=module> that imports ./js/app.js. All inline logic and inline <style> tokens get extracted out. This file becomes small and rarely edited so it is NOT a collision point.

Touch-adapted copy of design/project/assets/tokens.css (oklch palette, indigo/cyan two-mode --brand swap, radii, shadows, type scale) plus mobile-only tokens (--nav-h, 16px input sizing). Header comment listing intentional deltas vs the source-of-truth. Rename --on-accent back to --text-on-accent. Replaces the inline :root.

Touch component atoms extracted from the inline <style>: .btn (full-width primary), .seg segmented control, .field/.input, .item/.row-list, .card, .bar glass pill, bottom-sheet/modal base classes. Shared by all screens so panels stay visually consistent.

Entry module: boots i18n, mounts the router, registers global event listeners (conn-phase, play-ended, play-stats, etc.), wires the bottom-nav. Owns app-level state init. Imports every screen/feature module. Thin orchestration so it is edited rarely.

Thin wrapper around window.__TAURI__ (core.invoke, event.listen, path, clipboard). Single chokepoint for the 'window.__TAURI__ yok' guard. Every other module imports invoke()/listen() from here so the global access pattern is centralized and testable.

Self-contained tr/en catalogs (subset re-exported from desktop i18n.tr.ts/i18n.en.ts) + t(key,vars) with {var} interpolation and tr->en->key fallback, navigator.language detect, persist to localStorage 'pulsar.lang.v1', setLang(). All other modules import t().

Minimal hash/state router + bottom-nav controller switching between screens (connect, devices, host/Cihazim, settings, in-session). Manages body classes (in-session, data-mode) so the two personalities theme correctly. Owns which screen is mounted.

Vanilla port of desktop peers.svelte.ts: saved-devices address book + recents persisted to localStorage 'pulsar.peers.v1', normalizeId/fmtPeerId, savedPeers/historyPeers/gameHistoryPeers, recordConnection/addPeer/updatePeer/removePeer/removeFromHistory/toggleFav. Client-local, no Rust.

Settings/config access: reads/writes via get_config/set_config Tauri commands (relay, network_mode, device_name, codec, language, quality prefs), with localStorage as cache/fallback. Single source of truth for settings consumed by connect/host/settings screens.
Connect (Bağlan) tab: ID/IP entry + validation (ported connectTarget.ts), mode toggle (remote/game), pre-connect quality section, doConnect orchestration. Owns the connect form only.

Full-screen touch-first connecting overlay: phased reach->transport(P2P/relay)->auth->preparing step list driven by conn-phase events, 12s slow-host hint, big Cancel button. Distinct file so one owner builds the whole connect-feedback UX.

Saved Devices + Geçmiş screen: vertical touch list (icon/name/grouped-id/online dot/last-seen), tap-to-connect, long-press/bottom-sheet for edit/forget/favorite, add-device sheet, clear-history. Consumes store/peers.js.

Cihazim (phone-as-host) tab: go_online/go_offline lifecycle toggle, stable-ID display + copy + share, OTP chip + copy + rotate, unattended toggle, connected-peers list + kick, incoming-request approval sheet, a11y settings entry. Drives host.rs commands/events.
Ayarlar tab: relay host:port (validated), network-mode seg, device name, codec pref, language TR/EN seg, About/GPLv3. Reads/writes store/config.js.

In-session controller: owns body.in-session lifecycle, the multi-slot session registry (mirrors SessionManager), end_session teardown, play-ended/stall/firstframe handling, active-pane focus + audio routing. Coordinates the session sub-modules below.

Touch->input engine: rAF-coalesced absolute pointer, tap/drag disambiguation, double-tap=double-click, long-press=right-click, two-finger tap=middle, two-finger scroll, trackpad/relative mode, gamepad-active gating. Ported from input.svelte.ts. Calls send_pointer/send_button/send_scroll/send_pointer_rel.

On-screen keyboard: hidden input to raise soft keyboard, char/key forwarding via send_char/send_key, ported keymap.ts EVDEV table, sticky-modifier special-key bar (Esc/Tab/Ctrl/Alt/Win/arrows/F-keys/Ctrl+Alt+Del). Remote-mode only.

In-session DOM overlay/dock over the transparent webview: opened by FAB/status-pill, collapsible cards. Hosts the mode-aware control sections (Stream/Display/Audio/Tools in remote; slim Stream/Gauges/Controllers in game). Mounts hud.js + feature panels.
Perf HUD: listens to play-stats/play-vstats events, renders fps/mbps/RTT/decode strip (mono, cyan in game). Stall + first-frame loader states. Toggle from overlay.
Stream-quality controls (remote+game): pre-connect + live set_play_codec/bitrate/fps/resolution/quality/encoder via the new in-session restream channel. Segmented pills in the overlay Stream card.
Remote-only Display card: view-fit (fit/fill/stretch) via plugin setAspect, orientation handling, and multi-monitor picker (host-displays event + set_play_monitor). Gated to remote + displays>1.
Audio controls: local mute toggle (plugin setAudioMuted, instant) + optional volume, and the mic upstream toggle (mic_start/mic_stop, remote-only). Both personalities show mute; mic remote-only.

Remote-only side channels UI: clipboard send/receive, chat panel (bottom sheet, visualViewport keyboard-aware), driven by send_clipboard/clipboard-in and send_chat/chat-msg. Strictly hidden in game mode.

Remote-only file manager: single-column remote browser (fs_list/fs-entries), per-file download (fs_get/file-recv), upload via OS picker, transfer-progress list + concurrency queue. Hidden in game mode.

Game-only on-screen virtual gamepad (Moonlight-style dual sticks/D-pad/ABXY/shoulders/triggers, multi-touch) AND physical-controller poll loop (navigator.getGamepads), both calling send_gamepad/send_gamepad_disconnect; rumble via gamepad haptics from 'rumble' events. Disables pointer forwarding while active.

Split/multi-session: layout chooser (h2/v2/grid4 where sensible), per-pane target picker, exit-split, slot->pane mapping, per-cell reduced resolution, focused-pane routing. Coordinates with session.js registry and the Android plugin pane positioning.

NEW Rust module: load-or-create persistent X25519 Identity in the Tauri app data dir (Identity::load_or_create) so the relay ID is stable; shared by client.rs (connect) and host.rs (go_online). Removes Identity::generate().
NEW Rust module: get_config/set_config commands persisting pulsar_core::Config (or a minimal serde struct) to app data dir; consumed by connect_host/go_online when args omitted.

NEW Rust module: in-session control commands (end_session with cancellation token, set_play_codec/bitrate/fps/resolution/quality/encoder, set_play_monitor) using a per-slot restream mpsc the read loop selects on. Keeps client.rs from ballooning.

NEW Rust module: decode_data branch for non-media frames in the read loop + emit Tauri events (clipboard-in, chat-msg, fs-entries, file-begin/recv/progress, rumble) and the matching send commands (send_clipboard/send_chat/fs_list/fs_get). The DataMsg plumbing all features depend on.

NEW Rust module: extra input commands (send_scroll, send_key, send_char, send_pointer_rel, send_gamepad, send_gamepad_disconnect) mirroring send_pointer/send_button, looking up the per-slot SessionSender. Separated so input wiring doesn't collide with client.rs.

roadmap.title: Delete TEMP auto-self-connect/auto-go-online diagnostic + fix default relay
roadmap.approach: Remove the setTimeout diagnostic block at ui/index.html:414-424 that auto-go-online's as TestHost and self-connects on every launch (registers junk host + triggers MediaProjection unexpectedly). Replace the hardcoded dev LAN relay 192.168.68.51:21116 (index.html:299) with the product default relay matching desktop Config.relay. Pure deletion + one constant.

roadmap.title: Split monolithic index.html into ES modules + extract tokens/components CSS
roadmap.approach: Convert the single inline <script> into <script type=module src=js/app.js>. Extract tokens into css/tokens.css (rename --on-accent->--text-on-accent, add delta comment) and atoms into css/components.css. Create js/tauri.js (invoke/listen wrapper + window.__TAURI__ guard), js/router.js (bottom-nav + body.in-session/data-mode classes), and stub screen modules. Move existing connect/host/settings/recents/touch logic into their modules verbatim first (no behavior change), so later waves can each own a distinct file without colliding. This is the foundation that unblocks all parallel UI work.

roadmap.title: Persistent device identity (stable relay ID across restarts)
roadmap.approach: Add src/identity.rs implementing load-or-create of an X25519 Identity in the Tauri app data dir (mirror desktop util.rs identity_path + Identity::load_or_create). Use it via Node::bind_with_identity in BOTH client.rs connect_host (replacing Identity::generate() at client.rs:111) and host.rs go_online (host.rs:46). One stable identity shared by both roles so the 9-digit ID is consistent. No UI change.

roadmap.title: Decode non-media DataMsg in the read loop + event/command plumbing
roadmap.approach: In src/datachan.rs (called from client.rs read loop ~lines 189-220), after service::media::parse returns None, call pulsar_core::service::decode_data(&bytes) and route DataMsg variants (Clipboard/Chat/FsEntries/FileBegin/FileChunk/FileEnd/Rumble) to app.emit Tauri events. Add a SessionSender send_data helper in pulsar-core/src/service/client.rs if absent (mirror send_input_via). This is the single blocker for clipboard/file/chat/rumble — none can work until it lands. Import tauri::Emitter (client.rs currently imports only Manager).

roadmap.title: i18n shim (tr/en catalogs + t() helper) for the webview
roadmap.approach: Create js/i18n.js with tr/en plain-object catalogs (subset re-exported/copied from desktop i18n.tr.ts/i18n.en.ts), a t(key,vars) helper mirroring i18n.svelte.ts:69-75 ({var} interp + tr->en->key fallback), navigator.language detect, persist to localStorage 'pulsar.lang.v1'. Replace hardcoded Turkish literals + the ago()/netPill/host-status string builders with t() calls. Route raw error strings ('window.__TAURI__ yok','Hata: ') through the catalog. Foundation so every later screen uses t() from the start.

roadmap.title: Core-backed config persistence (get_config/set_config) + store/config.js
roadmap.approach: Add src/config.rs with get_config/set_config commands loading/saving pulsar_core::Config (or a minimal serde struct) to the Tauri app data dir, registered in lib.rs. Create js/store/config.js reading/writing those (relay/network_mode/device_name/codec/language/quality), with localStorage as cache. Have connect_host/go_online read from Config when args omitted. Add relay host:port validity check + inline error.

roadmap.title: Saved-devices store (peers.js) replacing flat history
roadmap.approach: Port desktop peers.svelte.ts pure logic into js/store/peers.js (vanilla): peers array persisted to localStorage 'pulsar.peers.v1' (match desktop KEY), normalizeId/fmtPeerId, savedPeers/historyPeers/gameHistoryPeers, recordConnection/addPeer/updatePeer/removePeer/removeFromHistory/toggleFav, remote-vs-game timeline split via the mode flag. Replace ad-hoc addHistory with recordConnection. No Rust.

roadmap.title: Phased, cancelable connecting screen + real transport (P2P/relay) feedback
roadmap.approach: Build js/screens/connecting.js: full-screen touch overlay with target id+mode, reach->transport->auth->preparing step list, 12s slow-host hint, big Cancel. Emit conn-phase events from client.rs: after node.connect() read sess.transport() (Direct vs Relay) and app.emit before authenticate(); emit again after auth and after request_stream. Cancel needs an abort flag/JoinHandle so connect_host can be dropped. Update the in-session net pill to the REAL transport, not the configured mode.

roadmap.title: Connect timeout + friendly localized error mapping
roadmap.approach: Wrap connect/auth/request_stream awaits in client.rs in tokio::time::timeout (~45s overall + 30s post-auth) returning a distinct timeout error string. Port friendlyConnectError's substring->Turkish/English table into js/screens/connect.js (run through t()) so raw core ConnError strings (relayDown/peerUnreachable/notOnline/p2pFailed/timeout) become friendly copy. Re-enable the connect button + clear in-session on reject.

roadmap.title: Connect by IP / IP:port (LAN direct)
roadmap.approach: Port connectTarget.ts (isAddr/ipRe/fmtTarget/canConnectTarget) into js/screens/connect.js; stop forcing inputmode=numeric digit-stripping (allow digits + . + :). In client.rs branch: if target parses as SocketAddr use the address connect path pulsar-core exposes (verify against desktop play.rs), else DeviceId::parse for relay id. Keep grouped-3 formatting only for pure-digit IDs.

roadmap.title: Clean end_session teardown + play-ended/disconnect handling + reconnect
roadmap.approach: Add end_session(slot) in src/session_cmds.rs storing a per-slot cancellation token (Notify/AbortHandle) beside InputSenders; on call break the read loop, drop the Session/Node (so the host's serve_with recv returns immediately instead of waiting ~6s keepalive), and stop_stream(slot). Emit play-ended {slot,reason} when the read loop exits (Ok(None) or error). In js/session/session.js listen for play-ended: drop body.in-session, detach surface, show a touch 'Bağlantı kesildi — Tekrar bağlan' card reusing doConnect from last id+slot. Replace the detach-only btn-end path (index.html:366) to invoke end_session first.

roadmap.title: Extended input commands: scroll, key, char, relative pointer, multi-button
roadmap.approach: Add src/input_cmds.rs with send_scroll/send_key/send_char/send_pointer_rel commands mirroring send_button (look up per-slot SessionSender, send_input_via with InputEvent::Scroll/Key/Char/PointerRelative). send_button already accepts a button index. Register all in lib.rs. This unblocks the touch-input engine and on-screen keyboard. No new wire/host work (variants exist in wire.rs and host already injects them).

roadmap.title: Thread game_mode + quality through connect (StreamReq honors codec/fps/bitrate/res/quality)
roadmap.approach: Read data-mode in doConnect and pass game flag + persisted codec/fps/bitrate/res/quality into connect_host. In client.rs add params and thread into StreamReq (replace hardcoded game_mode=false at client.rs:154 and the codec=caps.codecs.first() at client.rs:136; honor pref when in caps; set decode_codecs + native mime). Set QualityPref::latency when game. Precondition for all gaming + quality work and fixes the dead codec-pref toggle.

roadmap.title: In-session DOM overlay/dock (mode-aware, opened by FAB/pill)
roadmap.approach: Build js/session/overlay.js: a touch-first bottom sheet / expanding dock over the transparent webview (DOM, NOT egui), opened by tapping the status pill or a dedicated FAB (the desktop hotspot+combo model does not fit touch). Collapsible cards styled with css/components.css atoms. Mode-aware: remote shows Stream/Display/Audio/Tools; game shows slim Stream/Gauges/Controllers. Mounts hud.js + the feature panels as child modules. Provides the host surface that quality/display/audio/sidechannels/files/gamepad panels attach to.

roadmap.title: Touch input engine: right-click/middle, scroll, double-click, tap/drag, trackpad
roadmap.approach: Build js/session/input.js (ported from input.svelte.ts): rAF-coalesce touchmove (one send per frame), 8px movement threshold for tap vs drag, double-tap=double-click, long-press=right-click (button 2 remap), two-finger tap=middle, two-finger drag=throttled scroll, plus a trackpad/relative mode toggle with an on-screen cursor dot. Gate pointer forwarding off when the game gamepad overlay is active. Calls the W2-inputcmds commands.

roadmap.title: On-screen keyboard + special-key/modifier bar (remote only)
roadmap.approach: Build js/session/keyboard.js: keyboard toggle in the overlay focuses a hidden input to raise the soft keyboard; map printable input->send_char and special keys->send_key via a ported keymap.ts EVDEV table with held-key release semantics. Add a scrollable special-key bar (Esc/Tab/arrows/F-keys) with sticky/latching Ctrl/Alt/Shift/Win modifiers + a Ctrl+Alt+Del macro. visualViewport-aware layout. Remote-mode only.

roadmap.title: Perf HUD + stall/first-frame feedback
roadmap.approach: In client.rs read loop compute fps/mbps over ~1s from existing frame/byte counters and app.emit play-stats {slot,fps,mbps,transport}; emit play-firstframe on first decoded AU and play-stall when no TAG_VIDEO for ~2s (toggle off on resume). Optionally surface decode ms from the plugin. Build js/session/hud.js: compact mono strip (cyan in game) listening to those events, first-frame pulse loader, 'Yayın durdu' stall overlay. Mounted in the overlay; toggleable.

roadmap.title: Live + pre-connect stream-quality controls (codec/bitrate/fps/res/quality/encoder)
roadmap.approach: Add a per-slot restream mpsc the read loop select!s on; in src/session_cmds.rs add set_play_codec/bitrate/fps/resolution/quality/encoder that push a new StreamReq onto it and re-call request_stream + re-arm pulsar_video().start_stream for the new SPS. Build js/session/quality.js: segmented pills + bitrate slider in the overlay Stream card (and a pre-connect quality section in connect.js with Auto/Data-saver/Balanced/Performance presets). Reuse desktop res->WxH mapping. Encoder is advanced/read-from-caps, default auto.

roadmap.title: Local audio mute toggle (+ optional volume)
roadmap.approach: Add setAudioMuted(muted) to the pulsar-video plugin (mobile.rs run_mobile_plugin 'setAudioMuted', desktop.rs no-op, Android @Command audioTrack.setVolume(0/gain) under audioLock, permission toml). Build js/session/audio.js with a speaker/mute icon-button in the overlay/bar (both personalities) persisting via localStorage. Local mute (instant, no round-trip) is the correct mobile model — do NOT port host-side set_play_audio. Verify hardware volume rocker already drives the USAGE_MEDIA AudioTrack before adding an in-app slider.

roadmap.title: Aspect/fit mode (fit/fill/stretch) + orientation handling
roadmap.approach: Add setAspect(slot,mode) to the plugin and branch applyAspect (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:555-569): fit=min-scale (current), fill=max-scale+crop, stretch=MATCH_PARENT; add setRequestedOrientation(SENSOR_LANDSCAPE) on stream entry, restore on detach. Build the fit segmented control in js/session/display.js, persist in localStorage. Mobile-specific UX (desktop relies on window resize).

roadmap.title: Host go_offline + incoming-connection events + approval/kick + OTP rotation
roadmap.approach: Store the accept-loop JoinHandle + Node in managed HostState; add go_offline (abort task, drop Node, stop_host). Make go_online re-runnable. Move OTP to Arc<Mutex<String>>, rotate + emit host-password on each successful auth; add new_password/session_password commands. Replace the silent auto-accept in host.rs with a race: emit session-request (peer id, pw-status) and await respond_request oneshot OR a correct password; emit host-peer-connected/disconnected with peer name (fill the no-op on_connect at host.rs:158); add disconnect_session(sid). Build js/screens/host.js: go online/offline toggle, stable-ID + copy/share, OTP chip + copy + rotate, connected-peers list + kick, touch-first incoming-request approval sheet with 30s auto-deny, unattended toggle (skip OTP). Add throttle (per-peer lockout, global auto-rotate) ported from desktop auth.rs.

roadmap.title: Client interactive OTP prompt + wrong-password retry
roadmap.approach: Replace the single-shot authenticate() in client.rs (currently returns Err on NeedPassword) with the desktop race loop: send empty Auth first, loop on recv_host_auth; on NeedPassword emit auth-prompt (req id + peer) and await a submit_password command oneshot; on Ok/Denied finish. Port pw_pending map + next_auth counter. Build a touch-first bottom-sheet password modal in js/session/session.js (or a small auth.js): big mono input, Gönder/İptal, inline 'yanlış şifre' on re-fire. Keep the up-front pw field as first attempt.

roadmap.title: Saved Devices / Geçmiş screen (touch-first)
roadmap.approach: Build js/screens/devices.js consuming store/peers.js: a 'Cihazlar'/'Geçmiş' tab with a Kayıtlı section + Son bağlananlar, each row = icon/image + name + grouped id + online dot + last-seen, tap-to-connect (doConnect path, recordConnection on success), long-press/bottom-sheet for Edit/Forget/Favorite (no desktop hover buttons), swipe-to-delete, '+ Cihaz ekle' sheet (name + id + 4 icon chips, image upload optional), 'Geçmişi temizle'. Remote/game timeline split via mode chip.

roadmap.title: Clipboard sync (remote-only): send + receive
roadmap.approach: Add send_clipboard(slot,text) in src/datachan.rs (look up SessionSender, send_data DataMsg::Clipboard). The read loop already decodes DataMsg (W1-datachan) -> emit clipboard-in. In js/session/sidechannels.js add a clipboard icon-btn in the overlay Tools card (remote only): read navigator.clipboard.readText() and invoke send_clipboard; on clipboard-in write via navigator.clipboard.writeText with a 'Panoya kopyala' toast fallback (Android WebView may need a gesture; add a Rust write_clipboard via tauri clipboard plugin if unreliable). Strictly hidden in game mode.

roadmap.title: In-session text chat (remote-only)
roadmap.approach: Add send_chat(slot,text) in datachan.rs; read loop emits chat-msg {slot,text} (W1-datachan). Build the chat panel in js/session/sidechannels.js: chat icon-btn with unread badge in the overlay (remote only), bottom-sheet message list + composer, my/them styling, visualViewport keyboard-aware so the composer isn't obscured over the native surface. Listen chat-msg; optimistic outbound append. Hidden in game mode.

roadmap.title: File transfer + remote file browser (remote-only)
roadmap.approach: In datachan.rs port hold.rs reassembler (FileBegin/Chunk/End state machine, per-xferId BTreeMap, 8-concurrent cap, idle sweep, MAX_XFER_BYTES) and add fs_list/fs_get senders + a periodic file-progress event. Add a mobile save helper writing to the app external files dir 'Pulsar Alınanlar' (later MediaStore/share sheet) and an upload pick-and-send via tauri-plugin-dialog reusing the CHUNK=2048 chunker. Build js/session/files.js: single-column remote browser (fs_list/fs-entries), breadcrumb, 44px rows, per-file download (fs_get + file-begin/recv tracking), 'Gönder' OS picker, transfer-progress list + ported concurrency queue (MAX_WIRE_XFERS, timeouts). Reuse i18n files.* keys. Gated to remote (non-game) mode.

roadmap.title: Multi-monitor picker + live switch (remote-only)
roadmap.approach: In client.rs capture caps.displays (currently discarded at client.rs:135), emit host-displays and stash per-slot. Add set_play_monitor(slot,display_idx) in session_cmds.rs using the restream channel (re-request_stream with display_idx + re-arm decoder), with a ~400ms leading-edge debounce. Build the picker in js/session/display.js: a bottom sheet listing displays (name + WxH + 'ana' badge) shown only when remote + displays>1, tap to switch with a brief switching spinner. Do NOT port Windows-only screen adaptation.

roadmap.title: On-screen virtual gamepad (game-only, Moonlight-style)
roadmap.approach: Add send_gamepad(slot,buttons,lx,ly,rx,ry,lt,rt) + send_gamepad_disconnect(slot) in input_cmds.rs building pulsar_core GamepadState (axis_to_i16/trigger_to_u8, UP-positive Y) as InputEvent::GamepadSlot{target:Auto}. Build js/session/gamepad.js: a landscape touch overlay (dual floating analog sticks, D-pad, ABXY, L1/R1/L2/R2, Start/Select/Guide) over the transparent video, multi-touch via touch identifiers, send-on-change throttled via rAF; bit constants mirroring input/types.rs. Shown only in-session+game; disables pointer forwarding while active; send disconnect on End/overlay-off. Optional later: layout customization + navigator.vibrate haptics.

roadmap.title: Physical controller forwarding + rumble (both modes)
roadmap.approach: In js/session/gamepad.js add a requestAnimationFrame poll of navigator.getGamepads() while in-session, mapping standard buttons/axes to Pulsar bits (UP-positive Y, triggers*255, sticks*32767), send-on-change + ~1Hz keepalive via send_gamepad; map gamepad.index->slot for multiple pads; send_gamepad_disconnect on gamepaddisconnected. For rumble, handle the session's Rumble control frame in datachan.rs -> emit 'rumble' {slot,large,small}; in JS call gamepad.vibrationActuator.playEffect('dual-rumble',...). Reuses the W4 send_gamepad commands; host already replays pads + forwards rumble.

roadmap.title: Microphone upstream (phone mic -> host, remote-only)
roadmap.approach: Add RECORD_AUDIO to AndroidManifest + runtime request. Implement native mic capture in the pulsar-video Android plugin (AudioRecord, VOICE_COMMUNICATION for AEC/NS, raw s16le 48kHz mono to interop with desktop hosts that pipe DataMsg::Audio straight to paplay/aplay) exposed as a plugin command, with mobile.rs binding + desktop.rs no-op. Add mic_start/mic_stop in client.rs pulling PCM and sending DataMsg::Audio frames (~20ms) + DataMsg::AudioEnd on stop via the SessionSender. Build a mic toggle in js/session/audio.js (remote only), mute on background (visibilitychange). Defer Opus negotiation. (Phone-as-host playback of client mic is a later, lower item.)

roadmap.title: Reverse direction (role swap) Tools control
roadmap.approach: Add reverse_play(slot) in client.rs sending the reverse request with this device's id over the session (reusing the go_online host path to receive the inbound). Add a single button in the overlay Tools card (remote). Reuses existing host plumbing.

roadmap.title: Multi-session registry + touch session switcher
roadmap.approach: Introduce a JS session registry in js/session/session.js (array of {slot,id,codec,mode}) mirroring SessionManager, with a touch-first switcher (horizontal pill row or swipe-down sheet, not desktop tabs). Bump the Android plugin slot cap (currently coerceIn(0,1)) and keep background decoders paused or torn down on switch. Add setActivePane/setActiveSession to route input + audio (currently slot-0-pinned) to the foregrounded slot. Per-session optional rename stored in the registry.

roadmap.title: Split layout chooser + per-pane target + per-cell resolution + focus routing
roadmap.approach: Build js/session/split.js: replace the insta-2up #btn-split with a touch layout sheet (landscape side-by-side h2 as the natural default, v2 stacked, grid4 deferred to tablets) + an exit-split control. Per empty pane show a compact connect sheet to pick a DISTINCT target (instead of reusing rawId) and thread display_idx for same-host second panes (add a freeDisplayFor-style claimed-display map). Set StreamReq width/height for reduced per-cell resolution (720p when a 2nd slot is live) instead of 0x0. Focused-pane audio/input routing via setActivePane. Update Android positionPanes for left/right + quadrant gravity.

roadmap.title: Host HEVC/AV1 codec probe (phone-as-host caps)
roadmap.approach: Add a plugin command enumerating MediaCodecList encoders (video/hevc, video/av01, video/avc) and build StreamCaps.codecs dynamically in host.rs (prefer h265>h264 when an HEVC encoder exists) instead of the hardcoded h264-only (host.rs:151). startHost already maps h265->video/hevc. Keep h264 fallback. Improves quality-per-bit for phone-as-host.

roadmap.title: AV1 decode path (RTP OBU depacketizer + MediaCodec av01)
roadmap.approach: Implement an AV1 RTP depacketizer (RFC 9043 aggregation header + OBU fragment reassembly) as a Codec::Av1 arm in rtp.rs emitting length-delimited OBUs; map codec_enum av1->Av1 (today it falls through to H264, so requesting av1 feeds OBUs through the H.264 NAL reassembler = garbage). Configure Kotlin MediaCodec video/av01 with csd from the sequence-header OBU. Until done, explicitly exclude av1 from the mobile codec preference so it cannot be selected.

roadmap.title: HDR (HDR10/HLG) decode + tonemap
roadmap.approach: Plumb an hdr flag UI->connect_host->StreamReq.hdr (host encodes HEVC Main10/PQ). In Kotlin pass KEY_COLOR_TRANSFER/STANDARD/RANGE + HDR static info (from SEI) on the MediaFormat for 10-bit hevc, opt the window/SurfaceView into HDR (setColorMode / Display.HdrCapabilities), fall back to SDR. Gate on Android O+/device support. Heavy; do after core h264/h265 parity.

roadmap.title: Decoder error recovery / keyframe resync feedback
roadmap.approach: Detect MediaCodec exceptions / prolonged garbage in the plugin, emit an event; from client.rs request a keyframe if pulsar-core exposes a force-IDR/restream nudge (else re-arm the decoder by reconfiguring on the next SPS/PPS). Show a brief 'yeniden eşitleniyor' overlay in js/session/hud.js. Improves resilience on lossy mobile links.

roadmap.title: Audio jitter buffer tuning
roadmap.approach: Tune the Android AudioTrack buffer (PulsarVideoPlugin.kt:401) toward an ~80-120ms target like the desktop native player, and/or add a small bounded leaky queue in feedAudio that drops the oldest packet past target. Measure A/V drift on a real device first; start with buffer sizing. No UI.

roadmap.title: Language TR/EN switcher in Settings
roadmap.approach: Add a 'Dil' TR/EN segmented control to js/screens/settings.js (reuse .seg pattern); on pick call setLang() from i18n.js, persist to 'pulsar.lang.v1' + Config.language via set_config, set <html lang>, re-render visible strings.

roadmap.title: LAN online-presence dots for saved devices
roadmap.approach: Add a lan_devices command in client.rs backed by pulsar-core LAN discovery, register in lib.rs, poll every 3s from js/screens/devices.js and render .dot.on (reuse desktop isOnline/addrHost matching). Skip if mobile is treated as connect-out-only on cellular.

designTasks.title: Connecting screen (phased, cancelable, transport-aware)
designTasks.description: Full-screen portrait/landscape touch overlay: target id + mode badge, animated pulse-ring, a 3-4 step list (reaching -> P2P vs Relay transport -> auth/awaiting-approval -> preparing/first-frame), a 12s slow-host hint, and a large Cancel button. Re-laid-out for a phone, not a port of desktop Connecting.svelte. Indigo (remote) / cyan (game) themed.

designTasks.title: In-session overlay/dock (mode-aware) + open affordance
designTasks.description: Touch-first bottom sheet / expanding dock over the transparent video, opened by tapping the status pill or a FAB (no keyboard combo on touch). Collapsible cards. Remote layout = Stream/Display/Audio/Tools; game layout = slim Stream/Gauges/Controllers. Defines the container, card grid, segmented-pill controls, and how it coexists with the floating glass bar without obscuring video. Must NOT show file/clipboard/chat/mic/multi-monitor in game mode.

designTasks.title: Perf HUD + stall / first-frame states
designTasks.description: Compact translucent mono strip showing fps/mbps/RTT/decode, readable at arm's length, safe-area aware; cyan in game, thin in remote. Plus a first-frame 'ilk kare bekleniyor' pulse loader and a mid-session 'Yayın durdu' stall overlay over the transparent surface. Collapsible/toggleable.

designTasks.title: Touch gesture model for remote pointing
designTasks.description: Define and hint the gesture set: tap=left click, double-tap=double-click, long-press=right-click, two-finger tap=middle, two-finger drag=scroll, plus a trackpad/relative mode with an on-screen cursor dot and an absolute/direct toggle. Include a small first-use gesture hint card.

designTasks.title: On-screen keyboard + special-key/modifier bar (remote)
designTasks.description: A keyboard toggle that raises the native soft keyboard, plus a scrollable special-key pill row (Esc/Tab/arrows/F1-F12) and sticky/latching modifier chips (Ctrl/Alt/Shift/Win) with a one-tap Ctrl+Alt+Del. visualViewport-aware so it sits above the keyboard. Remote-mode only.

designTasks.title: Stream-quality control sheet + pre-connect presets
designTasks.description: Segmented pills (codec/fps/resolution/quality) + a bitrate (Mbit) slider for live in-session changes in the overlay Stream card, plus a pre-connect quality section with Auto/Data-saver/Balanced/Performance presets. Show a brief 'switching' veil during encoder rebuild. Game + remote.

designTasks.title: View-fit + orientation + multi-monitor picker (remote)
designTasks.description: A fit/fill/stretch segmented control, an orientation affordance, and a bottom-sheet host-monitor list (name + WxH + 'ana' primary badge, current selection highlighted, switching spinner). Shown only when remote + >1 monitor.

designTasks.title: Audio mute toggle (+ mic) affordance
designTasks.description: A speaker/mute icon-button for the in-session bar/overlay (instant local mute, both personalities) with a clear muted state, optional volume; and a separate mic-on/off toggle (remote only) with a recording indicator and background auto-mute.

designTasks.title: Saved Devices / Geçmiş screen + add/edit sheets
designTasks.description: Touch-first vertical device list (icon/image + name + grouped id + online dot + last-seen), tap-to-connect, long-press/swipe actions (Edit/Forget/Favorite/Remove-from-history), a '+ Cihaz ekle' bottom sheet (name + id + 4 icon chips), a clear-history action, and a remote-vs-game timeline split (section or mode chip).

designTasks.title: Cihazim host screen: lifecycle, ID/OTP, peers, approval
designTasks.description: Online/offline toggle, big stable device ID with copy + share, OTP chip with copy + rotate, an unattended-access toggle with warning copy, a live connected-peers list with per-peer kick, and a touch-first incoming-connection approval sheet (peer id + password-status chip + Izin Ver/Reddet + 30s auto-deny countdown).

designTasks.title: Client password prompt (bottom sheet) + retry
designTasks.description: Touch-first bottom-sheet OTP prompt raised when the host requests a password: big mono input, Gonder/Iptal, inline 'yanlis sifre' error on re-fire without reconnecting. Separate from the up-front connect-form pw field.

designTasks.title: Clipboard + chat side-channel UI (remote only)
designTasks.description: A clipboard send/receive control (send button + inbound 'Panoya kopyala' toast) and a chat panel: chat icon with unread badge, bottom-sheet message list + composer with my/them styling, visualViewport keyboard-aware over the native surface. Strictly hidden in game mode.

designTasks.title: Mobile file-manager (remote only)
designTasks.description: Single-column remote directory browser with breadcrumb + 44px tap rows + per-file download, an upload entry via the OS document picker (replacing the desktop local-HOME pane), and a transfer-progress list with success/fail + cancel. Touch-first, indigo accent, hidden in game mode.

designTasks.title: On-screen virtual gamepad (game only)
designTasks.description: Moonlight-class landscape touch controller over the transparent video: dual floating analog sticks, D-pad, ABXY face buttons, L1/R1/L2/R2 shoulders/triggers, Start/Select/Guide; multi-touch, translucent, cyan, pass-through except on controls. Optional v2: drag-to-reposition/resize + haptics.

designTasks.title: Microphone capture affordance + permission flow
designTasks.description: A mic toggle with active/recording state and a clear permission-request explanation, background auto-mute. Remote-only.

designTasks.title: Multi-session switcher + split layout chooser
designTasks.description: A touch session switcher (horizontal pill row or swipe-down sheet, NOT tiny desktop tabs) for holding/switching multiple hosts, plus a split layout sheet (landscape side-by-side default, stacked alt, grid4 tablet-only), an exit-split control, a per-pane target-pick sheet, and focused-pane indication.

designTasks.title: Language TR/EN segmented toggle in Settings
designTasks.description: A compact touch-first TR/EN segmented control in the Ayarlar/About area that re-renders strings live; reuse the existing .seg pattern.
